runners.comment: 42,000euros half-brother to winner in Malaysia; dam 5f winner; yard's first 2yo went close

runners.name: Celestial Noir
runners.spotlight: 28-1, green and weakened into ninth of the 11 runners on her Beverley debut 17 days ago, shaping with more encouragement than the distance beaten might suggest; stable won both divisions of this race 12 months ago, including with a filly who had been well held in the corresponding Beverley race; can do better.
runners.comment: Green and ran better than her distance beaten suggests on debut; likely improver

runners.name: Bea Kindly
runners.spotlight: 50-1 and always behind on her Redcar debut 12 days ago (5f, good to firm); hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 50-1 and always behind on her Redcar debut 12 days ago (5f, good to firm); hard to fancy

races.race_name: British EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Town Queen
runners.spotlight: 160,000euros yearling; pedigree is a mixture of stamina and speed; unraced as 2yo but she kept up the chase against a previous winner, at Pontefract (6f, good; 7-2) last week, and looks likely to benefit from the experience and the extra furlong; good claims.
runners.comment: 160,000euros yearling; kept up the chase on 6f debut when 2nd to previous winner; improve

runners.name: Distant Shore
runners.spotlight: Hooded for Salisbury debut (6f, good to firm) last July where she raced with her head on one side virtually throughout but showed spunk to force a dead-heat; off since and it's hard to judge the merit of that muddling race but the new trip shouldn't be a problem; can give it a good shot.
runners.comment: Hooded for 6f Salisbury debut last July when forcing a late dead-heat; off since

runners.name: Perfect Ruler
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two juvenile runs over 6f (good and heavy) but she went off shortish prices each time and her 3yo career could be more fruitful, especially over a trip that should suit; market can guide on comeback.
runners.comment: Modest form in two juvenile runs (6f); this trip should suit better; market can guide
runners.owner: A E Oppenheimer And Ms Emily Oppenheimer

runners.name: Porth Eilian
runners.spotlight: No impact at Haydock in October but she looked more of a prospect on Southwell AW (7f) last month when doing some steady late work; might find this test on the sharp side but another step forward is possible.
runners.comment: No impact in sole juvenile start (6f); promising 5th over 7f on AW in April; can progress
runners.owner: Julie & David Martin, Dan Hall & Partner

runners.name: Karvala
runners.spotlight: Out of a 1m Listed winner who was runner-up in the 1,000 Guineas; signs of ability in a valuable Haydock event on debut (6f, good; 5-1) in September, albeit just running on at one pace in the closing stages; lacks a recent run but no surprise if she does better over 7f.
runners.comment: Signs of ability on 6f debut in September; should achieve more now upped to 7f
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

races.race_name: Sky Bet Thirsk Hunt Cup Handicap

runners.name: Blue Rc
runners.spotlight: 7f course winner on good to firm last summer and has done little but progress since, winning over 1m at Southwell on New Year's Day; excellent second of 14 at Newcastle (1m again) since, with reopposing Flight Plan and Apiarist in behind; should continue the good work back on turf; top of the list.
runners.comment: Progressive course winner; excellent second in strong AW handicap on Good Friday; player

runners.name: Sea Force
runners.spotlight: Won a Nottingham maiden (1m, good) and a Yarmouth handicap (1m1f, soft) last year; respectable sixth (Mirsky third) of 12 in 1m1f Musselburgh handicap on reappearance three weeks ago; wandered around a little under pressure there and now refitted with the cheekpieces he wore once last season; could resume his progression and much respected from a handy inside stall.
runners.comment: Two wins in 2025; fair sixth on return and could resume progression in refitted headgear

runners.name: Flight Plan
runners.spotlight: Failed to build on a decent fourth at Lingfield (1m2f) in February when behind Blue Rc at Newcastle (1m) on Good Friday but the fact he made all in this race off 8lb lower last year means a revival can't be discounted, particularly as he's well drawn to attack again; tongue-tied first time.
runners.comment: 8lb lower than when winning this last year; revival isn't ruled out in new tongue-tie
runners.owner: Fine Clarets Racing, Tp Watson & C Wood

runners.name: City Of Poets
runners.spotlight: Has looked quirky on occasions but he produced a decidedly useful effort when coming with a strong run to get up late in 8.6f Wolverhampton handicap in November; matched that form when a close third there on reappearance six weeks ago; won a fast-ground novice on turf last year (also second here on debut); one of two solid contenders for James Tate.
runners.comment: Has odd quirk but done well on AW in recent starts; one of two solid contenders for yard

runners.name: Esherann
runners.spotlight: Pair of 1m wins on good ground in Ireland last summer, namely a Naas maiden (wore cheekpieces) and Curragh handicap (visored); low-mileage new recruit for Declan Carroll but he'll need to leave last month's Newmarket reappearance with a tongue-tie added (retained) well behind to play a leading role here.
runners.comment: Two wins in Ireland last year but well held on recent first run for Declan Carroll

runners.name: Apiarist
runners.spotlight: Three fine efforts on AW to start the year but his run of good form came to a halt when well behind Blue Rc at Newcastle over Easter; acts on turf but has a much better strike-rate on AW; drawn widest.
runners.comment: Started year in good form on AW but lesser run latest; others preferred back on turf

runners.name: A War Eagle
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who was having only his second start for this yard when clinging on over the extended 7f at Beverley (good to firm) nine days ago; only raised 3lb but this is a step up from that Class 4 and his draw isn't ideal for one who usually races close to the pace.
runners.comment: Only nudged up 3lb for recently Beverley success but this is a much stronger race

runners.name: Mirsky
runners.spotlight: Hit a hot spell at the beginning of last summer, winning over C&D (good to firm) before following up at the Epsom Derby meeting (8.5f, good to soft); creditable third (Sea Force sixth) of 12 on Musselburgh reappearance (1m1f, good) three weeks ago; likely to be competitive again.
runners.comment: C&D win last May; creditable third of 12 on reappearance; likely to be in the mix

runners.name: Bellarchi
runners.spotlight: Last four wins at Musselburgh, including two last month, latterly producing a career-best performance to see off ten rivals over 1m on good; also went close at this course last year; a further 4lb rise demands even more but can't rule out in her current mood.
runners.comment: In the form of life with two Musselburgh wins last month; can't discount in current mood

runners.name: Dashing Darcey
runners.spotlight: Just second outing for Geoff Harker when runner-up off 1lb higher at Haydock (1m, good) last May but never involved when dropped to 7f at York the following month and not seen since (gelded); this is a tough race to return in.
runners.comment: Second off 1lb higher last May but off since a lesser run the following month; gelded
runners.owner: C H McGie & Northumbria Leisure Ltd

runners.name: Accentuate
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a Windsor novice (1m, good) last June; far from disgraced despite only beating one home when fifth on his handicap debut at Ascot (good to firm) last July and perhaps unsuited by soft ground when well held there in September; sold for 23,000gns in November and gelded ahead of this return for a new stable; drawn wide and probably best watched this time.
runners.comment: Lightly raced new recruit for Tim Easterby but drawn wide and perhaps best watched

runners.name: Redarna
runners.spotlight: Sprung a 66-1 surprise in this on good to firm in 2022 (also fourth in 2024); added career win number 17, as an 11yo, at Ayr (1m, good to soft) last September but arrives with something to prove after finishing tailed off behind a couple of these on his Musselburgh reappearance.
runners.comment: Won this in 2022 (also fourth in 2024) but recent reappearance was poor

runners.name: Rhoscolyn
runners.spotlight: Last year's Golden Mile win at Goodwood (strong record there) shows he's still smart on his day; respectable return to action when fifth in good conditions race over 7f here three weeks ago but he's gained the vast majority of his ten career wins on slower ground than he's likely to encounter this afternoon.
runners.comment: Golden Mile winner last year; fair reappearance but ideally wants slower ground

runners.name: Al Mubhir
runners.spotlight: Smart at his best for William Haggas, winning a Listed race at Sandown (1m2f, good) in July 2024; campaigned solely in Australia in 2025 and well beaten on his last two starts; potentially well handicapped back in Britain for a new yard after wind surgery, but he carries obvious risks.
runners.comment: Smart at his best and back in Britain after spell in Australia; has had wind surgery

races.race_name: Sky Bet Sunday At Thirsk 31 May Book Now Handicap

runners.name: Analogical
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has finished out of the first two only once in eight handicap starts, gaining compensation for a pair of near-misses this year when well on top over an extended 1m4f at Newcastle 12 days ago; just as effective at 1m6f and on turf; the one to beat.
runners.comment: Well on top at Newcastle latest and this consistent sort is the one to beat back on turf

runners.name: Ashdown Forest
runners.spotlight: Touch disappointing over hurdles for this yard but fared better back on the Flat when fifth of 12 over 1m4f at Pontefract (good to soft) 25 days ago and three subsequent winners have emerged from that race; swaps the first-time cheekpieces he wore last time for blinkers he tried once over hurdles; his best RPR on the Flat was achieved over this trip.
runners.comment: Underwhelming over hurdles but ran better back on Flat latest, with form boosted

runners.name: Shipshape
runners.spotlight: Positive start to his time with Bernard Llewellyn, getting off the mark in 1m6f Soutthwell handicap 17 days ago; showed promise on turf for previous yard and should remain competitive off 4lb higher.
runners.comment: Came good over 1m6f on AW last time and should remain competitive off 4lb higher

runners.name: Marbuzet
runners.spotlight: Had a good 2025, winning over 1m7f at Ayr (good to firm) and 2m at Chester (good); also runner-up on five other occasions, including his reappearance.
runners.comment: Good 2025, winning twice and runner-up five times, including reappearance

runners.name: Scottish Dancer
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner at up to 2m for Keith Dalgleish in early summer 2023; consistent in handicap hurdles in Ireland in 2024, including a 2m6f win, but lost his way badly there last year; back on the Flat for a new yard after 196 days off with a fair bit to prove.
runners.comment: Lost his way way badly over hurdles in Ireland last year and plenty to prove back on Flat

races.race_name: Alison And Andrew Richardson Ruby Anniversary Handicap

runners.name: Kats Bob
runners.spotlight: Enthusiastic front-runner who shone for Ruth Carr in 2025, winning five 6f handicaps (including here on good to firm); encouraging seasonal return at Catterick (6f, good) last month and likely remains in better form than his subsequent Ripon ninth might suggest as he was second home on his side.
runners.comment: C&D win one of five in 2025; better than result at Ripon latest (second home in group)

runners.name: Mr Dreamseller
runners.spotlight: Advanced his RPR with each start in sprint novices on AW, culminating with a 6f win at Wolverhampton in February; his run of progressive form came to an abrupt halt on his C&D handicap debut (good) a fortnight ago but it's too soon to suggest he won't prove effective on turf; wears a first-time hood now.
runners.comment: Progressive in AW novices but well held on C&D handicap debut; hood added now

runners.name: Kosometsuke
runners.spotlight: Better than ever when making a winning start for the Michael Dods stable over 7f here (good) a fortnight ago; 6f winner for previous yard last autumn so the return to sprinting shouldn't be an issue; respected off just 2lb higher.
runners.comment: Winning start for new yard over 7f here this 6f winner is only 2lb higher today

runners.name: Mister Sox
runners.spotlight: Started 2025 with three wins but didn't seem as forward for this year's reappearance, never threatening at Ripon 16 days ago; he'll need to have come on a bit but is nicely handicapped again if staging a revival.
runners.comment: Seemed rusty on reappearance but well handicapped again if staging a revival

runners.name: Rock Opera
runners.spotlight: Hold-up sprinter and on a losing run of 21; well handicapped as a consequence and should be sharper for his Ripon reappearance 16 days ago.
runners.comment: On a losing run but well handicapped as a result and should be sharper for reappearance

runners.name: Aberama Gold
runners.spotlight: Retains a large portion of his ability despite his advanced years and took advantage of a reduced mark at Cattterick (6f, good) ten days ago; only nudged up 1lb for that narrow success.
runners.comment: 9yo who took advantage of reduced mark at Catterick last week; only up 1lb

runners.name: Emperor Caradoc
runners.spotlight: Two 6f wins on fast ground last season; made a pleasing return to action at Newcastle (6f) in March and raced away from the main action when well held back on turf at Doncaster (5f, good to soft) since; not discounted.
runners.comment: Pleasing return on AW in March and excuses on turf since

runners.name: Sugar Baby
runners.spotlight: Four wins last year, including 6f on good to firm; mark went up as a result but showed he can still be very competitive when runner-up on AW on his penultimate outing of 2025; no great record fresh so it might be best to look elsewhere this time.
runners.comment: Four wins in good 2025 but no great record fresh so possibly best watched here
runners.owner: Hard Held Partnership And Angus Racing

runners.name: Khabib
runners.spotlight: All six wins over 5f; also second over 5f here (good) in August of 2024 but hasn't been seen since a poor run at Beverley later that month; previously trained by his owner; can only really watch after such a long time off.
runners.comment: Six-time 5f winner but off since August 2024

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: American Bay
runners.spotlight: Beat Equity Law over C&D last June but the tables were turned when the pair repposed here in August; two solid efforts on AW in March; should give his running.
runners.comment: Closely matched with Equity Law on two C&D runs last summer; should give his running

runners.name: Sports Coach
runners.spotlight: A wind op last spring had a positive effect; back to winning ways with a ready Southwell win in March; both runs since have been respectable but others in the field look better handicapped.
runners.comment: AW win in March and two sound efforts since; others perhaps better handicapped though

runners.name: Elegant Erin
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 in top form, winning twice at Beverley (5f, soft) before a close fourth in Listed company at Ascot; that did her handicap mark no good and she has made no impact in two runs this year; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 in fine form but paid the price with the handicapper; yet to get going in 2026

runners.name: Vantheman
runners.spotlight: Good handicap form as a 3yo; drew a blank last year but back to winning ways at Newcastle in January (5f; 3lb lower mark than today); two runs since haven't been so encouraging and connections now turn to blinkers.
runners.comment: Newcastle win in January (5f); not so good twice since and blinkers now added

runners.name: Equity Law
runners.spotlight: Conditions to suit and back to his last winning mark; two good runs this year, third of eight at Ripon last week (5f, good), and he's more appealing than many.
runners.comment: C&D winner; arrives on the back of a good Ripon third last week; more appealing than many

runners.name: Kinetic Force
runners.spotlight: Two good runs since joining Ruth Carr, fourth of 12 at Newcastle in March and third of 17 at Musselburgh three weeks ago; up in class but should give it another good go.
runners.comment: Two good runs for Ruth Carr this spring; up in class but should make another bold bid

runners.name: Wheels Of Fire
runners.spotlight: Easily saw off 11 rivals at Musselburgh last month (5f, soft), form that has received some boosts since; not well drawn when down the field at Newbury two weeks ago (6f, good); mark left alone and perhaps vulnerable over 5f on a quick surface.
runners.comment: Musselburgh win was decisive but on soft ground; high in weights now

runners.name: Squealer
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on last season's best, including a York win off 4lb higher (5f, good); only midfield at Beverley on his reappearance 17 days ago (5f, good to soft) but the ground would have been slower than ideal and he travelled strongly before plugging on past a few tiring rivals in the final furlong; better expected back on a quicker surface.
runners.comment: Well handicapped and his Beverley return was encouraging; contender back on better ground

runners.name: Brazen Bolt
runners.spotlight: Narrowly landed big-field events at York and Goodwood last July, both for Warren Fentiman; ended the year quietly but he has had a wind op since last seen and is only 1lb higher than for the Goodwood win.
runners.comment: Two big-field wins last summer; on a fair mark if a wind op has the desired effect

runners.name: Toca Madera
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate for one of his ability when trained by Brian Meehan but he can go well fresh and is starting out for David O'Meara (sold 22,000gns last October) off a dangerous mark, some 6lb lower than when third in a hot 5f handicap at York's Dante meeting last May; possible he'll have one eye on that prize but still more interesting than many.
runners.comment: Modest strike-rate but talented for B Meehan; sold 22,000gns; interesting on stable debut
runners.owner: L Bond & Sheikh Aymen Jawad Al Hamandi

runners.name: Tatterstall
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; well handicapped on his 2024 form but he was only seen twice last year and finished down the field on both occasions; enough to prove back from a break.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on 2024 form but down the field in both runs last year; enough to prove

races.race_name: Sky Bet Irish Day Saturday 16th May Book Now Handicap

runners.name: Law Supreme
runners.spotlight: He had dropped a long way in the weights prior to his Wolverhampton win on Boxing Day; added further wins at Wolverhampton (twice) and Bath (1m, good) since; similar form when fourth at Nottingham two weeks ago (1m2f, good); shouldn't be far away if the ground is deemed suitable (untried on faster than good).
runners.comment: Back on the up since December, winning four times; shouldn't be far away if ground is okay

runners.name: Kiss Me My Love
runners.spotlight: Only 1lb higher than for her Beverley win (8.5f, soft) last September; running herself back into form, fifth of ten at Ripon last week having fluffed the start; not out of it.
runners.comment: On a fair mark and looks to be running herself into form; each-way shout

runners.name: Rising Force
runners.spotlight: Too good for 21 rivals at Doncaster in March (1m, good to soft) in March; last of 13 at Southwell under a penalty soon after but he didn't see a lot of daylight and he can be given a pass; revival on the cards.
runners.comment: Can be given a pass for latest AW run and earlier Doncaster win was a good effort

runners.name: Pension Pot
runners.spotlight: Promise in three 2yo runs and again on his handicap debut at Nottingham two weeks ago, shaping as though the run might bring him on; still has potential and that wouldn't apply to many of these.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo; encouraging run at Nottingham on handicap debut; can take a step forward

runners.name: Front Gunner
runners.spotlight: He has won on fast turf (7f) but he is a better horse on AW; not at his best on his last two starts and now tries 1m for the first time on his 26th start.
runners.comment: Better on AW and not at best in that sphere on last two starts; new trip today

runners.name: Thats My Boy Luke
runners.spotlight: Two wins last summer, both over 1m at Ripon (good and good to firm); ended that campaign with two lesser efforts; betting useful back from six months off with his trainer running three.
runners.comment: Two 1m wins at Ripon last summer; returns from six months off; stable run three here

runners.name: Sunny Orange
runners.spotlight: Four of his five wins have come at Beverley, the latest nine days ago (8.5f, good to firm); 4lb higher in a stronger race but as the only last-time-out winner in the field he has to come right into the reckoning.
runners.comment: Gained 4th Beverley success nine days ago; 4lb higher in a better race but can't discount

runners.name: Off Spin
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; ran well at Newmarket (1m1f, good) last October before a lesser effort on the AW at Southwell (1m3f) one month later; one of three runners for Tim Easterby.
runners.comment: 0-11 and inconsistent; likely best watched back from a break; stable run three here
runners.owner: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor

runners.name: Lightning Galaxy
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden but runner-up on three occasions, including both seasonal debuts; ran well when second over C&D last July too and he needs serious respect back from 176 days off.
runners.comment: 0-8 but has C&D form and can go well fresh; one to consider off a dangerous mark

races.race_name: Howden Cross Country Chase

runners.name: The Goffer
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; second to Vanillier in P P Hogan Memorial over C&D in February and most recently a close third (beaten 1.25l) to Busselton in 4m1f La Touche here on Thursday; big player if turning out again.
runners.comment: Third to Busselton in Thursday's la Touche, big player if turning out again; reserve

runners.name: Lisnamult Lad
runners.spotlight: Decent sort at his best, career-best performance when close second in last season's 2m5f Topham at Aintree; very lightly raced this season, most recently running well for long way in same event before fading from last; type who could go well here if taking to the banks (won only point-to-point).
runners.comment: Decent Topham run latest; type who could go well here if taking to the banks

runners.name: Fakir d'Oudairies
runners.spotlight: Four-time Grade 1 chase winner (peaked at 167) for Joseph O'Brien but left yard after a disappointing cross-country debut in December 2024; just okay efforts back on cross-country circuit this term, most recently poor effort back over conventional fences at Wexford last month; hard to assess right now.
runners.comment: Four-time Grade 1 chase winner seems well off the form of those days; hard to assess

runners.name: Chemical Energy
runners.spotlight: Very smart as a novice and some good runs in the Galway Plate and Munster National in 2024; generally ineffective in cross-country races since then however, reportedly burst blood vessels when fifth in this race last year, most recently (after two point-to-point wins) pulled up after never counting in 3m1f Fairyhouse hunter-chase at Easter; questions remain.
runners.comment: Classy at his best but so far unconvincing in this discipline; fifth last year

runners.name: Olympic Man
runners.spotlight: Decent performer at his best for Willie Mullins, winning 2m4f Naas beginners' chase last season and ran well for a long way in 4m Scottish National; struggled back hurdling earlier this season before let go for 16,000gns in January; surpassed expectations (125-1) on yard debut in 3m Kim Muir at Cheltenham when beaten under 10l in first-time cheekpieces; can't be ruled out on banks' debut.
runners.comment: Kim Muir debut effort for new yard encouraging; can't be ruled out on banks' debut

runners.name: Fountain House
runners.spotlight: Prolific points' winner extended his excellent record around this course when running away with Ladies' Cup over this trip on Tuesday; stiffer task now conceding weight all-round, but in excellent fettle and big shout.
runners.comment: Tuesday's Ladies Cup winner in excellent fettle and big shout

runners.name: What Path
runners.spotlight: Decent novice hurdler for Willie Mullins (suited by a sound surface) but still a maiden chaser; however, comes here on the back of three point-to-point wins for Michael Mangan, having earlier chased home Fountain House over the banks at Lingstown; not one to rule out on return to the track.
runners.comment: Good point-to-point form of late; not one to rule out on return to the track

runners.name: I A Connect
runners.spotlight: Veteran gained his second win over fences, and fifth in all, in 2m4f handicap at Down Royal's Christmas meeting and most recently finished a good third in 3m4f Ulster National at Downpatrick; previous two runs over this course have been poor (pulled up in this last year).
runners.comment: In good form of late over conventional fences but previous banks runs poor

runners.name: Vaureal
runners.spotlight: Off the mark chasing in 2m6f Fairyhouse handicap in November (yielding-to-soft) and better again when just denied over same C&D early this month on similar ground; in between wasn't discredited in strong renewal of PP Hogan Memorial over today's C&D (fifth to Vanillier); definite claims.
runners.comment: Just denied in recent Fairyhouse handicap; claims on earlier C&D fifth in stronger company

runners.name: The Folkes Tiara
runners.spotlight: Both chase wins have been in quite valuable 2m1f handicaps (yielding), most recently at Fairyhouse in November 2024; below best this season, however, and needs to be revitalised by this switch to the banks; ground should suit.
runners.comment: Below best this season and needs to be revitalised by this switch to the banks

runners.name: Gentleman De Reve
runners.spotlight: Regular in cross-country races in France, though no success since September 2022; joined Peter Maher after finishing sixth in the Velka Pardubicka in October and reportedly found with blood in nostrils on yard debut when soundly beaten over C&D following month; has had plenty of time off since.
runners.comment: Burst blood vessels on yard debut over C&D in November; plenty of time off since

runners.name: Escaria Ten
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won since Thurles beginners' chase win in 2020 for Gordon Elliott; some okay cross-country form in recent seasons, standout run this term when third in 3m6f Cheltenham handicap in January, tame enough Exeter effort latest when ground may have been on the quick side; others preferred.
runners.comment: Cheltenham third gives prospects, may want it softer

runners.name: Broken Ice
runners.spotlight: Most recent of three wins came in 3m Perth handicap hurdle last summer; tailed off on comeback run in early March in Newcastle handicap hurdle; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Tailed off on comeback run in early March in Newcastle handicap hurdle; hard to fancy

runners.name: Vital Island
runners.spotlight: Standing dish around this course and his three wins here include this race last year when beating some higher-rated rivals on good ground; light prep this season, pulled up over C&D in November and, following month, brought down before halfway in 3m6f Cheltenham cross-country contest; well beaten here on Thursday.
runners.comment: Last year's winner hasn't had the ideal prep this time and likely vulnerable

runners.name: Call The Tune
runners.spotlight: Won 3m Navan handicap chase on heavy ground in 2022 for Barry Connell, his sole win; some okay runs in point-to-points for new yard this season, but seems an unlikely one.
runners.comment: Some okay runs in point-to-points for new yard this season but seems an unlikely one

runners.name: Quornofamonday
runners.spotlight: Gained a second chase success with a good display over 2m2f at Thurles in February (soft); well held twice since (blinkered last time) and second experience of the banks here, having never counted in this race last year.
runners.comment: Second experience of the banks here, having never counted in this race last year

races.race_name: DAR Golf Handicap Chase

runners.name: Fortunate Man
runners.spotlight: Began this campaign with 3m1f Aintree handicap win in December (good-to-soft) but hasn't repeated that sort of form in three runs since, including treated for post-race heat stress after Eider Chase fifth; most recently never nearer fourth under top-weight in lesser 3m2f Kelso contest; more needed but not one to rule out.
runners.comment: Tougher task than at Kelso last time although not one to rule out

runners.name: Shanbally Kid
runners.spotlight: Came good over fences in this race last year with 15l win (raised 14lb) on yielding; four runs this term in admittedly hot handicaps have been disappointing, including remote fourth in Leinster National, most recently pulled up after racing prominently for long way in Irish National; handicapper relenting but still 7lb above last year's mark.
runners.comment: Last year's winner hasn't hit form this season; still 7lb above last year's mark

runners.name: Union Station
runners.spotlight: Maiden went close to landing breakthrough win at Leopardstown (3m1f, yielding) in December but hasn't backed that up, most recently never counting when modest enough fourth at Fairyhouse; remains opposable.
runners.comment: Maiden not really progressing in handicaps; remains opposable

runners.name: Saint Davy
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner is 0-7 chasing, pick of this season's form when placed in 3m1f Carlisle handicap in February; didn't get home on softer ground  at Uttoxeter on latest; tough task here upped in trip.
runners.comment: 0-7 chasing and seemingly tough task here upped in trip

runners.name: J'Arrive de L'Est
runners.spotlight: Two of his five chase wins in France were on cross-country courses (very soft/heavy) and good second on first two runs for current trainer in 3m6f Cheltenham cross-country contests over the winter; favourite, reportedly made respiratory noise when pulled up in Midlands National at Uttoxeter on latest so caution advised, albeit he looks on a workable mark.
runners.comment: Made respiratory noise when pulled up in Midlands National; caution advised but nice mark

runners.name: Will Do
runners.spotlight: Bumper and two-time hurdle winner; 0-15 chasing but has run well in defeat at times, most notably over 3m6f at the Cheltenham Festival in 2025 and when a neck second to Better Times Ahead in the Porterstown at Fairyhouse in November (yielding); out of sorts in four runs since however so big upturn in performance required but 13lb better off with Better Times Ahead now, plus rider's claim.
runners.comment: Porterstown second was good but major improvement needed on recent efforts

runners.name: Uncle Pat
runners.spotlight: Bumper/hurdles winner is 0-8 chasing but has being going close in handicaps, notably second in 3m4f National Trial here in February and when chasing home 5l winner Born Braver in 3m5f Ulster National at Downpatrick last time; 5lb better off with that rival here.
runners.comment: Ulster National second is now 5lb better off with winner Born Braver for 5l defeat
runners.owner: Patrick Corbett & Michael Corbett & J J Corbett & W Clancy

runners.name: Jasmin De Grugy
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in 3m1f handicap on this card last season and progressing well this season, recording third handicap win when scoring over 3m2f at Doncaster with runaway success in valuable 3-finisher contest in late February in first-time cheekpieces; plausible he could be open to more improvement at this extreme trip.
runners.comment: Progressive handicapper; could be open to more improvement at this extreme trip

runners.name: Born Braver
runners.spotlight: Much improved since going handicapping, just denied at Down Royal before decisive 3m5f success in Ulster National at Downpatrick in late March, winning going away; didn't count just eight days later when tough ask in 3m5f Irish National but don't rule out back in grade here and handles this quicker ground.
runners.comment: Ulster National winner for whom Irish National probably came too soon; respected

runners.name: Duffle Coat
runners.spotlight: Sole win over fences came in 3m Mayo National in 2024 on good ground; good second in Galway Plate that summer but last year's form was poor; pulled up after never counting on return from long absence in Irish National; hard to be confident in although has ground to suit.
runners.comment: No show on comeback run in Irish National; hard to be confident in although ground to suit

runners.name: Brucejack
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at 19th attempt chasing last month when forcing tactics adopted in 3m2f Down Royal handicap, ready success on good ground; 7lb rise leaves him just 1lb out of the handicap which is negated by rider's claim; interesting if ground continues to dry out.
runners.comment: Came good on better ground at Down Royal; much more on his plate here

runners.name: Fishery Lane
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle win over 2m4f; 0-6 chasing this term, decent handicap debut run at Leopardstown (2m6f), most recently halfway faller in 3m5f Ulster National; others possibly better handicapped and trip remains an unknown.
runners.comment: Ulster National faller; others possibly better handicapped and trip remains an unknown

runners.name: Evies Vladimir
runners.spotlight: Two chase wins on heavy in the 2023/24 season; sole piece of form this season came when second to an improver over 3m at Navan in similar ground, well held the last twice at 3m4f including in National Trial here; 6lb wrong.
runners.comment: Hard to fancy on balance of recent form and really wants testing conditions

runners.name: Brideswell Lad
runners.spotlight: Six-time chase winner (including here) but not since May 2024; missed 2025 entirely and hasn't convinced since January return over both hurdles and fences (dropped 6lbs).
runners.comment: Hasn't convinced since January return over both hurdles and fences

runners.name: Daily Present
runners.spotlight: Career highlight when winning last season's Kim Muir at Cheltenham (3m2f, good-to-soft); two prep runs over hurdles this season before attempting repeat Cheltenham bid but none too fluent and never landed a blow on quicker ground; bit to prove right now but rider's claim a positive.
runners.comment: Kim Muir also-ran has a bit to prove right now but rider's claim a positive

runners.name: Sa Majeste
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner last term and good third to Daily Present in 3m2f Kim Muir at Cheltenham; has struggled in big handicaps this season, exception a never-nearer remote third in Leinster National; never really recovered from bad early blunder when pulled up in Irish National on latest; others look more reliable.
runners.comment: Largely struggled in big handicaps this season; opposable

runners.name: Intense Raffles
runners.spotlight: All three chase wins have been at Fairyhouse, notably the 2024 Irish National; well below best this season however, most recently at same venue in Grade 3 Bobbyjo Chase in February; hard to be confident in and wants softer ground.
runners.comment: Fairyhouse specialist but no trace of form this season; hard to be confident in

runners.name: Lord Lariat
runners.spotlight: Without a win since landing the 2022 Irish Grand National; much reduced mark nowadays is for a reason; visor replaces cheekpieces but remains opposable.
runners.comment: Former Irish National winner races off much reduced mark now for a reason

runners.name: Ain't That A Shame
runners.spotlight: Thyestes winner of 2024 for Henry de Bromhead (off 144) went on to finish decent sixth in Grand National but long absent until faller at the last after never featuring on yard debut in 3m bet365 Premier Chase at Kelso in February; best form on heavy so obvious ground concerns.
runners.comment: 2024 Thyestes winner hard to fancy on evidence of comeback run; wants it softer too

races.race_name: Palmerstown House Estate Pat Taaffe Handicap Chase (Listed Race)

runners.name: Montregard
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to top-class Protektorat; surprise withdrawal from last weekend's bet365 Gold Cup due to ground, having made notable progress before that on similar good ground in 3m Ascot handicaps, winning twice including latest in premier handicap in February; potentially a big player.
runners.comment: Thrived on good ground at Ascot this season at 3m; potentially big player

runners.name: Iceberg Theory
runners.spotlight: Made winning return in 2m5f Cork handicap in November (yielding) and given winter off until not looking to stay 3m6f in National Hunt Chase at Cheltenham in March; interesting contender back in trip here with ground to suit.
runners.comment: Non-stayer in National Hunt Chase;  interesting contender back in trip with ground to suit

runners.name: As The Fella Says
runners.spotlight: Improved for going chasing this season, winning 3m Newbury handicap and better again when clear second at Uttoxeter over same trip, both on soft ground; potentially still well-handicapped and could go well if handling better ground.
runners.comment: Progressive of late on soft; potentially still well-handicapped

runners.name: Your Darling
runners.spotlight: Most of five chase wins have come on decent ground, including last time when taking 3m Ludlow handicap (just 2 finishers) having been dropped in grade following some good runs in better-class handicaps; seems suited by going right-handed; respected.
runners.comment: Recent Ludlow winner likes decent ground going right-handed; one to consider

runners.name: Beachcomber
runners.spotlight: Last win came in 3m Kempton handicap in Christmas of 2024; third to Bill Baxter in this race last year but not at best this season, chasing home a clear winner at Ludlow last time (only two finishers); 7lb lower than last year so don't rule out.
runners.comment: Below best this season but 7lb lower than when third in this last year so don't dismiss

runners.name: No Time To Wait
runners.spotlight: Now 0-10 chasing and hasn't progressed from his novice season; looks to have a good bit to find.
runners.comment: Hasn't progressed from his novice season; looks to have a good bit to find

runners.name: Uhavemeinstitches
runners.spotlight: Has taken well to chasing this term, winning 2m4f Wexford beginners' and acquitted herself well in stronger company, notably third in 2m7f Grade 2 Limerick mares' novice, most recently landing odds in weaker four-runner 3m1f Grade 3 novice at same track in late March, making most from halfway; those runs came in testing conditions but handles better ground; likely has more to offer.
runners.comment: Easy task latest; back in handicap company with likely more to offer

runners.name: Beckett Rock
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner is 0-4 chasing, good second in 2m4f Gowran handicap and most recently did well after being hampered in finishing fifth of 24 in BetMGM Handicap Hurdle at Cheltenham; remains unexposed as a chaser (7lb lower than hurdles) and upped in trip here.
runners.comment: Creditable Cheltenham hurdles run in circumstances; unexposed over fences at this trip

runners.name: Invictus Machin
runners.spotlight: Sole chase win came in 3m1f Kilbeggan handicap in 2024 but career-best on latest when just failing to get up in 2m6f Leopardstown handicap in March (yielding-to-soft), his first run since August; should go well.
runners.comment: Just denied on comeback run at Leopardstown in March; should go well

runners.name: Joystick
runners.spotlight: Much improved to win 3m Navan beginners' in January on testing ground; stiff tasks since, too keen and didn't get home in Brown Advisory and never counted when pulled up in Irish National; cheekpieces added now and has a bit to prove off this mark.
runners.comment: Stiff tasks of late; cheekpieces added now and has a bit to prove off this mark

runners.name: Argento Boy
runners.spotlight: Made amends for a fall on his chasing debut with wins in a beginners' event at Punchestown and Naas Grade 3  (extended 3m, heavy); struggled in the Brown Advisory and didn't land a blow (mistakes) when remote fourth in 3m5f Irish National last time; jumping still needs attention.
runners.comment: Novice made mistakes when a remote fourth in 3m5f Irish National; needs to jump better

runners.name: Bill Baxter
runners.spotlight: 10yo won this last year off 126 (yielding); mixed bag this term, undergoing wind surgery after third start and good comeback run when second in 3m Kelso handicap in February; mistakes when pulled up in Topham at Aintree but remains of interest.
runners.comment: Last year's winner needs to jump better than in Topham; respected

runners.name: Barry Lyndon
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner made winning chase debut in 2m5f Fairyhouse beginners' in October (yielding) but hasn't really progressed, albeit solid first handicap effort when third over 3m1f at same venue last month; 3m tends to stretch him so stamina concerns.
runners.comment: Solid first handicap effort at Fairyhouse but this trip stretches him so stamina concerns

runners.name: The Doyen Chief
runners.spotlight: Better chaser than hurdler, third win coming in 3m Kempton handicap in January (good); better again when second in better-class contest over same C&D following month, disappointingly never counted in Ultima at Cheltenham latest; possibly best going right-handed so could go well here, especially if ground continues to dry out.
runners.comment: Strong Kempton form on good ground makes him a player

runners.name: Perceval Legallois
runners.spotlight: Won major 3m handicaps at Leopardstown last season over fences and hurdles; light campaign this term with Aintree in mind but never counted before unseating four out in National last month; fresher horse than most of these so could improve but certainly needs to.
runners.comment: Unseated at Aintree when no threat; fresh horse so could improve

runners.name: Goraibhmaithagat
runners.spotlight: Improver in Naas handicaps over the winter on testing ground, winning 2m4f novice contest and only caught late  in 3m1f Leinster National off 132 (clear of remainder); didn't appear to stay 3m5f of Irish National latest but definite contender back in trip here if handling quicker conditions.
runners.comment: Didn't stay in Irish National but definite contender back in trip if handling ground

runners.name: Dartmoor Pirate
runners.spotlight: Tongue-tie brought about improvement with wins over the winter in handicaps at Lingfield and valuable 3m Great Yorkshire Chase at Doncaster (soft); reportedly never travelled when unseating at latter venue in February however and given time off since; can't rule out.
runners.comment: Progressive until no show last time; had a break since and could bounce back

runners.name: Kinturk Kalanisi
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler and 0-8 but better chaser, standout run on seasonal return when third of 28 in 3m1f Paddy Power Handicap at Leopardstown over Christmas (yielding); hasn't backed that up however on softer ground, well held in both Leopardstown handicap and Leinster National; return to better ground and first-time cheekpieces could see an improved showing.
runners.comment: Paddy Power 3rd; return to better ground and first-time cheekpieces could see improvement

runners.name: High Class Hero
runners.spotlight: Sole chase win came in 2m7f beginners' here last season but some fine runs in competitive handicaps, fourth in last season's bet365 Gold Cup and this term when second in 2m6f handicap here and most notably chasing clear leader when blundering last in 4m2f Aintree National and weakening late on when fifth of 16 finishers (good-to-soft); tendency to jump left is a negative but could go well if putting it all together here (in-form rider's claim a positive).
runners.comment: Tendency to jump left a negative but top-weight could go well if putting it all together
runners.owner: Sullivan Group Limited & P Crowley & Neill Hughes

runners.name: Captain Cody
runners.spotlight: Last season's 4m Scottish National winner but mixed bag this term however, including early faller in Thyestes and most recently mistakes before unseating four out in Aintree National; hard to be confident in and handicapper not relenting.
runners.comment: Last season's Scottish National winner but jumping letting him down of late
runners.owner: Vincent Caldwell & Angela Shamoon & Mrs A Shamoon Ibgi

runners.name: Clonmeen
runners.spotlight: Not a great strike rate but on song on latest when readily landing 3m1f Fairyhouse handicap last month in first-time blinkers; raised 12lb for that but still 12lb out of handicap here so plenty on his plate.
runners.comment: Fairyhouse winner raised 12lb for that but still 12lb out of handicap here

races.race_name: SBK Irish EBF Mares Champion Hurdle (Grade 1)

runners.name: Wodhooh
runners.spotlight: Has won 10 of her 11 hurdle starts, including this term in Grade 2 Ascot Hurdle, Grade 3 mares' contest at Leopardstown over Christmas and 2m5f Grade 1 Close Brothers Mares' Hurdle at Cheltenham, getting first run on Jade De Grugy going to the last when beating that rival 1l (good); first run here since winning as a 3yo and deserved favourite but could be pushed very close by Jade De Grugy.
runners.comment: Beat Jade De Grugy 1l at Cheltenham, could be pushed even closer this time

runners.name: Jade De Grugy
runners.spotlight: Last year's winner took well to fences this season, finishing second in Limerick Grade 2 over Christmas before going one better in similar company at Thurles (2m5f, yielding) in January; right at her best back hurdling in Grade 1 Mares' Hurdle at Cheltenham when a clear staying-on second to Wodhooh (beaten 1l) having allowed winner first run (good); cheekpieces now fitted for the rematch.
runners.comment: Last year's winner; beaten 1l by Wodhooh at Cheltenham; cheekpieces now fitted

runners.name: Place De La Nation
runners.spotlight: Second-season hurdler has twice landed odds in ordinary company this term and has progressed well in Graded company, notably third in Grade 2 Ryanair Mares' Novice at Cheltenham and most recently Grade 1 Honeysuckle Mares' Novice at Fairyhouse when second to Zanoosh under a positive ride; meets two superior rivals here however so seems booked for third.
runners.comment: Running well in graded company of late but meets two superior rivals here

runners.name: Ill Raise A Glass
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner got off the mark at the third attempt over hurdles in 2m4f maiden here in January (soft-to-heavy); two runs since in much better company suggest she'll struggle again here.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner but recent form leave her with lots to find

races.race_name: Ballymore Champion Four Year Old Hurdle (Grade 1)

runners.name: Apolon De Charnie
runners.spotlight: French recruit failed to win in two runs for Yannick Fouin (6l behind Proactif on his second start at Auteuil); sent off at 50-1 on his first start for Willie Mullins in the Triumph Hurdle (2m1f, soft) but belied those odds to win by 1.5l; obvious chance of following up, and the forecast rain won't be an issue for him; preferred by Townend.
runners.comment: Shock winner of Triumph on stable debut, obvious chance and preferred by Paul Townend

runners.name: Saratoga
runners.spotlight: Flat winner for Aidan O'Brien; hinted there was a bigger performance in him when placed in first three maiden hurdles, one over this C&D (yielding) and twice at Naas on softer ground; duly delivered the goods on handicap debut, winning the Fred Winter at Cheltenham (2m, gd-sft) by 2.25l off 130; steps into Grade 1 company for the first time and very much respected.
runners.comment: Won the Fred Winter on latest off 130, very much respected in first Grade 1

runners.name: Charme De Faust
runners.spotlight: Placed on sole start in France; easy winner on debut for this yard at Thurles in January (2m, heavy); respectable efforts twice since, 3.5l fourth of 22 in mares' Grade 2 at Cheltenham; 9.25l third of 11 behind Zanoosh in mares' Grade 1 at Fairyhouse; needs to find a bit more to win this.
runners.comment: Thurles maiden hurdle winner, respectable runs up in class last twice, more needed

runners.name: Macho Man
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner at Auteuil (2m2f, very soft) on debut for Gabriel  Leenders in October; beaten favourite behind Proactif on first start for Willie Mullins in a 4yo hurdle at Fairyhouse (2m, yielding); pulled up after a bad mistake at the fourth in the Triumph; back on track when beaten a neck by Proactif in Grade 2 at Fairyhouse (2m, yld-sft); in the mix with a repeat of that.
runners.comment: PU in the Triumph, back to form in Fairyhouse Grade 2 latest, in the mix with a repeat

runners.name: One Horse Town
runners.spotlight: British raider from yard that had a Grade 1 winner here earlier this week; won first four starts over hurdles on varying ground; fine run when 2l behind Maestro Conti in Cheltenham Grade 2 in January (2m1f, soft); beaten 13l when runner up in another Grade 2 at Kempton; beaten 26l in the Triumph on latest; much more needed.
runners.comment: British raider, won first four hurdles, limitations exposed last twice, much more needed

runners.name: Moulin Labbe
runners.spotlight: French AQPS winner; well-beaten on Irish debut at Naas (2m, heavy) in January; justified short odds on next start when winning by 13l at Navan (2m, heavy) a  month later; last of seven finishers when behind a couple of these in Fairyhouse Grade 2 latest; rated 123 and looks to be up against it in this company.
runners.comment: French AQPS and Navan maiden hurdle winner, outclassed in Gr 2 latest, tough task

runners.name: Matin Midi Et Soir
runners.spotlight: Won two of three starts in France; bolted up by 13l on debut in a 3yo hurdle at Auteuil; runner up in 17.5f Grade 3 (very soft) back there last May; went one place better in another Auteuil Grade 3 (2m2f, very soft) when last seen in September (subsequent Grade 3 winner 8.5l behind); lacks a recent outing but he's an exciting prospect and no surprise to see a big run on his first start for Henry De Bromhead.
runners.comment: 2/3 in France inc' Gr' 3 at Auteuil, very exciting prospect, go well on stable debut

runners.name: Bartholomew
runners.spotlight: Minor promise in three runs on the Flat (rated 76); nice run on hurdles debut at Leopardstown in December; built on that to land a Cork maiden (yielding) under a positive ride in a first-time tongue tie (retained), beating a 121 rated horse by 10l; this is harder but he's well-regarded and unexposed; could go well, though the forecast rain is a concern.
runners.comment: Bolted up in second start over timber at Cork, well-regarded, unexposed, rain a worry

runners.name: Majolique
runners.spotlight: French Flat winner; won a 4yo Listed hurdle at Naas (15.5f, heavy) on Irish and stable debut; found just Proactif and Macho Man too good in Grade 2 Juvenile at Fairyhouse (2m, yld-sft) on latest when a 2.25l third; if the forecast rain arrives she could get much closer to those two rivals but has to be viewed as a negative that Paul Townend has jumped ship.
runners.comment: Listed hurdle win on Irish debut, not far behind two of these at Fairyhouse, rain a plus

runners.name: Bertutea
runners.spotlight: French Flat AW winner; won a Limerick maiden hurdle (2m, heavy) in dominant fashion on his Irish debut; not so good since, pulled up in a Grade 1 Juvenile Hurdle at the DRF and beaten 28l when down the field in the Fred Winter at Cheltenham; others have more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: AW winner, impressive on hurdles debut but poor twice since, others preferred

runners.name: Madness d'Elle
runners.spotlight: Placed on sole start in France for Damien Mele at Auteuil (1m7f, very soft) last May; beaten at skinny odds over this C&D (sft-hvy) on Irish debut in January; made amends despite racing keenly at Gowran (2m, heavy), making most to score by 2.75l; never involved when beaten 23l in the Fred Winter at Cheltenham (16.5f, gd-sft); needs to leave that behind but the forecast rain is at least in his favour.
runners.comment: Gowran maiden winner on heavy, well beaten in Fred Winter, forecast rain a plus

runners.name: Kai Lung
runners.spotlight: Won over 7.5f on the Flat in France (soft); beat Saratoga when landing a 4yo maiden hurdle at Naas in January (2m, heavy) but has seemingly had his limitations exposed in the Triumph at Cheltenham (pulled up) and a Grade 2 at Fairyhouse (unseated when beaten); can only be watched after those two efforts.
runners.comment: Naas maiden hurdle winner, well beaten upped in class twice since, can only watch now

races.race_name: Lawlor's Of Naas Handicap Hurdle (Listed Race)

runners.name: Dani Donadoni
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; off the mark at the fourth attempt over hurdles in a Limerick maiden (2m3f, soft) in March, winning by 8.5l; that form has taken some knocks since and opening mark doesn't look overly generous; probably best watched in first handicap.
runners.comment: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner, opening mark not overly generous, likely best watched

runners.name: Fierce Handay
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner, both at Fairyhouse; won a 2m (soft) novice handicap off 109 there in December; added a 21.5f handicap (yld-sft) off 122 last month, scoring by 5.5l; 9lb rise for that seems fair; should be competitive again but wouldn't want to see too much rain (well beaten in four previous starts on soft to heavy).
runners.comment: Two-time Fairyhouse handicap winner, up 9lb for easy win latest, forecast rain a negative

runners.name: Jump Allen
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; won two handicaps last season, over 19.5f (soft) at Naas off 114 and then off 14lb higher at Sandown (2m4f, good); excellent 3.25l third despite trouble in running on seasonal reappearance in the Martin Pipe (20.5f, soft) when sent off at just 8-1 and 2lb higher now; Paul Townend now takes over; leading claims.
runners.comment: Excellent third last time in Martin Pipe, just 2lb higher now, Townend up now, claims

runners.name: Storm Heart
runners.spotlight: Consistent sort with plenty of smart form in the book; won first two starts this season, a 2m4f hurdle at Limerick (heavy) and the Grade 3 Red Mills at Gowran; respectable third in BetMGM Cup at Cheltenham off 151; 7.75l third of 10 in Fairyhouse Grade 2 latest; rider's claim a big plus and forecast rain in his favour; should go well.
runners.comment: Consistent, smart graded and h'cap form this season, rain and rider's claim positives

runners.name: County Final
runners.spotlight: Navan maiden hurdle winner in September (2m3f, yielding); runner up in a novice handicap here (2m1f, sft-hvy) in November and beaten a head in a Grade 2 Novice at Limerick the following month; pulled up (coughing post-race) and soundly beaten in handicaps the last twice though so a revival is needed.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner, runner up in Grade 2 in Dec', poor last twice, needs to revive

runners.name: Letos
runners.spotlight: Won the Brown Lad at Naas (2m4f, sft-hvy) in November off 119; 3.25l runner up in Pertemps qualifier at Carlisle (3m1f, soft) off revised mark in December; soundly beaten in Naas Grade 1 but bounced back with a respectable 7.25l fourth in the Pertemps at Cheltenham (3m, good), not quite getting home; could go well back in trip returned to softer ground.
runners.comment: Won the Brown Lad in November, solid run in Pertemps latest, drop in trip a plus

runners.name: Iberico Lord
runners.spotlight: Ground versatile sort is 4-15 over hurdles; back to form in fine style when landing the Lanzarote at Kempton in January (2m5f, good) off 136; hiked up 8lb and came up short in the BetMGM Cup at Cheltenham, though did meet trouble in running; dropped 1lb but may need more help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Back to form with easy win in Lanzarote, beaten off 8lb higher at Cheltenham, tough mark

runners.name: Laurets d'Estruval
runners.spotlight: Placed on heavy sole start in France; runner up on stable debut in 4yo maiden at Limerick (2m, heavy); went one place better at Thurles (2m, soft) in February, winning by 13l; midfield in the Turners at Cheltenham latest (2m5f, gd-sft); not discounted on handicap debut with forecast rain in his favour.
runners.comment: Thurles maiden winner on soft, beaten in Turners latest, can't discount on h'cap bow

runners.name: Thisistheway
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over timber; career highlight a 1.25l win in Red Mills Auction Final at this meeting last year (19.5f, yielding); excellent effort when a 3.5l second behind Champagne Kid on last start over hurdles at Leopardstown in December (20.5f, gd-yld); fit from a spin on the Flat last month but very soft ground would not be ideal; 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: Won auction final here last year, fine effort at Leopardstown in Dec', needs rain to miss

runners.name: Tripoli Flyer
runners.spotlight: Fruitful novice campaign included a Grade 2 win at Kempton (2m, gd-sft); best run this season a 2.75l second in an Ascot handicap (19.5f, soft) in cheekpieces which return now; not a bad effort when a keeping on 7l seventh in the County Hurdle (2m1f, soft); modest run on the Flat latest; not discounted back up in trip over timber.
runners.comment: Grade 2 Juvenile winner, decent efforts last two h'cap hurdles, not discounted

runners.name: Champagne Kid
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; off the mark at the eighth attempt over hurdles in a maiden here in November (2m6f, sft-hvy); followed up in a Listed handicap hurdle at Leopardstown (20.5f, gd-yld) in December; raised 7lb and pulled up in Listed handicap at the DRF on latest but 3m on heavy may have stretched him; could rebound dropped in trip; tongue tie added.
runners.comment: Won off 7lb lower at Leopardstown in Dec', 3m too far latest, drops in trip; tongue tied

runners.name: Mojoe
runners.spotlight: Has won two of his last three starts, a 19.5f handicap here (sft-hvy) off 107 in January and a 2m3f handicap at Naas (sft-hvy) on latest in March; career high mark now but he looks progressive and the forecast rain won't inconvenience him; 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: Won two of last three inc' here, looks progressive, forecast rain no inconvenience

runners.name: Hello Neighbour
runners.spotlight: Won Grade 1 Juvenile at the DRF last season; below best first three starts this season but better effort when a 7.5l third of 18 in Listed handicap hurdle at Fairyhouse latest (2m, yld-sft); same mark here but stamina to prove for this longer trip.
runners.comment: Grade 1 Juvenile winner, back to form at Fairyhouse latest, stamina to prove for this trip

runners.name: Kel Histoire
runners.spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner; beaten 3l when runner up behind Salvator Mundi in Grade 2 novice at this track (2m1f, soft) in January 2025; no show in two heavy ground Grade 3s on first two runs this term; fine effort in Martin Pipe off 137 on latest and effectively 6lb lower here with rider's claim; chance but forecast rain a concern.
runners.comment: Back to form with fine effort in Martin Pipe latest, chance but forecast rain a concern

runners.name: Kopeck De Mee
runners.spotlight: Highly touted French recruit has yet to win in four starts for Willie Mullins; flopped when favourite for the 2025 Martin Pipe on stable debut; narrowly denied in premier handicap at Aintree; tailed off when falling on seasonal reappearance in a Navan beginners' chase; midfield in BetMGM Cup latest; 3lb drop not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Standout effort at Aintree last year, hard to fancy on what he has done this season

runners.name: No Looking Back
runners.spotlight: Smart form as a younger horse but on losing run since Grade 2 success at Limerick in December 2022; a step in the right direction in a 2m Cork conditions hurdle on latest but he's unproven at this trip; 4lb wrong and hard to fancy.
runners.comment: On long losing run, better effort latest but unproven at this trip, hard to fancy

runners.name: Sony Bill
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner on heavy in France is 0-7 for Willie Mullins; below best first two runs this season but more like it at Fairyhouse on latest when staying on for a 7.5l fourth over 2m (yld-sft); same mark here, forecast rain a positive and this trip should suit; interesting.
runners.comment: Yet to win for Willie Mullins, stayed on well over 2m latest, rain a plus, interesting

races.race_name: Sebden Steel Celebrating 30 Years In Business (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race

runners.name: Found It
runners.spotlight: Maxios gelding; off the mark at the second attempt in point-to-points at Ballygogue House in February for Pat Doyle; that form doesn't look overly special but he's in good hands and has to be respected on Rules' debut.
runners.comment: Point winner for Pat Doyle, Maxios gelding respected on Rules' bow for top yard

runners.name: The Captain's Word
runners.spotlight: Promise in both starts in point-to-points at Damma House and Nenagh for Colin Bowe; dam a bumper winner; in good hands and interesting on Rules' debut.
runners.comment: Promise in both points for Colin Bowe, dam a bumper winner, interesting on Rules' debut
runners.owner: E M Walsh & T O'Sullivan & Lord Waterford & R Ames

runners.name: Crystasia
runners.spotlight: Crystal Ocean gelding; 31,000GBP yearling; dam bumper and 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner, sister to useful 2m5f hurdle/3m1f chase winner Everglow; appeals on paper and in good hands; could go well.
runners.comment: Crystal Ocean gelding, dam a bumper and jumps winner, appeals on paper

runners.name: Runningtostandstil
runners.spotlight: Walk In The Park gelding; half-brother to 2m5f hurdle winner Lord Ffrench; dam bumper/2m hurdle winner, sister to 1m2f-1m5f Flat/smart 2m hurdle winner Cockney Sparrow; in good hands and top rider booked; should go well for yard that has won two of the last four renewals and owner has already had a bumper winner here this week.
runners.comment: Walk In The Park gelding, dam bumper winner, yard has won two of last four renewals

runners.name: Getaway Tobin
runners.spotlight: Getaway mare; E60,000 3yo; half-sister to Monkfish (high-class 2m5f-3m hurdle/chase); dam placed bumper/2m-2m6f hurdles; well-bred and yard can ready one; tongue tied; check the market.
runners.comment: E60,000 Getaway mare, half-sister to top class Monkfish, check the market

runners.name: Kells House
runners.spotlight: Home-bred Mount Nelson mare; half-sister to winners Blackbow (high-class bumper and 2m1f/2m4f chase) and Banntown Girl (useful bumper/2m hurdle); dam unraced; yard has had success in this sphere; interesting on paper.
runners.comment: Mount Nelson mare, half-sister to top bumper performer Blackbow, interesting

runners.name: Mia In Milan
runners.spotlight: Soldier Of Fortune mare; half-sister to winners Whatdoyawanttoknow (2m3f hurdle) and Judge John Deed (point); dam unraced half-sister to smart 2m3f-3m1f hurdle/chase winner Tullymurry Toff; bred to need further than this in time.
runners.comment: Soldier Of Fortune mare, dam unraced, bred to need further than this in time

runners.name: Fiery Robin
runners.spotlight: Vadamos filly; sister to 1m7f/2m hurdle winner Star Of Guiting, half-sister to winners Petrossian (bumper) and Realistic Optimism (bumper/2m2f hurdle); dam unraced half-sister to useful On Raglan Road; good claimer booked; check the betting.
runners.comment: Vadamos filly, half-sister to two bumper winners, good claimer booked, check betting

runners.name: Market Square
runners.spotlight: Order Of St George gelding; E32,000 3yo; dam unraced half-sister to winners Vics Canvas (bumper and smart 2m4f-3m4f hurdle/chase), Art Lord (2m-3m hurdle/chase) and Horse Force One (bumper/2m2f hurdle); hooded for debut; check market.
runners.comment: Order Of St George gelding, dam unraced, hooded for debut, check the betting

runners.name: Ballypollard
runners.spotlight: E34,000 3yo son of Califet; lots of stamina in pedigree; won sole start in point-to-point at Kirkistown in March by 4l; that form looks ordinary but experience should stand to him and yard won this in 2023.
runners.comment: Won by 4l in Kirkistown point, stamina in pedigree, yard won this in 2023, chance

runners.name: Casper Charlie
runners.spotlight: Berkshire gelding; brother to bumper winner Amelia's Star, half-brother to winners Treasure Mountain (bumper) and Toppy's Boy (2m2f hurdle); dam unraced; rider booking a plus; check market.
runners.comment: Berkshire gelding, related to two bumper winners, rider booking a plus, check market

runners.name: Tale Of Intrigue
runners.spotlight: Fascinating Rock gelding; half-brother to French 9.5f AW winner Guitry (RPR 82); dam unplaced 6f/7f 2yo (48), half-sister to Flat winner Foundation (1m 2yo Group 2); others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Fascinating Rock gelding; dam unplaced 6f/7f, others make more appeal

runners.name: Working Class Hero
runners.spotlight: Had a subsequent bumper winner behind when third in a Dromahane point on debut for Luke Murphy in November 2024; absent since pulling up at Borris House the following month; probably best watched after such a long absence.
runners.comment: Promise on point debut, off since pulled up at Borris House in Dec' 2024, watch

runners.name: Ard Ri Na Heireann
runners.spotlight: Arizona gelding; half-brother to winners Saga Sprint (1m2f; RPR 74), Cliffs Of Malta (1m3f AW; 64) and Saga Romane (Italian 5.5f/7f); dam unraced half-sister to useful 7f-8.6f winner Ballard Down; stamina a query on breeding; likely best watched.
runners.comment: Arizona gelding, half-brother to three Flat winners, likely best watched

runners.name: Barrow Side Boy
runners.spotlight: Lucky Speed gelding; dam unraced half-sister to winners Golden Sunrise (2m2f hurdle/3m chase) and Queens Present (point/2m4f hurdle), out of bumper winning sister to Kicking King; likely to need further than this in time.
runners.comment: Lucky Speed gelding, dam from family of Kicking King, may need further in time

runners.name: Jacks Bridge
runners.spotlight: The Irish Rover gelding; E1,000 yearling; half-brother to bumper winner King Ulanda; dam maiden on Flat/over hurdles, half-sister to two hurdle/chase winners; best watched.
runners.comment: The Irish Rover gelding, half-brother to bumper winner but likely best watched

races.race_name: 35th Running Of The "Have The Conversation - Say Yes To Organ Donation" Punchestown Charity Race

runners.name: Mordor
runners.spotlight: Rated 126 over fences and 94 on the Flat, 1m6f win off 87 at Navan latest, leading chance.
runners.comment: Rated 126 over fences and 94 on the Flat, 1m6f win off 87 at Navan latest, leading chance

runners.name: Touch The Moon
runners.spotlight: Three Flat wins at 1m-1m3f on fast ground, rated 90, chance if forecast rain misses.
runners.comment: Three Flat wins at 1m-1m3f on fast ground, rated 90, chance if forecast rain misses

runners.name: Dark Note
runners.spotlight: Two-time Flat winner rated 71, won this last year, yard form a plus, should go well.
runners.comment: Two-time Flat winner rated 71, won this last year, yard form a plus, should go well

runners.name: Jungle Cove
runners.spotlight: 3-34 on the Flat and rated 74, off since poor run at Laytown last September.
runners.comment: 3-34 on the Flat and rated 74, off since poor run at Laytown last September

runners.name: Lightkeeper
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner, rated 128 over fences, flat debut, top yard, respected.
runners.comment: Four-time winner, rated 128 over fences, flat debut, top yard, respected

runners.name: Thewaybackhome
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdler rated 88, 0-3 in points, others have stronger credentials.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdler rated 88, 0-3 in points, others have stronger credentials

runners.name: Moonshine Girl
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden over hurdles and fences, hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Modest maiden over hurdles and fences, hard to make a case for

runners.name: Roxboro River
runners.spotlight: 1-12 over hurdles, rated 100, poor run after break at Clonmel latest, others preferred.
runners.comment: 1-12 over hurdles, rated 100, poor run after break at Clonmel latest, others preferred

runners.name: Ethereal Star
runners.spotlight: 0-8 on the Flat and rated just 42, seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: 0-8 on the Flat and rated just 42, seems safe to rule out

runners.name: Game And Glory
runners.spotlight: Two-time hurdle winner but rated just 87, pulled up latest, up against it here.
runners.comment: Two-time hurdle winner but rated just 87, pulled up latest, up against it here

runners.name: Bucaneer's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Four Flat wins at 1m-1m1f and rated 66 in that sphere, stamina a query over this trip.
runners.comment: Four Flat wins at 1m-1m1f and rated 66 in that sphere, stamina a query for this trip

runners.name: Pride Of Place
runners.spotlight: 122-rated chaser, best form over much further than this, trip may be on sharp side.
runners.comment: 122-rated chaser, best form over much further than this, trip may be on sharp side

runners.name: Carnfunnock
runners.spotlight: Two-time hurdles winner, rated 126 over fences, 3m his best trip, this too sharp.
runners.comment: Two-time hurdles winner, rated 126 over fences, 3m his best trip, this too sharp

runners.name: Oogum Boogum
runners.spotlight: 0-18 over hurdles and rated 84, pulled up in Tramore chase latest, others preferred.
runners.comment: 0-18 over hurdles and rated 84, pulled up in Tramore chase latest, others preferred

runners.name: Smackwater Jack
runners.spotlight: Dual-hurdles winner, rated 85 in that sphere, out of form this season, unlikely.
runners.comment: Dual-hurdles winner, rated 85 in that sphere, out of form this season, unlikely

runners.name: Riggs
runners.spotlight: Veteran rated 109 over fences, out of form when last seen in August, would be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Rated 109 over fences, out of form when last seen in August, would be a surprise winner
runners.owner: Paul M Butler & James Patrick Byrne

runners.name: Eyeeye
runners.spotlight: Maiden, rated 90 over hurdles and 50 on the Flat, off since September, others appeal more.
runners.comment: Maiden, rated 90 over hurdles and 50 on the Flat, off since September, others appeal more

runners.name: L'Un Deux Trois
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner rated 64, on long losing run, off since August, hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Three-time Flat winner rated 64, on long losing run, off since August, hard to fancy

runners.name: Buttermere Ridge
runners.spotlight: First reserve; 1-8 on the Flat and rated 71, off since poor run in Oct', oppose.
runners.comment: First reserve; 1-8 on the Flat and rated 71, off since poor run in Oct', oppose

runners.name: Indulging
runners.spotlight: Modest hurdles form, 0-14 on the Flat and rated 54, up against it.
runners.comment: Modest hurdles form, 0-14 on the Flat and rated 54, up against it

runners.name: In Excelsis Deo
runners.spotlight: Rated in 140s at peak over fences, best form over much further, others preferred.
runners.comment: Rated in 140s at peak over fences, best form over much further, others preferred

runners.name: Solar Falcon
runners.spotlight: Won a Gowran maiden hurdle in Oct' 2024, seen just twice since, hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Won a Gowran maiden hurdle in Oct' 2024, seen just twice since, hard to fancy

races.race_name: QuinnBet Daily Free Bet Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Diesel Line
runners.spotlight: Both wins over hurdles have been over C&D, the latest in September off 6lb lower; will have benefited from his recent pipe-opener when fourth at Market Rasen (2m,good) and should run well.
runners.comment: Both wins over hurdles have been over C&D, the latest in September off 6lb lower.

runners.name: Portcammon
runners.spotlight: 0-10 over hurdles and continues to drop in the weights; doesn't look ready to take advantage.
runners.comment: 0-10 over hurdles and continues to drop in the weights.

runners.name: Genbu
runners.spotlight: Landed a juvenile hurdle for Sam England last October; sold to join new yard for 1,400gns in February but finished last of ten to complete on stable debut at Stratford (2m, good) in March; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Finished last on stable debut at Stratford in March.

runners.name: Saccary
runners.spotlight: Yet to score over hurdles but has finished runner-up on his last two starts, the latest over C&D (good to soft) in March; needs to brush up his jumping but should run well.
runners.comment: Yet to score over hurdles but has finished runner-up on his last two starts.

runners.name: Auntie Maggie
runners.spotlight: Won three around this time last year, latest over C&D (good to soft); beaten four times since, the last two over fences; switches back to hurdles and worth a look in the betting.
runners.comment: Won three around this time last year, latest over C&D.

runners.name: Raffles Nobu
runners.spotlight: Sole success came when trained in France in 2023; has had plenty of chances since and his mark continues to drop; blinkers replace cheekpieces and not one to have faith in.
runners.comment: Sole success came when trained in France in 2023; has had plenty of chances since.

runners.name: Desert Halo
runners.spotlight: No joy in four goes over fences and made a winning return on his handicap debut in this sphere at Plumpton (2m, good to soft) in March; bumped into an improver when second at Hereford (2m, good) last month and should be on the premises.
runners.comment: Bumped into an improver when second at Hereford last month.

runners.name: Playful Fox
runners.spotlight: Has shown little in three runs, latest when last of five at Catterick (1m7f, soft) in January on handicap debut; should be capable of better however, and he's in the hands of the newly-crowned champion NH trainer.
runners.comment: Has shown little in three runs, latest when last at Catterick in January.

runners.name: Little Soiree
runners.spotlight: Sole success came at Huntingdon (2m, good) last May when trained by Chris Gordon; hasn't shown anywhere near the same form since.
runners.comment: Sole success came at Huntingdon last May when trained by Chris Gordon.

runners.name: Kalkaroo
runners.spotlight: Doubled his tally over hurdles when winning at Market Rasen (2m, soft) in February; has held his form in three runs since and holds place claims at least.
runners.comment: Doubled his tally over hurdles when winning at Market Rasen in February.

runners.name: Ten Ten Twenty
runners.spotlight: Dual purpose sort who hasn't stood much racing since winning here (2m4f, soft) in July 2024; first run in this sphere since when a faller at Hereford in December; may need further and softer conditions to be at his best.
runners.comment: Dual purpose sort who hasn't stood much racing since winning here in July 2024.

runners.name: Iolaos Du Mou
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over hurdles at Warwick (2m, good) last May off 6lb lower; may need the run following 11 months off.
runners.comment: Got off the mark over hurdles at Warwick last May off 6lb lower.

runners.name: Crown Of India
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark in a Worcester maiden (2m, good) last June but has been beaten in four handicaps since; his mark continues to drop but he doesn't look ready to take advantage.
runners.comment: Landed a Worcester maiden last June but has been beaten in four handicaps since.

runners.name: Spartan Times
runners.spotlight: Landed a 2m Worcester novice last June before winning four on the Flat between 1m2f-1m3f; last seen when beaten 22l in a Bangor novice (2m, soft) in November; has scope for further improvement in this sphere and an interesting contender on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Last seen when beaten 22l in a Bangor novice in November; handicap debut.

runners.name: Now Then Wendy
runners.spotlight: Yet to be placed from nine starts over hurdles; easy to dismiss.
runners.comment: Yet to be placed from nine starts over hurdles; easy to dismiss.

runners.name: Westerton
runners.spotlight: Sole success on the Flat came in a Sandown maiden in 2023 when trained by Alan King; no joy in three runs over hurdles; sold for 10,000gns to join new yard in October; makes handicap debut and probably best watched for now.
runners.comment: No joy in three runs over hurdles for Alan King; handicap/stable debut.
runners.owner: A Baxter, S, Sowray, K Ward

races.race_name: Support The Stoke City Foundation "National Hunt" Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Trigg
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut in a Worcester bumper in September and ran with credit when runner-up in similar events at Sedgefield and Chepstow; sure to have been well-schooled and should take plenty of beating on this first start over hurdles.
runners.comment: Won bumper at Worcester and second in a couple of similar contests; hurdles debut.

runners.name: Lexington Wood
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in three Irish bumpers last year; sold to join current yard for £105,000 in November; novicey jumping, in at the deep end, on his hurdles debut in a Grade 2 at Kempton (2m, good to soft) when eighth of ten finishers in February; sure to have been well schooled since and could be a different proposition here.
runners.comment: Novicey jumping on his hurdles debut in a Grade 2 at Kempton when eighth in February.

runners.name: Barry Fife
runners.spotlight: Made a promising debut in this sphere when second here (2m7f, good) in March 2025; beaten 25l when seventh of nine on his first run since back here (2m4f, soft) in March; step down in trip not certain to suit.
runners.comment: Seventh on his first run for 12 months in March; step down in trip not certain to suit.

runners.name: Not At That Price
runners.spotlight: Showed up well until fading from two out on the second of two starts over hurdles; holds place claims.
runners.comment: Showed up well until fading from two out on the second of two starts over hurdles.

runners.name: Pappariche
runners.spotlight: Won a small-field Irish point last February (heavy ground) but well beaten in a couple of Ffos Las maidens over hurdles last month.
runners.comment: Well beaten in a couple of Ffos Las maidens over hurdles last month.

runners.name: Pretty Darn Quick
runners.spotlight: Has shown poor form in three bumpers; massive improvement required on this hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Has shown poor form in three bumpers; massive improvement required on this hurdles debut.

runners.name: Maestrow
runners.spotlight: Showed little in four bumpers but no surprise were he to show improvement on this hurdles debut for good yard.
runners.comment: Showed little in four bumpers but may show improvement on this hurdles debut.

runners.name: Noble Don
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in the point-to-point sphere in 2023 but his two efforts over hurdles in March leave a lot to be desired.
