runners.comment: His two efforts in this sphere in March leave a lot to be desired.

runners.name: Pic N Mix
runners.spotlight: Down the field in a couple of bumpers for Michael Keady at the end of last year; this looks to be a tall order on his hurdles/stable debut.
runners.comment: Down the field in a couple of bumpers for Michael Keady at the end of last year.

races.race_name: Support The Stoke City Foundation "National Hunt" Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Tap Tap Shamie
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who made promising rules debut when close second at Wincanton (1m7f, good to soft) in February; raced too freely and was turned over and skinny odds at Carlisle (2m3f, good to soft) last month but drops back in trip with a hood fitted here; remains of strong interest.
runners.comment: Second on both hurdling starts, albeit beaten at skinny odds last time; big player

runners.name: On The Bayou
runners.spotlight: Found shock 300-1 winner too strong on hurdle debut at Exeter (2m, heavy) in December and again a beaten favourite when only fifth of 16 over that C&D (good to soft) in March; however, he was well clear of the others on the first occasion and had excuses last time; has tongue-tie fitted today; can still do better.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite on both hurdling starts but probably has a big role to play here

runners.name: Evening Tess
runners.spotlight: With a top stable but showed nothing more than minor promise in two Cheptstow hurdle races (both 2m, good to soft) towards end of last season.
runners.comment: Showed nothing more than minor promise on first two hurdling starts

runners.name: Saxon Cross
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in good-ground Perth bumper for an Irish stable last summer but did not get seriously competitive in two novice hurdles for Stuart Edmonds towards end of last season; significant improvement required.
runners.comment: Second in a bumper last summer but well held on both hurdling starts this year

runners.name: Final Surprise
runners.spotlight: Placed on Irish point debut in April 2025 and also showed promise in all three bumpers last season; should have a future over hurdles; considered each-way.
runners.comment: Placed in Irish point debut and has also shown promise in bumper; hurdle debut today

runners.name: Chedworth Boy
runners.spotlight: 35,000euros 3yo; tenth foal; half-brother to six winners including smart jumper Tea Clipper and useful jumper Rathlin Rose; dam useful Italian jumper; entitled to learn from this debut outing but still worth a glance in the betting.
runners.comment: Half-brother to smart jumper Tea Clipper and useful jumper Rathlin Rose; makes debut here

runners.name: Kingofthefrontier
runners.spotlight: Won five-runner maiden point (2m4f, good to soft) by 5l debut in March; has much more on his plate on this rules debut but good market support will make him of interest.
runners.comment: Won maiden point on debut in March; now makes his rules debut

runners.name: Anxious Moments
runners.spotlight: Came to grief on Irish point debut in March and struggled when 25-1 for last month's rules debut at Bangor (2m, good); remains best watched.
runners.comment: Struggled when 25-1 for last month's rules debut at Bangor; remains best watched

runners.name: Annual Invitation
runners.spotlight: Placed in two slow-ground Irish points during the winter but soundly beaten in two hurdles races (2m3f/2m) towards end of last season; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Placed in two Irish points but soundly beaten on both hurdling starts

races.race_name: Staffordshire Chamber Of Commerce Staffordshire Plate Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Calimystic
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 7yo who is 2-2 over fences, the latter win gained at Sandown (3m, soft) in March; withdrawn twice with good ground deemed unsuitable since but his first two wins (one over hurdles) were gained on good; probably still has more improvement to come; must be considered.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 7yo who is 2-2 over fences; probably still has more improvement to come

runners.name: Cruz Control
runners.spotlight: Took big step back in right direction when fifth at Aintree (3m1f, good to soft) three weeks ago, in race he won the previous two seasons; still on a workable mark and has a fighting chance here if first-time cheekpieces have any positive effect.
runners.comment: Still on workable mark after last month's Aintree fifth; player if aided by cheekpieces

runners.name: King's Threshold
runners.spotlight: Below form in Kim Muir at Cheltenham in March but showed career-best form when winning at Newbury (3m2f, good) just after Christmas and will be a danger to all here if back at that level in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Well held at Cheltenham Festival but big player if judged on Newbury win in December

runners.name: Party Vibes
runners.spotlight: Won four-runner mares' chase at Hereford (2m5f, good) last month and is now 4-9 over fences; this race is much more competitive but she could have untapped potential over 3m.
runners.comment: 4-9 over fences after last month's small-field win; unexposed over this sort of trip

runners.name: Joyeux Machin
runners.spotlight: Appreciated eased in grade at Bangor (3m, good to soft) six weeks ago, winning readily by 10l; contests deeper race here but probably still on workable mark after 5lb rise, and quicker ground also suits.
runners.comment: Appreciated eased in grade at Bangor six weeks ago, winning readily by 10l

runners.name: Bhaloo
runners.spotlight: Seemingly boosted by first-time cheekpieces when placed in Summer Plate at Market Rasen (2m5f, good) last year but looked out of sorts when pulled up twice in November and it's hard to predict whether this will be a going day.
runners.comment: Placed in Summer Plate last year but pulled up twice since; others are much less risky

runners.name: Peaky Boy
runners.spotlight: Early faller in February, after one-year layoff, and safely held in two major handicaps since (2m4f/2m5f); has more to prove than some of these but will be suited by the forecast good ground and is not written off just yet.
runners.comment: Below form during light campaign last season but not written off just yet

runners.name: Horantzau d'Airy
runners.spotlight: Now 5lb lower than when second for Micheal Keady here (2m4f, good) just under a year ago but both subsequent runs for Sara Bradstock were disappointing and he was well beaten in Cheltenham Festival cross-country chase on stable debut in March; now sports cheekpieces; needs to get career back on track.
runners.comment: Second over 2m4f here last May but returns here with quite a bit to prove; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Capodanno
runners.spotlight: High-class chaser for Willie Mullins when on song but became inconsistent for him and, following a one-year absence, was pulled up in veterans' race for new stable in February; current ability hard to gauge.
runners.comment: High-class chaser in his prime but pulled up in veterans' race on stable debut

races.race_name: Lord Thomas Playdough Ryan Novices' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Tom Desjy
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 6yo who ran big race when close third of five on chase/handicap debut at Fontwell (2m5f, good to soft; first-time cheekpieces) in March; moves up in trip here and his promising Irish point debut in 2024 suggests the forecast faster ground will suit; respected.
runners.comment: Ran big race when third on chase/handicap debut at Fontwell in March; respected

runners.name: Choice Runner
runners.spotlight: Still unexposed after only nine races but he was safely held in all three handicap hurdles (2m-2m4f) then pulled up when upped to 2m7f for last month's chasing debut; needs new tongue-tie to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Safely held in all three handicap hurdles then pulled up on chasing debut; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Rule Me In
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles on rules debut in February 2025 but disappointing since switched to chasing in the autumn (six attempts); still unexposed over this sort of trip but was beaten 40l+ over about 2m4f on both outings in March and needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: Unexposed over 3m but 0-6 and generally very disappointing since switched to fences

runners.name: Ultra Viers
runners.spotlight: Quite well handicapped on last spring's form but was well beaten when back on good ground at Hereford in March and is now 0-24 under rules.
runners.comment: Well treated on last spring's form but well beaten last time and now 0-24 under rules

runners.name: Crac De Megaudais
runners.spotlight: Won 2m4f hurdle at start of 2025 and made a fairly encouraging chase debut when third of six at Newton Abbot (2m, good) just under a year ago; returned from break with three disappointing runs in November-January, though, and has questions to answer on first attempt at 3m; hood refitted.
runners.comment: Ran quite well on chasing debut last May but out of form when last seen in winter

runners.name: Butchers Bill
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden; made low-key stable debut in October (breathing issue reported) then looked beaten when falling on latest outing in December; returns with quite a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Low-key stable debut in October; beaten when falling in December; quite a bit to prove

runners.name: Cher Monsieur
runners.spotlight: Battled well to win over hurdles at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) in January and also ran well when clear second to next-time-out winner at Southwell (2m4f, good; first-time cheekpieces) in March; moves back up in trip for this chasing debut and has Irish pointing experience to call upon; high on the list.
runners.comment: Struck form over hurdles this year and has pointing experience to call upon on chase debut

runners.name: Jo Arengeot Bey AA
runners.spotlight: 0-11 under rules; didn't run badly when back over hurdles in March but was safely held in both chases last season (2m7f/3m) and has become disappointing.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly over hurdles in March but was safely held in both previous chases

runners.name: Warner's Cross
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on chase debut in summer 2024 but returned from long absence with two disappointing hurdle runs towards end of last year and was also well beaten when back over fences in March (2m5f; first run after wind op); upped to 3m for first time under rules; needs to prove his ability remains.
runners.comment: Second on chase debut in 2024 but out of form last season, after a layoff

runners.name: Stonegate
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden who has no worthwhile form over jumps and was pulled up on his last two appearances; can't be recommended from out of the weights.
runners.comment: 14-race maiden who has no worthwhile form over jumps

runners.name: New Invention
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all six hurdle runs; might improve over fences at some stage but was well beaten when 33-1 for chasing debut at Bangor (3m, good) in March and could be considered only if much stronger in the betting here; swaps cheekpieces for first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Well beaten when 33-1 for chasing debut in March; headgear switched here

races.race_name: Pirtek UK & Ireland Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Sir Galahad
runners.spotlight: Completed a four-timer on his first four starts in this sphere at around 2m; held his form in defeat until weakening right out of it when 40-1 at Aintree (2m4f, good to soft) last month; the same distance is being persevered with but he's down in class and not out of this.
runners.comment: Four-timer on his first four starts in this sphere at around 2m; 40-1 when he tried 2m4f

runners.name: Bluegrass
runners.spotlight: All five wins over hurdles have been at 2m; hasn't won since his return to action in October and ran poorly when eighth of nine finishers at Chepstow (2m3f, good to soft) last month; handicapper is at last relenting but his best chance of success in the short-term may be back at 2m.
runners.comment: Ran poorly when eighth of nine finishers at Chepstow last month.

runners.name: Corsican Caper
runners.spotlight: Lowly rated these days on the Flat and has shown little in three runs over hurdles; makes handicap hurdle debut up in trip and needs to show significant improvement.
runners.comment: Lowly rated these days on the Flat and has shown little in three runs over hurdles.

runners.name: El Galactico
runners.spotlight: Made a good start over hurdles when winning twice after finishing second on his debut; the wheels have fallen off this year and he pulled up on his last two runs, latest in first-time cheekpieces (retained); a hood is now on for the first time; steps back in trip and has questions to answer.
runners.comment: Won twice over hurdles but pulled up on his last two runs.
runners.owner: Feilim O' Muiri And Brendan Barrett

runners.name: Crebilly
runners.spotlight: Continues to fall in the weights without showing any sign of taking advantage; first-time cheekpieces (retained) failed to spark him back to life when fifth of eight at Wincanton (2m4f, good) in March.
runners.comment: Continues to fall in the weights without showing any sign of taking advantage.

runners.name: Miss Maverick
runners.spotlight: Four of her five wins over hurdles have been at this track (three over today's distance); must overcome a lengthy absence and is 7lb higher than for her last success but she cannot he dismissed around here.
runners.comment: Four of her five wins over hurdles have been been at this track.

runners.name: Liverpool Knight
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame several times since his last win in September 2023; returns from a winter break on a career-low mark and not out of this if he is ready to roll.
runners.comment: Has made the frame several times since his last win in September 2023.

runners.name: Top Coeur
runners.spotlight: Fair form when reaching the frame in a bumper and two maiden hurdles (all 2m) in Ireland for Gavin Cromwell last year but below that form in two runs for current yard; needs to show more.
runners.comment: Below form in two runs for current yard; needs to show more.

runners.name: Whatsupwithyou
runners.spotlight: Grand servant to the yard with five wins in this sphere, the latest at Ascot (2m5f, soft) in December off 2lb lower; well held since and it's hard to see him being able to give weight away to these younger rivals.
runners.comment: Grand servant to yard but it's hard to see him being able to give weight away to these.

runners.name: Torrent
runners.spotlight: Got off to a flyer when winning two of his first three starts over hurdles in 2024 but has been unable to get his head in front since; his mark continues to drop and cheekpieces are back on replacing a visor; best watched.
runners.comment: Won two of his first three starts over hurdles but no wins since.

runners.name: Last Kingdom
runners.spotlight: Both hurdles wins when trained by Willie Mullins; has shown little in four starts for current yard and needs to come further down the weights.
runners.comment: Has shown little in four runs for current yard and needs to come further down the weights.

races.race_name: Pirtek Stoke Classic Novices' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Coolkatie
runners.spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles but would have finished closer but for being badly hampered last time when third at Fakenham (2m4f, good); the step up in trip should suit and she has good claims.
runners.comment: Would have finished closer but for being badly hampered last time when third at Fakenham.

runners.name: Walkinthepresent
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at the sixth attempt over hurdles, at Taunton (3m, good) in March; unable to match that effort when fourth of six finishers at Wincanton (3m, good) last month off 7lb higher but should not be far away.
runners.comment: Got off the mark at the sixth attempt over hurdles, at Taunton in March.

runners.name: Hillwalk
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a bumper on his rules debut in November 2024 and filled the same position when well backed on his handicap debut at Chepstow (2m3f, good to soft; 6-5) three weeks ago; runs off the same mark and should go close on this step up in distance.
runners.comment: Runner-up when well backed on his handicap debut at Chepstow three weeks ago.

runners.name: Good Boy Griff
runners.spotlight: The penny is slowly starting to drop and he ran his best race when beaten a short head at Bangor (2m3f, good) two weeks ago on his handicap debut; should go close, at the least, if stepping up in trip has a positive effect; Sean Bowen rides for the first time.
runners.comment: Ran his best race when beaten a short head at Bangor two weeks ago on handicap debut.

runners.name: Cinnodin
runners.spotlight: Showed fair form on his third hurdles start when runner-up at Stratford in October; below that form when fifth of seven at Exeter (2m7f, good to firm) on his handicap debut later that month; still early days and may show more here.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Stratford in October; below that form when fifth at Exeter later that month.

runners.name: Golly Gosh
runners.spotlight: Modest form in four starts over hurdles, latest when pulled up at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) in March on handicap debut; first-time tongue-tie is now fitted on this step up in trip; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Modest form in four starts over hurdles, latest when pulled up at Huntingdon.

runners.name: Jaja Jimmy
runners.spotlight: He is not progressing and made it 0-5 over hurdles when pulled up at Huntingdon (3m1f, good) in March in first-time cheekpieces (retained); needs to show more.
runners.comment: He is not progressing and made it 0-5 over hurdles when pulled up at Huntingdon in March.

runners.name: Saint Vic
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent 11-race maiden who finished seventh of 11 finishers at Chepstow on his return three weeks ago from 12 months off.
runners.comment: Seventh of 11 finishers at Chepstow on his return three weeks ago from 12 months off.

runners.name: Inca
runners.spotlight: Has failed to show any notable form in four runs over hurdles, handicap debut on latest; takes a big step up in trip but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Has failed to show any notable form in four runs over hurdles, handicap debut on latest

runners.name: Donttellyamumjack
runners.spotlight: In the money but well beaten in his last three starts; cheekpieces are now applied and they need to have a significant impact for him to be successful.
runners.comment: In the money but well beaten in his last three starts; cheekpieces are now applied.

runners.name: The Kings Soldier
runners.spotlight: Showed much more when fourth of nine finishers up in trip at Stratford (3m2f, good) 13 days ago on his handicap debut following wind surgery; has place claims if he can repeat the dose.
runners.comment: Showed much more when fourth of nine finishers up in trip at Stratford 13 days ago.

runners.name: Shinealight
runners.spotlight: Landed three-runner Irish bumper last May; has shown ability in three runs over hurdles but needs more stepping up in trip on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Needs more stepping up in trip on this handicap debut.

races.race_name: QuinnBet Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Ukantango
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner and has three wins over hurdles; got off the mark on the return to fences when a convincing winner at Southwell (2m4f, good) last month; up 7lb but looks sure to give another bold account.
runners.comment: Got off the mark on the return to fences when a convincing winner at Southwell last month.

runners.name: Ski Lodge
runners.spotlight: Yet to get off the mark over fences in five starts; cheekpieces worn last time are retained; steps back in trip but is getting expensive to follow.
runners.comment: Yet to get off the mark over fences in five starts; is getting expensive to follow.
runners.owner: Noel Fehily Racing Syndicate - Ski Lodge

runners.name: Pony Soprano
runners.spotlight: Has shown solid form in at least three of his four races over fences without winning; the handicapper has given him a chance on this return from a three-month break; both first-time tongue-tie and cheekpieces go on and any improvement should see him go close.
runners.comment: Has shown solid form in at least three of his four races over fences without winning.

runners.name: Nickelforce
runners.spotlight: Has three wins over fences but was beaten 28l when fourth of five finishers at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) four weeks ago on his return from a winter break; still 6lb higher than for his last success and others look better handicapped.
runners.comment: Has three wins over fences but was beaten 28l when fourth at Newton Abbot four weeks ago.

runners.name: Grand Clermont
runners.spotlight: Ex-French where all his winning was on heavy ground; difficult to assess on his British debut and the betting is probably the best guide to his chance.
runners.comment: Ex-French where all his winning was on heavy ground; market helpful on British debut

runners.name: Jet Smart
runners.spotlight: Won twice over fences last summer, including over C&D; won a couple of points last month and looks reasonably treated on this return to fences; not out of this.
runners.comment: Won twice over fences last summer, including over C&D; won a couple of points last month.

runners.name: Doyouknowwhatimean
runners.spotlight: Won four times for the Skeltons but, following a good second on his stable debut here (2m4f, heavy) in December, he has been pulled up on three of his last four starts; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: He has been pulled up on three of his last four starts; hard to fancy.

runners.name: Start Again
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over hurdles in a maiden at Exeter (2m2f, good to firm) in October and ran well when third at Ascot the following month; steps up in trip on his chasing debut and should run well.
runners.comment: Steps up in trip on his chasing debut and should run well.

runners.name: Tune In A Box
runners.spotlight: Has four wins over hurdles but is still looking for first chase success; has gone close on last two starts when placed; this drop back in trip may not suit as he looks a stayer in the making but he's not out of this.
runners.comment: Has four wins over hurdles but is still looking for his first chase success.

races.race_name: mybettingsites.com/ie Everything New On Irish Bookies Selling Hurdle

runners.name: Seeyouinmydreams
runners.spotlight: Four hurdle wins at trips ranging from 2m-2m4f; was running well in handicaps in the autumn after chancing her arm over fences last September and this is a very winnable race given the favourable terms; strong claims for top yard that's 4-5 here.
runners.comment: Four hurdle wins at 2m-2m4f; running well last autumn; favourably treated on these terms

runners.name: Demoiselle Kap
runners.spotlight: Four wins over hurdles and fences in 2025 (about 2m2f; ground ranging from good to soft); not in the same form since returning from a layoff in January, including in a Stratford seller in March, but this is easier and she's a viable option.
runners.comment: Four hurdle/chase wins in 2025; below par now but still a viable option in a weak race

runners.name: Mr Globalist
runners.spotlight: Winless after 26 hurdle starts and although he often runs respectably at the right level, he has no realistic chance against rivals who mostly would be giving him plenty of weight in a handicap.
runners.comment: Winless after 26 hurdle starts; has a mountain to claim at these unfavourable weights

runners.name: Palm Beach
runners.spotlight: Has won over hurdles for three different yards, most recently at Worcester (2m7f, good) last July, but he's been pulled up in three starts for this stable and drops into a seller with masses to find at the weights.
runners.comment: Out of sorts for this yard and has masses to find at the weights

runners.name: Starman
runners.spotlight: Three hurdle wins in Ireland during 2023 but a close second over fences at Catterick (about 1m7f, good) in January came from out of the blue and hasn't been repeated since; returns to hurdles with a cloud hanging over him.
runners.comment: Three hurdle wins in Ireland; one good chase run for this yard but basically under a cloud

runners.name: Bray Dale
runners.spotlight: Encouraging handicap debut when third at Ayr (2m, soft) last March but soundly beaten or pulled up in the meantime and no improvement for cheekpieces at Ayr last time out; visored this time; all uphill on these terms.
runners.comment: Encouraging handicap debut last March but very modest form since; no realistic chance here

races.race_name: Bobby Roberts Memorial Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Crackalackin
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer who won over 1m4f on Polytrack last autumn; satisfactory start over hurdles when 6l third of four at Fakenham (2m, good) last month, jumping soundly in the main; open to improvement and high on the list.
runners.comment: 1m4f winner on Flat; satisfactory hurdling debut when third at Fakenham; high on the list

runners.name: Lipstick Traces
runners.spotlight: Usually runs some sort of race and she was third of eight at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) at the end of March, fading; will be fine back at around 2m; each-way possibilities.
runners.comment: Usually runs some sort of race; third at Newcastle last time; each-way possibilities

runners.name: Passing Diamond
runners.spotlight: Won two points in Ireland; third in two hurdle races, although the latest effort at Sedgefield was a step in the wrong direction; needs to bounce back and may prove ideally suited by longer distances.
runners.comment: Third in two hurdle races, although latest effort was a step in the wrong direction

runners.name: Cloud Free
runners.spotlight: 1-25 on Flat, tailed off when last seen at Southwell in January; bit to prove on hurdling debut but this is not a strong contest and he would have a squeak if taking well to the new discipline.
runners.comment: 1-25 on Flat, tailed off last time, but not ruled out if taking well to hurdling

runners.name: Crest Of Arms
runners.spotlight: Seemed to go wrong and was pulled up early when favourite for bumper at Taunton (tongue tied) six weeks ago, but he'd previously shaped quite well when seventh of 16 in a Listed event at Cheltenham (good ground) on New Year's Day; now goes hurdling (is bred for the job) and worth another chance.
runners.comment: Pulled up last time but previously seventh in Listed bumper; worth another chance

runners.name: After The Ball
runners.spotlight: Some promise in Irish points but hasn't achieved much in two runs over hurdles, always behind at Stratford (50-1) two weeks ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Some promise in Irish points but hasn't achieved much over hurdles; others appeal more

runners.name: Jet Noir
runners.spotlight: Third in an Irish point but beaten at least 25l in two runs under rules; improvement required.
runners.comment: Third in an Irish point but beaten at least 25l in two runs under rules

runners.name: Mickthesoldier
runners.spotlight: Won a point in April 2024 (soft/heavy) but recent runs in that sphere have been underwhelming; perhaps he'll be revived by a switch to Phil Kirby's stable but he's probably best watched unless strong in the market.
runners.comment: Recent point runs have been underwhelming; new stable today; probably best watched

runners.name: The Brave Guy
runners.spotlight: Placed in a heavy-ground Irish point in March 2024; well held in two runs over hurdles, jumping none too fluently; major improvement required.
runners.comment: Well held in two runs over hurdles, jumping none too fluently; major improvement required

races.race_name: Bobby Roberts Memorial Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Just A Poet
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much on her first two starts but there was more encouragement in a close sixth of ten in novice at Kelso (2m, good to soft; 300-1) last month; may improve further.
runners.comment: More encouragement when sixth at Kelso last month and may improve further

runners.name: Earnest Belief
runners.spotlight: 0-6 on Flat for the Gosdens; perfectly satisfactory start over hurdles when third at Carlisle (2m1f, good to soft) six weeks ago, keeping on from midfield; open to improvement and high on the list.
runners.comment: Perfectly satisfactory start over hurdles when third at Carlisle; open to improvement

runners.name: Blue Skies Ahead
runners.spotlight: From a good jumping family and she's quite a promising type; won a point bumper in March 2025 and she wasn't hard pressed to account for six rivals in bumper here (good ground) three weeks ago; leading claims now tackling obstacles.
runners.comment: 2-3 so far; won bumper here three weeks ago; leading claims now tackling obstacles
runners.owner: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson

runners.name: Masekela
runners.spotlight: On a long losing run on the Flat but he ran creditably when last seen, finishing second at Redcar (1m2f, good; final outing for Tim Easterby) in October; changed hands for £5,000 soon after; going hurdling rather late but not without hope.
runners.comment: Second when last seen on Flat in October; going hurdling rather late but not without hope

runners.name: Scopic
runners.spotlight: Weak in the market and tailed off in Sedgefield bumper (25-1) last month; difficult to recommend.
runners.comment: Weak in market and tailed off in Sedgefield bumper last month; difficult to recommend

runners.name: Terrano
runners.spotlight: Third in Irish point (good/yielding) last April and that form looks solid, with the runner-up easily landing a bumper next time and the fourth winning a point; has joined an excellent yard and commands respect on rules debut.
runners.comment: Third in point looks solid form; has joined fine yard; commands respect on rules debut

runners.name: Autumn Getaway
runners.spotlight: Well held in bumpers seven months apart; related to winning jumpers but may require time and more of a test of stamina.
runners.comment: Well held in bumpers; related to winning jumpers but may require time and more of a test
runners.owner: Mrs K Elliott & Mrs E J Quinlan

runners.name: Karthala
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat maiden; no obvious promise in two runs over hurdles; others preferred.
runners.comment: Modest Flat maiden; no obvious promise in two runs over hurdles; others preferred

runners.name: Just Keeps Rolling
runners.spotlight: 1-10 in Irish points; pulled up both starts in maiden hurdles; opposable.
runners.comment: 1-10 in Irish points; pulled up both starts in maiden hurdles; opposable

races.race_name: Max And Naomi Are Getting Married Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Sonigino
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler with 6-22 strike-rate; beat sole other finisher by 16l on chasing debut at Fakenham (2m5f, good) four weeks ago; that form is not easy to read but he jumped soundly and should go well again.
runners.comment: Useful hurdler; beat sole other finisher 16l on chase debut; should go well again
runners.owner: Sir A Ferguson, G Mason, L Hales, The Late J Hales

runners.name: Moonshine Man
runners.spotlight: Already placed three times over fences, third of ten at Kempton (3m, good to soft) when last seen in February; faces a stiffer assignment here from 6lb out of the weights but his experience should stand him in good stead and he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: Third at Kempton in February; 6lb out of the weights but has plenty of chasing experience

runners.name: Kingston Narcissus
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles in a first-time tongue-strap (retained since) at Sedgefield (2m4f, good to soft) in December; however, two below-par runs have followed and he now goes over fences; others may have more potential.
runners.comment: Two below-par runs have followed Sedgefield hurdles win; others may have more potential

runners.name: Crackerjacque
runners.spotlight: Has had less racing than his rivals and his record is 3-7; gained second win over hurdles at Sandown (2m, heavy) in January and ran just as well when second at Fontwell next time; bred for chasing; may encounter quicker ground than he's used to, but otherwise he has plenty in his favour.
runners.comment: 3-7 over hurdles; bred for chasing and may not be fully exposed

runners.name: Rubber Ball
runners.spotlight: Not at his best in competitive handicap hurdles at Sandown in March and Aintree in April, but he'd previously won well at Newbury (2m, good to soft; probably stays 2m4f); began his career in points (1-1) and has a jumps pedigree so there's a reasonable chance he'll make a chaser; respected in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Best hurdles effort when winning well at Newbury in February; should make a chaser

races.race_name: mybettingsites.com Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Lawrenny
runners.spotlight: Won a Wetherby novice hurdle (2m) and a C&D handicap, both on good to soft, two seasons ago; ordinary efforts on both outings last term (2m3f/2m4f) but drops back in trip with headgear tried; quite interesting.
runners.comment: C&D win in 2024; modest runs over further last term; may fare better at 2m with headgear

runners.name: Double Digits
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner whose latest success was a handicap chase at Sedgefield in February (2m, good to soft); better handicapped over hurdles; over 8l behind Filibustering at Newcastle last time but meets the winner on 7lb better terms.
runners.comment: Last win was a chase, but better treated over hurdles; closely matched with Filibustering

runners.name: Filibustering
runners.spotlight: Back to form in first-time tongue-tie when winning at Newcastle last time (2m1f, good; third has since won on the Flat); up 4lb; chance.
runners.comment: Back to form to win on good ground at Newcastle last time and chance off 4lb higher mark

runners.name: Winds Of Winter
runners.spotlight: Won a Cartmel seller and two handicap hurdles (one over this C&D) last summer; may have needed the run when pulled up at Perth last week; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Three wins last summer and may have needed the run when pulled up at Perth last week

runners.name: Marhaba Prince
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner (2m, soft) who has run well all three starts over hurdles, last time getting off the mark in a maiden at Chepstow (2m, soft) when beating a subsequent dual winner who is now rated 16lb higher; interesting on handicap debut although unproven on this better ground.
runners.comment: Won a maiden last time (soft; form sound); chance on h'cap debut; first run on good

runners.name: Banderas
runners.spotlight: Won a Southwell maiden hurdle (2m4f, good; first-time tongue-tie) and a Carlisle Flat handicap (2m1f, soft) last summer; off since September; likely to be better for the run.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle/Flat h'cap winner last summer; off for 218 days; may improve for the run

runners.name: Perseus Way
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles in 2022-23; has changed stables three times since and perhaps best watched after a break on debut for latest new trainer, although is well handicapped now.
runners.comment: Two hurdle wins in 2022-23; three stable changes since; perhaps best watched on yard debut
runners.owner: J Monkhouse R De Robeck A Miller

races.race_name: Elizabeth Elliott Birthday Celebration Handicap Chase

runners.name: Divas Doyen
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D off this mark in March last year (good to soft); plenty of decent efforts since, including when third at Kelso last time (2m5f, good); each-way chance.
runners.comment: C&D winner in March 2025; good third at Kelso last time and has an each-way chance

runners.name: Royal Deeside
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in May and August (good) last year; has been in good form both over fences and on the Flat (second over 2m5f at Pontefract 11 days ago) on last four starts; conditions suit and he's a contender.
runners.comment: C&D winer in August; in decent form over fences and on the Flat last four starts; chance

runners.name: Cave Hill
runners.spotlight: Returned to form to win this race off a 3lb higher mark last year; has been rather disappointing on his last couple of starts and more is needed for a repeat win.
runners.comment: Won this race off 3lb higher last year; well beaten last few starts and needs to improve

runners.name: Play By The Rules
runners.spotlight: Ex-Nigel Hawke; fair fifth after a long break on stable debut at Newcastle last time (2m4f, good to soft; 3-1); should come on for that; one with a chance.
runners.comment: Fair fifth after long break on stable debut last time; improvement likely; contender

runners.name: Uncle Al
runners.spotlight: Has been a bit disappointing since winning a novice handicap chase off this mark in first-time cheekpieces at Sedgefield in December (2m5f, soft) and has a bit to prove at present.
runners.comment: Disappointing since Sedgefield win in December and has a bit to prove at present

runners.name: Maggies Boy
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdler; got off the mark when overcoming some trouble in running to make a winning chase debut at Sedgefield last time (2m5f, good); up 4lb; should go well again.
runners.comment: Off the mark on chase debut at Sedgefield last time; up 4lb; should go well again

runners.name: Jack Doyen
runners.spotlight: Has come right back to form on last two starts, winning at Hereford (2m5f, good) and Newton Abbot (2m5f, good to soft); has gone up 9lb but should go well again.
runners.comment: Has won at Hereford and Newton Abbot on last two starts; up 9lb but should go well again

runners.name: Kingofthegame
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden; two fair efforts since returning from a 395-day break, notably when fourth over 3m at Carlisle last time (good; second-time cheekpieces); visor now tried and he's a possible.
runners.comment: 0-9; two fair runs since 395-day break, notably last time; new headgear; a possible

runners.name: Mademewhatiam
runners.spotlight: !6-race maiden; two fair runs at Wetherby in December (2m3f, soft); well beaten since (tried over hurdles last time); no easy task from 5lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Two fair runs at Wetherby in December, but well beaten since; 5lb out of the handicap

runners.name: Yapper Hill
runners.spotlight: Best form over hurdles when fourth in a 2m novice handicap at Wetherby (soft) in February; needs to improve for the switch to fences.
runners.comment: Best hurdles form when fourth at Wetherby in February; more needed on chase debut

runners.name: Scairp Dubh
runners.spotlight: Won a novice handicap hurdle at Carlisle in February (2m3f, soft); creditable second over 3m at Doncaster next time but was disappointing with cheekpieces tried at Musselburgh last time (3m, good to soft; reportedly never travelled); headgear retained for chase debut.
runners.comment: Novice handicap hurdle winner; modest run in headgear (retained) latest; chase debut

runners.name: Quickasican
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete on first two starts over fences, but shaped better when second of five at Newcastle last time (2m4f, good); could go well.
runners.comment: Completed the course for first time over fences when 2nd last time; could go well again

runners.name: Valtteri Its James
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner in November (3m, yielding); fair form over hurdles, including when fourth on handicap debut at Newcastle last time (2m4f, good); likely to improve again now going chasing.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; 4th in h'cap hurdle latest and improvement likely now going chasing

runners.name: Tell Me Again
runners.spotlight: Creditable third in an Irish point in October (3m, good to yielding; second has won twice since) but previous rules form only modest; worth a market check on stable debut.
runners.comment: Irish point third in October; rules form modest; worth a market check on stable debut

races.race_name: drainrat.co.uk Rat Entry Diagnosis Specialist Handicap Chase

runners.name: Halfway House Lad
runners.spotlight: Three chase wins for Maurice Barnes including over C&D in March 2025 (good to soft); well beaten last three starts; 5lb out of the handicap for stable debut.
runners.comment: Three chase wins (one over C&D) for Maurice Barnes; well beaten in 2026; 5lb wrong

runners.name: Killybegs Jet Lady
runners.spotlight: Point winner; yet to win under rules, best of her three runs over fences when second to an in-form rival over C&D last August (good; Ribeye back in third); chance on that form but below par both runs since.
runners.comment: Point winner; best of three chase runs was C&D second last August; below par since

runners.name: Kicksaftersix
runners.spotlight: Three wins over C&D on good ground last season, including in this race, his mark going up 14lb as a result; won over 1m7f off his new mark here last time; up another 4lb but should again be competitive.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins, including this race, last year; won over 1m7f here last time; contender

runners.name: Passengerontheship
runners.spotlight: Maiden; fine second to a subsequent three-time winner in a handicap hurdle at Carlisle in April 2025 (3m1f, good); hasn't done as well since, although not disgraced when sixth on chase debut at Newcastle last time (2m4f, good to soft); tongue-tie now tried; chance back up in trip.
runners.comment: Maiden; fair run over 2m4f on chase debut; chance in first-time tongue-tie back at 3m

runners.name: Rob Roy Macgregor
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner on debut (2m1f, heavy); four fair runs with cheekpieces at up to 4m this year, all with give in the ground; worth considering.
runners.comment: Four fair chase runs with cheekpieces this year, including over 4m here; worth considering

runners.name: Ribeye
runners.spotlight: Two wins last season, the second off 3lb lower over C&D in October (good); hasn't been in quite such good form in 2026, but has had a break and can't be rule out.
runners.comment: Two wins last term, the second over C&D; not so good in 2026, but back after a break

runners.name: Dr Shirocco
runners.spotlight: Has won four chases, including over C&D in May 2024 (good); best recent form has been at Musselburgh and he was below par over C&D last time (good).
runners.comment: Four chase wins including over C&D in May 2024; below par here last time; bit to prove

runners.name: Cardamon Hill
runners.spotlight: Won twice in November 2024 (2m7f/3m, good/good to soft) but mainly disappointing since including on both runs since a 404-day break; plenty to prove at present.
runners.comment: Two wins in November 2024; below par after 404-day break last twice; plenty to prove

runners.name: West Lawn
runners.spotlight: Point/hurdle winner but 0-18 over fences; good second in a four-runner mares' handicap chase at Catterick in February (3m1f, soft) but pulled up last time.
runners.comment: Point/hurdle winner; 0-18 over fences; 2nd of four in February but pulled up last time

runners.name: Princesse Milania
runners.spotlight: Two wins over hurdles last October (first over 3m) and creditable runs since; quite interesting on her chase debut.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner in October and mainly creditable runs since; interesting on chase debut

runners.name: Rightbackatyou
runners.spotlight: Ran his best race for nearly two years when a close fourth over C&D last time (good) on his third start over fences; each-way chance on that form.
runners.comment: Ran his best race since 2024 when a close fourth over C&D last time; each-way chance

runners.name: Millford Hill
runners.spotlight: Point winner; well beaten in four runs over hurdles, including on handicap debut at Perth last time; now goes chasing with cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: Point winner; well beaten in 4 runs over hurdles; now goes chasing with cheekpieces tried

runners.name: Poetry Man
runners.spotlight: Won a 3m1f handicap chase at Catterick in December 2024 (good); some fair runs since, but plenty to prove having been pulled up on last two starts; tongue-tie now tried.
runners.comment: Catterick winner in 2024; some fair form since but pulled up last 2 runs; tongue-tie tried

races.race_name: Winchcombe Working Mens FC Tour 2026 Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: My Boy Christy
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; unexposed hurdler who put up a career best when runner-up in a 2m4f handicap here last time (good); this longer trip should suit; a possible.
runners.comment: Ex-Irsh; career best when second over 2m4f here last time; 3m should suit; could go well

runners.name: It's Maisy
runners.spotlight: Has won five of her 19 races over hurdles, the latest a four-runner mares' handicap for today's claimer at Newcastle in April (2m7f, good); up 4lb in a tougher race.
runners.comment: 5-19 over hurdles, latest win a four-runner mares' h'cap in April; stiffer task here

runners.name: Divilabother
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; won over 2m4f here on stable debut in August; mainly fair form since; interesting on first try at this trip (has run well over 2m6f).
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; 2m4f win here on stable debut; fair since; interesting on first run at 2m7f

runners.name: Robert d'Ores
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been over C&D, including in this race last season; shaped as though returning to form when sixth at Bangor last time; back on his last winning mark; one to consider.
runners.comment: All 3 wins have been over C&D, including this race in 2025; on last winning mark; chance

runners.name: Adveram
runners.spotlight: C&D winner on heavy ground in December; has pulled up on his three starts since; tongue-tie now tried; others stronger.
runners.comment: C&D winner on heavy in December; pulled up on three starts since; tongue-tie now tried

runners.name: Dillarchie
runners.spotlight: In fine form having won her last three races, one at Catterick (3m1f, good to soft) and the others over 3m3f on good at Sedgefield (Wee Alki second on both occasions); up 7lb for latest win, but a possible.
runners.comment: Has won her last three races, last twice at Sedgefield; stiffer task; could go well again

runners.name: East Eagle
runners.spotlight: Two wins on good ground last season, the latest 13 days ago when scoring by 9l at Stratford; stiffer task off this 8lb higher mark, but these conditions suit.
runners.comment: Two wins last term, latest by 9l at Stratford 13 days ago; 8lb higher; could go well
runners.owner: Mrs Janet Cooper & M E Sowersby

runners.name: Belladinotte
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in June 2025; gained a second success at Southwell last time (3m, good); conditions suit; should go well again despite 5lb rise.
runners.comment: C&D winner in June 2025; gained a second success at Southwell last time and should go well

runners.name: Knacker Trapper
runners.spotlight: Only win came in a Southwell novice handicap in May 2024 (2m4f, good); good run when second at Sedgefield last time (2m4f, good); stamina to prove, but could be in the mix.
runners.comment: Best on good ground and, following a good second last time, could be in the mix again

runners.name: Lord De Vinci
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden; tried chasing without success this year and, having been pulled up Carlisle (2m3f, good to soft) when returned to hurdling last time, is perhaps best watched for now.
runners.comment: 0-10; no improvement over fences this year and pulled up back hurdling last time

runners.name: Stylish Recruit
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of his reduced mark and some better ground to win off 3lb lower mark over 2m4f here last time (good); may fare even better up to this trip (3m Irish point winner).
runners.comment: Dropped to a good mark and won over 2m4f here last time; 2m7f may suit; interesting

runners.name: Victory Club
runners.spotlight: Ex-Dan Skelton; several good runs for Rebecca Menzies last season, including when third at Kelso on latest start in November (3m, soft); just as effective on this better ground; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Ex-Dan Skelton; several good runs for this yard last term; each-way chance
runners.owner: Miss E Emmerton & Major P H K Steveney

runners.name: Wee Alki
runners.spotlight: 1-16 over hurdles (sole win was over C&D in November); creditable second to Dillarchie over 3m3f at Sedgefield on last two starts; suited by good ground; each-way chance.
runners.comment: 1-16 (C&D winner in November); sound runs last twice; good going suits; each-way chance

runners.name: Dusky Days
runners.spotlight: Three sound efforts on last three starts last summer (good/soft), winning over 2m1f at Cartmel and finishing creditable second over 3m there on final start; off for 252 days and likely to be better for the run.
runners.comment: Three sound efforts on last three starts last summer; off 252 days and may need the run

runners.name: William Of York
runners.spotlight: Won three handicap chases on the trot in 2024; has struggled to finish since; may fare better back over hurdles with visor tried.
runners.comment: Hat-trick of chase wins in 2024; has struggled to finish since; visor tried back hurdling

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap

runners.name: Lady Hornblower
runners.spotlight: Started favourite when well held behind Indefensible on her comeback/turf debut recently; 2lb better off with that one and now goes beyond 7f for the first time (dam won twice over 1m on soft ground).
runners.comment: Favourite when well held behind Indefensible latest; goes beyond 7f for the first time

runners.name: Willowinghurn
runners.spotlight: Had plenty of racing last year; will need to settle better than when behind two of these at Beverley recently if she's to have a chance of staying now going back up in distance.
runners.comment: Had plenty of racing last year; behind two of these at Beverley recently; outsider

runners.name: Vidmiyr
runners.spotlight: Picked up from Johnny Murtagh for 48,000euros having run fairly well round Dundalk; solid start for his new yard when a clear second in muddling 7f Tapeta maiden on Monday and won't mind the longer trip on that evidence.
runners.comment: Clear second in a muddling 7f Tapeta maiden on Monday for his new yard; one to consider
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Indefensible
runners.spotlight: Solid turf/handicap debut effort on his recent comeback, having fluffed the start and been inclined to pull hard after seven months off; this longer trip should be within range on pedigree if he's more amenable.
runners.comment: Solid turf/handicap debut effort recently; longer trip should be within range if settling

runners.name: Thunderhoof
runners.spotlight: Has run to a similar level in all three starts this year, latterly upped to this trip on turf debut three weeks ago; needs more now handicapping but that's entirely possible.
runners.comment: Has run to a similar level in all three starts this year; needs more now handicapping

runners.name: Stay Salty
runners.spotlight: First two efforts well advertised since and he got no peace in front off this mark at Kempton last time (all Polytrack); will need to prove at least as good on turf, going back up in trip, after two months off.
runners.comment: Will need to prove at least as good on turf as AW, back up in trip, after two months off

runners.name: Horu Kanu
runners.spotlight: Gelded after three runs in 2025; hard to know the worth of his comeback Tapeta third to an easy, short-priced winner but he's entitled to have come on for that and shaped there as though this extra furlong would help.
runners.comment: Gelded after three runs last summer; entitled to have come on for his recent AW comeback

runners.name: Decem Starr
runners.spotlight: Stamina on dam's side of pedigree and he shaped as though this longer trip would suit on his comeback over an easy 7f early last month; first-time cheekpieces will need to bring out a bit more on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: First-time cheekpieces will need to bring out a bit more on his handicap debut

runners.name: Reliable Ricki
runners.spotlight: Chelmsford third last August (first run for this yard, among the winners of late) worked out well and he improved on that when off the mark on Wolverhampton AW (7f) in September; looks to start handicap career on a feasible mark and he goes on the shortlist.
runners.comment: 7f AW winner in September; improvement left; looks to go handicapping on a feasible mark
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Noelan Star
runners.spotlight: Starspangledbanner gelding who has looked a good prospect in landing a pair of 7f/1m novices on Newcastle AW, easily landing the odds on latter occasion 19 days ago; saddled with a double penalty but still the one to beat on his turf debut.
runners.comment: Impressively landed 7f/1m novices at Newcastle; saddles double penalty but the one to beat

runners.name: My Ballyquinn
runners.spotlight: Gelded and much improved from his debut when a promising second of seven in 1m novice at Thirsk 22 days ago; looks to have more to offer; a player.
runners.comment: Promising Thirsk second 22 days ago; looks to have more to offer; a player

runners.name: Auradue
runners.spotlight: £1,000 yearling; Due Diligence filly; dam 7f AW 2yo winner (RPR 71); makes a belated debut and others appeal more on this occasion.
runners.comment: Due Diligence filly; makes her belated debut and others appeal more on this occasion

runners.name: Leonardo Blu
runners.spotlight: 50,000gns yearling; Kameko half-brother to three winners, including Sirius Prospect (6f-1m Listed; RPR 115) and Bama Breeze (US 8.5f-10.5f; RPR 107); dam French/Canadian 7f-8.5f winner (Group 3; RPR 101); no forlorn hope on debut.
runners.comment: 50,000gns yearling; siblings include a Listed winner; no forlorn hope on his debut
runners.owner: J Collins, P Moorhead, C & J Cooley

runners.name: Mountain Cat
runners.spotlight: Teofilo colt; dam 7f-8.5f winner (German Listed; RPR 101); newcomer needs considering, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: Teofilo newcomer who needs considering, especially if the market vibes are positive.

runners.name: Beau Chevalier
runners.spotlight: Little promise shown in 7f Ascot novice last July and Southwell maiden on his return nine days ago.
runners.comment: Little promise shown in Ascot novice last July and Southwell maiden on his return

races.race_name: Ings Environmental Susan Duker Memorial EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Rocket Boots
runners.spotlight: Trainer had a 2yo winner this spring (second time out); easy to back, finished well held having made much of the running in a small-field 5f Newmarket novice recently; needs to improve a deal on that.
runners.comment: Easy to back, finished well held in a small-field 5f Newmarket novice recently

runners.name: Mohaymenah
runners.spotlight: First foal of Kidwah, who won on her C&D 2yo debut for William Haggas; the market will show what's expected of this February foal, who holds no notable entries.
runners.comment: First foal of Kidwah, who won on her C&D 2yo debut for William Haggas; market useful

runners.name: Crown Of Ivy
runners.spotlight: 60,000euros foal, 50,000euros yearling; half-brother to 6f 2yo winner King Of Bears; yard has a good record with juveniles down the years and he needs a market check.
runners.comment: 60,000euros foal, 50,000euros yearling; yard has good record with juveniles down the years

runners.name: Lion O
runners.spotlight: Trainer had a 2yo newcomer win for the same connections early last month; this April-foaled 150,000gns yearling, who holds a sales race entry in the autumn, is a half-brother to six winners (one as a 2yo); likely type.
runners.comment: 150,000gns yearling; holds a sales race entry in the autumn; half-brother to six winners

runners.name: Notable Dream
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns yearling who's closely related to a French middle-distance winner; holds a sales race entry later on but only turned two this week and may want more time.
runners.comment: Holds a sales race entry later on but only turned two this week and may want more time

runners.name: Note To Self
runners.spotlight: Powerful yard in top form lately and this February-foaled son of well-related 7f Polytrack winner Dubrava is their first 2yo runner of the season; looks a likely type on paper.
runners.comment: Powerful yard in top form lately; their first 2yo runner of the season; interesting

runners.name: Taseem
runners.spotlight: 80-1 outsider of six, ran as the market suggested he would when finishing a remote last at Yarmouth 11 days ago; can't be backed on that.
runners.comment: 80-1 outsider of six, ran as the market suggested he would on debut

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap

runners.name: Territorial Knight
runners.spotlight: Yard going well and won this in 2023 and 2024; solid comeback behind Akkadian Thunder over C&D five weeks ago, when picked off late by four who'd sat in the rear, and is 3lb better off with that one; player.
runners.comment: Solid comeback behind Akkadian Thunder over C&D; fair chance of turning things round

runners.name: Rose Of Honour
runners.spotlight: Impressive on her Kempton AW debut last September and easily defied a penalty from the front on Tapeta (both 6f) a fortnight later; dam won a French Group 3 over this trip and she's the only one open to improvement back from seven months off; market informative.
runners.comment: Unbeaten; the only one open to improvement back from seven months off; market informative

runners.name: Papa Cocktail
runners.spotlight: C&D win last summer came off 18lb lower and he returns to turf 10lb higher than when last running on grass, thanks to a consistent spell on Tapeta; will need something close to a career-best to take this and he's not improving at his age.
runners.comment: Returns to turf 10lb higher than when last running on it; not improving at his age

runners.name: Rousing Encore
runners.spotlight: Not much room for manoeuvre off this mark but has resumed in good form, latterly when inclined to pull hard at Newmarket (6f) in a race not run to suit; usually ridden patiently these days but has made all more than once in the past; a lot may hinge on how things unfold early.
runners.comment: Resumed in good form; can pull hard and a lot may hinge on how things unfold early

runners.name: Brummell
runners.spotlight: Often inclined to pull hard over this trip in the past and was kept to 5f last year, when winning three times; undergone wind surgery since last seen six months ago and will need to settle in tonight's small field if he's to have a chance off this mark.
runners.comment: Kept to 5f last year, when winning three times; undergone wind surgery; needs to settle

runners.name: Akkadian Thunder
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner (both 7f, good) who turned in a solid effort when second to a progressive rival on his C&D comeback (nudged up 2lb) in March; happiest coming from off a strong pace and the question is whether this will be run to suit on good to firm ground.
runners.comment: Second to a progressive rival on his C&D comeback (nudged up 2lb) in March; player

races.race_name: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Mafting
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of a drop in class and a career-low mark when making it 2-3 at Redcar on his recent comeback; tonight's extra distance and similarly long home straight won't hurt and he holds obvious claims off 4lb higher.
runners.comment: Winning comeback at Redcar and he holds obvious claims off 4lb higher

runners.name: Sixpack
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent ex-Irish gelding who's never run on ground faster than good; didn't take to hurdles in one try last autumn but solid comeback off this mark three weeks ago and this does seem to be his time of year; no surprise to see him take this.
runners.comment: Inconsistent ex-Irish gelding but solid comeback off this mark three weeks ago; player

runners.name: Saratoga Gold
runners.spotlight: Serial winner in his younger days but been dry in almost three years; does have a comeback spring victory to his name but all other wins came in the second half of the year and he may need this at his age back from five months off.
runners.comment: Most wins in second half of the year; may need this at his age after five months off

runners.name: Tazaman
runners.spotlight: Two wins last summer came at a lesser level, latterly over 2m1f off 3lb lower, and he wants a good test at this trip; doesn't boast the greatest reappearance record down the years and, like stablemate Saratoga Gold, may need this after seven months off.
runners.comment: Two wins last summer came at a lesser level, latterly over 2m1f; returns from a layoff

runners.name: Pleasant Man
runners.spotlight: Hasn't run on turf in getting on for two years and has had plenty of chances off a sliding mark on the AW lately; said to have hung right when again a beaten favourite last time and that leaves him with a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Not run on turf in almost two years; plenty of chances off a sliding mark on the AW lately

runners.name: Dreams Fled Away
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent hurdler who's been off for five months since a poor effort here in the winter; nothing to suggest he's best caught fresh and it's tough to find any positives.
runners.comment: Inconsistent hurdler; nothing to suggest he's best caught fresh; tough to find positives

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Trucial Pearl
runners.spotlight: In good form on the AW before acquitting herself well from a wide draw when sixth of 14 in 6f Pontefract handicap last month; eased 1lb so she's a player in her bid for a breakthrough success.
runners.comment: Did well from wide draw when sixth at Pontefract latest; very much one to consider here

runners.name: Too Darn Spicy
runners.spotlight: Shaped encouragingly when a debut third in 1m Newcastle novice in March but has twice failed to build on it, last of eight back there 19 days ago; needs this switch to handicaps to spark major progress.
runners.comment: Last of eight at Newcastle 19 days ago; needs switch to handicaps to spark major progress

runners.name: Thornaby Annie
runners.spotlight: Made the frame three times over 6f last season but she came in last of 13 in a 6f Newcastle handicap on her return so has something to prove in a first-time hood.
runners.comment: Last of 13 on return at Newcastle; has something to prove in a first-time hood now

runners.name: Forever Twenty
runners.spotlight: Only modest form shown thus far, finishing seventh of 13 on her handicap debut at Newcastle (6f) 19 days ago; tongue tied for her turf debut with more required.
runners.comment: Seventh on h'cap debut at Newcastle; tongue tied for turf debut with more required

runners.name: Tekitoff
runners.spotlight: Has made a positive start for his new yard, coming home strongly on his handicap debut when a close third of seven at Southwell (7f) last month; merits serious consideration off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Close third at Southwell (7f) last month; merits serious consideration off unchanged mark

runners.name: Go Teejay
runners.spotlight: Little promise shown in his three outings thus far, beating one in 6f novice at Pontefract recently; cheekpieces on for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Little promise shown in three outings thus far; cheekpieces on for his handicap debut

runners.name: Grey Force
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward when fourth of ten in 7f Chelmsford novice in February; that form has worked out well so he's not out of things on his handicap debut after a break.
runners.comment: Good Chelmsford fourth in February; not out of things on his handicap debut after a break

runners.name: Caitlin G
runners.spotlight: Beat just one rival in a trio of runs last summer for Michael Keady, last of nine in 5f Wolverhampton maiden ten months ago; tongue tied now; has lots to prove for her new yard.
runners.comment: Beat one rival in a trio of runs last summer for Michael Keady; lots to prove for new yard

runners.name: Vega's Virtue
runners.spotlight: Cut little ice in a trio of novices last autumn, only eighth of ten at Carlisle (6f) on final one, but she could still do better now venturing into handicaps.
runners.comment: Cut little ice in three novices last autumn but could do better now handicapping

races.race_name: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap

runners.name: Maxi Boy
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 in good form on the AW but this veteran failed to fire in a pair of 6f Newcastle handicaps at the start of the year; needs to bounce back after a break.
runners.comment: Failed to fire in two 6f Newcastle handicaps at start of year; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Evocative Spark
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has been back in fine form of late, landing a pair of 7f handicaps at Catterick; up another 3lb but he's not taken lightly in his current mood.
runners.comment: Landed pair of 7f handicaps at Catterick; up 3lb but not taken lightly in current mood

runners.name: Zubaru
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who shaped well on his return from eight months off when third of 11 in 7f AW Lingfield handicap 18 days ago; holds very good claims off an unchanged mark for yard among the winners.
runners.comment: Shaped well on return from eight months off with Lingfield third; holds very good claims

runners.name: Straight A
runners.spotlight: On a losing sequence but he arrives in decent nick, finishing fourth of six in 7f Kempton handicap 33 days ago; possibilities.
runners.comment: On a losing sequence but he arrives in decent nick; possibilities

runners.name: Lumenbourg
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D last July and in good form after until finishing only eighth in 7f Redcar handicap on his final run; this reliable sort can't be discounted despite a six-month absence.
runners.comment: C&D winner in July; largely in good form after so not discounted after six-month absence
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd & Julie Camacho

runners.name: Magic Music
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who failed to beat a rival in 6f Leicester handicap on her reappearance last month; hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Lightly raced but failed to beat a rival in 6f Leicester h'cap on reappearance last month

runners.name: Oscar's Sister
runners.spotlight: Without a win since 2022 but she has gone well fresh so can't be totally dismissed on her seasonal return; yard also runs Lumenbourg.
runners.comment: Without a win since 2022; has gone well fresh so can't be totally dismissed on return

runners.name: Mr Cool
runners.spotlight: A dual 6f scorer in first half of 2025 who was back on track when runner-up in 7f Kempton handicap in March; needs to back it up now returned to turf.
runners.comment: Back on track when runner-up in 7f Kempton handicap; needs to back it up on return to turf

runners.name: Valentine Catcher
runners.spotlight: It's now 13 starts since his last success but he was back on track when finishing second in 6f Pontefract handicap 11 days ago; well in the mix eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Back on track when second in 6f Pontefract handicap last week; well in the mix eased 1lb

runners.name: Chifa
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f Wolverhampton winner over the winter; not discredited, given he was set a lot to do, when sixth of 11 in 7f Lingfield handicap 18 days ago; considered from an easing mark.
runners.comment: Set a lot to do when solid sixth at Lingfield 18 days ago; considered from an easing mark

races.race_name: Edgewood Stakes Presented By Accenture (Grade 2) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Lorelei Lee
runners.spotlight: Has made a pleasing start to her career with two wins from three starts and related to Grade 2 winner Somelikeithotbrown; scored narrowly in a Listed race, showing early speed to lead from a wide draw upped to this trip at Turfway Park six weeks ago; deserves respect despite being up in class.
runners.comment: This represents a step up in class but should prove competitive.

runners.name: Taken By The Wind
runners.spotlight: Finished third, beaten 15l, in an allowance Grade 2 at Oaklawn Park 35 days ago; winner of three of her five career runs; has won in classy company before and no surprise to see her go well.
runners.comment: Has won in classy company before and has a decent shout.
runners.owner: Magdalena Racing, Terry Bradshaw Et Al

runners.name: Time To Dream
runners.spotlight: Couldn't quite get the job done when 11-5 runner-up (Tampa Bay Downs) behind Dandona two months ago in a Grade 3.
runners.comment: Minor honours two months ago; wins-runs record of 2-5.

runners.name: Lovely Grey
runners.spotlight: Went close when second in a Listed race at Turfway Park behind Lorelei Lee six weeks ago; also engaged in race 13 at Churchill Downs.
runners.comment: Not beaten far six weeks ago; wins-runs record of 1-10.

runners.name: Dandona
runners.spotlight: Winner of two of her three career starts; got the job done by just over 1l two months ago at Tampa Bay Downs in a Grade 3; the trainer continues to be among winners recently.
runners.comment: Just over 1l winner two months ago; winner of two of her three career runs.

runners.name: Imaginationthelady
runners.spotlight: Went down by a head in a Grade 2 (Keeneland) behind Storm's Wake four weeks ago; winner of two of her four career starts and won a Grade 2 in October; should go well.
runners.comment: Can be in the thick of things; beaten narrowly last month.

runners.name: Tam Tam
runners.spotlight: Landed a narrow success in a Listed race at Gulfstream Park 34 days ago; aiming to complete a hat-trick and improved form the last twice; tried over an additional 1f now.
runners.comment: Scored narrowly five weeks ago; out to complete a hat-trick.

runners.name: Connect The Stars
runners.spotlight: Cracking effort in defeat over 7 1/2f at Gulfstream Park (Listed race) behind Tam Tam 34 days ago; up in distance by an extra 1f here.
runners.comment: Just beaten 34 days ago; tried over an additional 1f now.

runners.name: To A Flame
runners.spotlight: This daughter of Justify looks the part on breeding and two from four in her career so far; safely held at Keeneland last month behind Storm's Wake when not beaten that far in a Grade 2.
runners.comment: Finished seventh last month; has made a pleasing start to her career with two wins from four starts.

runners.name: Storm's Wake
runners.spotlight: On target three times in her last seven outings; a course winner; shock 22-1 winner (Keeneland) last month overcoming four of these rivals in a Grade 2.
runners.comment: Beat a bunch of these last month; on target three times in her last seven outings.

runners.name: Just Aloof
runners.spotlight: Looks the part on breeding (by Justify) and has made a pleasing start to her career with two wins from three starts; fair effort when half a length fourth to Storm's Wake last month at Keeneland in a Grade 2.
runners.comment: Not beaten far last month; winner of two of her three career starts.

runners.name: Lion Lake
runners.spotlight: A neck behind the winner, Storm's Wake, over 1m at Keeneland (Grade 2) last month when not beaten far; managed two wins in her last three outings; showing consistent form.
runners.comment: Beaten narrowly 27 days ago; has chalked up two wins from her last three.

runners.name: Bohemian
runners.spotlight: Fair form when just over 3l third in a black type, finishing well behind reopposing Indigo Woods at Fair Grounds two months ago; a previous winner here; on target twice in her last four outings.
runners.comment: Fair level of form two months ago; has chalked up two wins from her last four.

runners.name: Indigo Woods
runners.spotlight: Landed a narrow success in a black type at Fair Grounds two months ago; bids to pull off a hat-trick and unbeaten after two career runs; not discounted despite having more on her plate.
runners.comment: This represents a step up in class but could be in the mix.

races.race_name: Longines Kentucky Oaks (Grade 1) (Fillies) (Dirt)

runners.name: Prom Queen
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner at Gulfstream Park of maiden in February (by 8l) and Grade 2 Gulfstream Park Oaks (8.5f; beat My Miss Mo by about 3l in steadily run race); travelled strongly, made eye-catching move on turn and not at all hard ridden to assert in latter race, scoring with lots in hand while still a bit green; should be suited by 1m1f; much stiffer test and must adapt to big field, but open to considerable improvement and an exciting prospect.
runners.comment: Big move on turn to win 8.5f Grade 2 at Gulfstream on only third start; exciting prospect

runners.name: Always A Runner
runners.spotlight: Most promising winner of both starts in a maiden at Tampa Bay in February (by just over 6l) and Grade 3 Gazelle Stakes (1m1f) at Aqueduct in April; always going well but had a bit to do on latter occasion, going on really strongly to beat Pashmina by just over 1l, well on top at finish; stays well (will likely have to come from some way back) and is very good prospect.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in two and stayed on strongly to win Grade 3 at Aqueduct (1m1f) by 1l; promising
runners.owner: Douglas Scharbauer & Three Chimneys Farm LLC

runners.name: Counting Stars
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner of four races, notably Grade 2 Fantasy Stakes (well placed, beat Search Party by 5l) at Oaklawn Park last time out; possibly unlucky when just under 1l second to Explora in Grade 3 Honeybee there on penultimate start, plenty to do off moderate fractions; strong claims on first start at 1m1f.
runners.comment: Easy Grade 2 winner latest; looked unlucky when second to Explora time before; good chance

runners.name: Percy's Bar
runners.spotlight: Demoted to second in Grade 1 last year, when also third in Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies despite being hampered; quickened through inside gap, got first run, when beating Zany by about 2l in Grade 1 Ashland at Keeneland (8.5f) on reappearance; others may improve more at 1m1f but she possesses impressive tactical speed.
runners.comment: Breeders' Cup third; got first run, beat Zany 2l in 8.5f Grade 1 at Keeneland; good chance

runners.name: Explora
runners.spotlight: High-class juvenile, easy winner of Grade 2 Oak Leaf at Santa Anita and good second in Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies at Del Mar; winner of two of three starts this year, on latest start beating Counting Stars by just under 1l in Grade 3 Honeybee at Oaklawn; should stay 1m1f and is a tractable, focused runner, but has a two-month layoff (missed intended prep) to overcome.
runners.comment: Grade 2 win in 2025 and beat Counting Stars in Grade 3 at Oaklawn; player but 2 months off
runners.owner: Michael E Pegram, Karl Watson & Paul Weitman

runners.name: Meaning
runners.spotlight: Winner of three of four starts, notably Grade 2 Santa Anita Oaks by 2l from Brooklyn Blonde, tracking pace and asserting steadily in straight; only defeat when 4l fourth to Super Corredora in Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies at Del Mar, where behind Explora and Percy's Bar; solid place claims.
runners.comment: Behind Explora and Percy's Bar when fourth at Breeders' Cup but Grade 2 win latest; claims

runners.name: Pashmina
runners.spotlight: In frame in black-type races three times after winning a maiden at Fair Grounds by 10l; very good second, beaten only around 1l and well clear or rest, behind Always A Runner in Grade 3 Gazelle at Aqueduct (1m1f) last time, when she looked to be getting away from eventual winner but hopped tracks in straight; smart filly, bit more exposed than some, but an interesting outsider.
runners.comment: In frame in black-type races and Grade 3 (improved form); smart but bit exposed

runners.name: Resist
runners.spotlight: Reserve who now gets in; progressive form at lower level; best effort when about 2l third (Lovely Grey second) to Lorelei Lee in Listed race at Turfway Park last time; very stiff task.
runners.comment: Very stiff task based on third in Listed race at Turfway Park on latest start

runners.name: Zany
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner of both starts last year in maiden at Gulfstream Park and Grade 2 Demoiselle at Aqueduct in December; landed the odds in Listed race at Tampa in February; unsuited by slow pace, caught wide and stayed on well late when about 2l second to Percy's Bar in Grade 1 Ashland at Keeneland last month (again edged left in straight); will relish first run beyond 8.5f; serious claims.
runners.comment: Grade 2 winner in 2025 caught eye when second to Percy's Bar at Keeneland; serious claims

runners.name: Search Party
runners.spotlight: Useful form at Oaklawn to win maiden (by 6l) last December and Listed race (by neck) in February; in frame there since, unable to get on terms, when fourth to Explora in Grade 3 Honeybee and second to Counting Stars in Grade 2 Fantasy; will be suited by further than 8.5f but something to find.
runners.comment: Listed winner but never on terms when in frame in Graded company at Oaklawn; needs more

runners.name: Brooklyn Blonde
runners.spotlight: First race outside maiden company (winner at Santa Anita in March) when 2l second to Meaning in 8.5f Grade 2 Santa Anita Oaks last month, making an effort to challenge in straight but brushed aside; should stay 1m1f but needs to find a good bit more.
runners.comment: Good 2l second to Meaning in 8.5f Grade 2 at Santa Anita, brushed aside; needs to improve

runners.name: Lovely Grey
runners.spotlight: Reserve who now gets in; beaten on all five starts outside maidens and yet to tackle a Graded race; very stiff task.
runners.comment: Beaten all five starts outside maidens and yet to run in a Graded race; very stiff task

runners.name: Nycon
runners.spotlight: Reserve who now gets in; runner-up in black-type race and Listed event this year but well held when fourth behind Always A Runner in Grade 3 Gazelle at Aqueduct; lots to find.
runners.comment: Well held when 10l fourth to Always A Runner in Grade 3 Gazelle at Aqueduct; lots to find

runners.name: Dazzling Dame
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has won four races, notably black-type race at Colonial Downs in March; well held on her only go in better company when beaten 10l in Grade 3 at Churchill Downs last September; would be a shock winner against this quality of opponent.
runners.comment: Four lesser wins, notably black-type race; well held in a Grade 3 race; outsider
runners.owner: Respect The Valleys LLC, Medallion Racing & Madaket Stables

races.race_name: Derby City Distaff Stakes Presented By Ford (Grade 1) (Fillies & Mares) (Dirt)

runners.name: Splendora
runners.spotlight: High-class performer at 7f/1m, winner of five consecutive races, including Grade 1 victories in Breeders' Cup Filly & Mare Sprint (Del Mar in November, by 5l) and Beholder Mile (Santa Anita in March, by nearly 6l while coasting home); has tons of pace but happy to accept a lead; hard to beat.
runners.comment: Five straight wins include easy Grade 1 scores at Del Mar and Santa Anita; hard to beat

runners.name: Haulin Ice
runners.spotlight: Superbly placed by trainer Saffie Joseph to win 12 of 21 career starts; five victories in 2025 included Grade 3s at Aqueduct (6.5f Vagrancy) in May and Gulfstream (7f Princess Rooney) in September; has picked up black-type event and Listed race at Oaklawn this year; speedy but sees out 7f.
