runners.spotlight: Failed to build on a decent fourth at Lingfield (1m2f) in February when behind Blue Rc at Newcastle (1m) on Good Friday but the fact he made all in this race off 8lb lower last year means a revival can't be discounted, particularly as he's well drawn to attack again; tongue-tied first time.
runners.comment: 8lb lower than when winning this last year; revival isn't ruled out in new tongue-tie
runners.owner: Fine Clarets Racing, Tp Watson & C Wood

runners.name: City Of Poets
runners.spotlight: Has looked quirky on occasions but he produced a decidedly useful effort when coming with a strong run to get up late in 8.6f Wolverhampton handicap in November; matched that form when a close third there on reappearance six weeks ago; won a fast-ground novice on turf last year (also second here on debut); one of two solid contenders for James Tate.
runners.comment: Has odd quirk but done well on AW in recent starts; one of two solid contenders for yard

runners.name: Esherann
runners.spotlight: Pair of 1m wins on good ground in Ireland last summer, namely a Naas maiden (wore cheekpieces) and Curragh handicap (visored); low-mileage new recruit for Declan Carroll but he'll need to leave last month's Newmarket reappearance with a tongue-tie added (retained) well behind to play a leading role here.
runners.comment: Two wins in Ireland last year but well held on recent first run for Declan Carroll

runners.name: Apiarist
runners.spotlight: Three fine efforts on AW to start the year but his run of good form came to a halt when well behind Blue Rc at Newcastle over Easter; acts on turf but has a much better strike-rate on AW; drawn widest.
runners.comment: Started year in good form on AW but lesser run latest; others preferred back on turf

runners.name: A War Eagle
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who was having only his second start for this yard when clinging on over the extended 7f at Beverley (good to firm) nine days ago; only raised 3lb but this is a step up from that Class 4 and his draw isn't ideal for one who usually races close to the pace.
runners.comment: Only nudged up 3lb for recently Beverley success but this is a much stronger race

runners.name: Mirsky
runners.spotlight: Hit a hot spell at the beginning of last summer, winning over C&D (good to firm) before following up at the Epsom Derby meeting (8.5f, good to soft); creditable third (Sea Force sixth) of 12 on Musselburgh reappearance (1m1f, good) three weeks ago; likely to be competitive again.
runners.comment: C&D win last May; creditable third of 12 on reappearance; likely to be in the mix

runners.name: Bellarchi
runners.spotlight: Last four wins at Musselburgh, including two last month, latterly producing a career-best performance to see off ten rivals over 1m on good; also went close at this course last year; a further 4lb rise demands even more but can't rule out in her current mood.
runners.comment: In the form of life with two Musselburgh wins last month; can't discount in current mood

runners.name: Dashing Darcey
runners.spotlight: Just second outing for Geoff Harker when runner-up off 1lb higher at Haydock (1m, good) last May but never involved when dropped to 7f at York the following month and not seen since (gelded); this is a tough race to return in.
runners.comment: Second off 1lb higher last May but off since a lesser run the following month; gelded
runners.owner: C H McGie & Northumbria Leisure Ltd

runners.name: Accentuate
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a Windsor novice (1m, good) last June; far from disgraced despite only beating one home when fifth on his handicap debut at Ascot (good to firm) last July and perhaps unsuited by soft ground when well held there in September; sold for 23,000gns in November and gelded ahead of this return for a new stable; drawn wide and probably best watched this time.
runners.comment: Lightly raced new recruit for Tim Easterby but drawn wide and perhaps best watched

runners.name: Redarna
runners.spotlight: Sprung a 66-1 surprise in this on good to firm in 2022 (also fourth in 2024); added career win number 17, as an 11yo, at Ayr (1m, good to soft) last September but arrives with something to prove after finishing tailed off behind a couple of these on his Musselburgh reappearance.
runners.comment: Won this in 2022 (also fourth in 2024) but recent reappearance was poor

runners.name: Rhoscolyn
runners.spotlight: Last year's Golden Mile win at Goodwood (strong record there) shows he's still smart on his day; respectable return to action when fifth in good conditions race over 7f here three weeks ago but he's gained the vast majority of his ten career wins on slower ground than he's likely to encounter this afternoon.
runners.comment: Golden Mile winner last year; fair reappearance but ideally wants slower ground

runners.name: Al Mubhir
runners.spotlight: Smart at his best for William Haggas, winning a Listed race at Sandown (1m2f, good) in July 2024; campaigned solely in Australia in 2025 and well beaten on his last two starts; potentially well handicapped back in Britain for a new yard after wind surgery, but he carries obvious risks.
runners.comment: Smart at his best and back in Britain after spell in Australia; has had wind surgery

races.race_name: HKJC World Pool Palace House Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Beckford's Folly
runners.spotlight: Has been described as a challenge by connections but he's a talented one and the drop to 5f suited when beating a smart rival (later runner-up at the Breeders' Cup) in a Group 3 over C&D (good; won a novice on good to firm) in October; nothing special on his recent return here but he was eased when his winning chance had gone and the return to 5f can suit; much respected.
runners.comment: Drop to 5f suited when beating good rival in C&D Group 3 as 2yo; respected on 2nd run back

runners.name: Frost At Dawn
runners.spotlight: A record of two wins from 16 turf starts in no way does justice to the Group 1 form she's capable of, such as a close second in the King Charles III at Royal Ascot and a third to Asfoora in the Nunthorpe at York (both 5f, good to firm); the likely fast ground is a blessing and has good claims if on song.
runners.comment: Two terrific runs in Group 1s at 5f in 2025; likes fast ground; form claims if on song

runners.name: Asfoora
runners.spotlight: Superb Australian 7yo who has had a career to be proud of, with multiple wins in home country and Europe, laying down the law in the Group 1 King Charles III at Royal Ascot in 2024 and adding further top-level wins in the Nunthorpe (good to firm) at York and the Abbaye (very soft) at Longchamp in 2025; has stayed in Britain over the winter but connections expect her to improve for the run and that the stiff uphill finish will test her.
runners.comment: Superb Australian 7yo; three Group 1 wins in Europe but expected to improve for the run

runners.name: Jm Jungle
runners.spotlight: Below best from a high draw in the Group 1 Abbaye on final 2025 start; thoroughly reliable previously and he richly deserved his Group 2 win at Goodwood (5f, good to soft; effective on good to firm) last August; versatile track-wise but has never run at Newmarket so there is a query to answer.
runners.comment: Thoroughly reliable; richly deserved Group 2 win (5f) last August; having first course run
runners.owner: MPS Racing Ltd & The Ayrshire Tradesmen

runners.name: Shagraan
runners.spotlight: In action between May and October last year for Michael Appleby, finding a form spurt late on with a close second to First Instinct in a Group 3 at Newbury sandwiched by Listed 5f wins at Beverley and Ascot (good to firm and soft); more needed back with former trainer but may yet do better.
runners.comment: Form spurt later in 2025 with Listed 5f wins (good to firm/soft); may yet progress again

runners.name: Quinault
runners.spotlight: Front-runner whose 12 wins include a Group 3 at York (7f, good) last June; he was an excellent third of 19 in the Group 1 Champions Sprint at Ascot (6f) in October; has since run up to his best in a 5f AW race at Newcastle in January and it will be interesting to see how he gets on back at this trip on turf with his regular hood discarded; ran creditably in 6f Group 3 at Newmarket last time.
runners.comment: Front-runner; usually seen at 6f-7f but useful 5f run on AW in January; regular hood off

runners.name: First Instinct
runners.spotlight: Down the field from a poor draw in the Group 1 Abbaye at Longchamp in October but had previously confirmed herself an improving 3yo when beating Shagraan in a Group 3 at Newbury (5f, good to soft; acts well on good); has hopes of doing better as a 4yo but this is a tough start to the year.
runners.comment: Improved to win Group 3 (5f, good to soft) in September; this is a tough return

runners.name: Rumstar
runners.spotlight: Acts on flat tracks but he comes into his own when tackling a stiff test at 5f, as when winning this race last year, taking C&D record to 2-2, and roaring home to win a Group 3 at Sandown (good to firm) in July; cheekpieces worn in October are discarded; yard in form; not to be underestimated.
runners.comment: Suited by tracks with stiff finishes and won this last year; not to be underestimated

runners.name: Night Raider
runners.spotlight: Often leads; he's pure quality on the AW (4-4) but has had trouble sustaining his effort on turf, even allowing for being first home on his side in a Group 2 at Goodwood in August; gelded since last seen but whether he'll appreciate this stiff finish is questionable.
runners.comment: Front-runs; pure quality on AW (4-4) but had trouble sustaining effort on turf

runners.name: Ain't Nobody
runners.spotlight: Listed winner at Royal Ascot as a 2yo; out of luck since but he showed what can be done on a good day when chasing home Asfoora in the Group 1 Nunthorpe at York (good to firm; 100-1) last August; no approach to that form since, however, and although Ryan Moore is booked, the absence of cheekpieces raises a query.
runners.comment: Winless since 2yo days; fine 2nd to Asfoora at York last August (first-time cheekpieces)

runners.name: Washington Heights
runners.spotlight: Can lead; won 6f Group 3 here in 2024 but a beaten favourite in this race last year and sole success in 2025 came in a Listed York race (5f, good to firm); below best on recent comeback and a win looks unlikely.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner at 6f here in 2024; sole success since came in Listed company; win unlikely

runners.name: Miss Attitude
runners.spotlight: She divided Washington Heights and Jm Jungle in a York Listed race (5f, good to firm) last July and later won at that level in France but, even allowing for having a fitness edge, she has to raise her game to win today.
runners.comment: Won 5f Listed race in France as 5yo; no fitness queries but has to raise her game here

runners.name: Town And Country
runners.spotlight: An improver last May/June, giving First Instinct a fright in a Cork Listed race then winning at Sandown (both about 5f; good and good to firm), but she wasn't the same filly after; has plenty to find this time.
runners.comment: An improver last May/June but wasn't the same filly after; plenty to find on these terms

runners.name: Five Ways
runners.spotlight: All races at 6f, improving on front-running novice (good to firm) and AW Group 3 wins when beaten less than 3l in the Group 1 Middle Park on this course in September; from a yard that's much respected with sprinters but this still asks a lot on the drop to 5f.
runners.comment: All runs at 6f; two front-running wins before 4th in Group 1 here in September; tall order

races.race_name: Betfred 2000 Guineas Stakes (Group 1) (No Geldings)

runners.name: Gstaad
runners.spotlight: Proved rock-solid as a 2yo, readily winning the Coventry at Royal Ascot (6f) then posting form figures of 2221 at Group/Grade 1 level, including in the Dewhurst at Newmarket (7f) when doing well considering he was free to post and in the race; ended the campaign by overcoming a wide trip in the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf at Del Mar (1m, firm; won with something to spare); supplemented (effectively re-added) into this at a cost of £30,000 having been removed in late March due to a clerical error; leading contender in his bid to end a 2,000 Guineas drought for Aidan O'Brien, who has landed this Classic a record ten times but not since 2019.
runners.comment: Rock-solid as a 2yo; ran well in the Dewhurst prior to Breeders' Cup win; leading claims
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Distant Storm
runners.spotlight: Aptly bred, being by Night Of Thunder and out of a mare by another 2,000 Guineas winner, namely George Washington; 1,900,000euros breeze-up purchase last spring and duly made a big splash in his 2yo races, aside from when pulling too hard in the Acomb; showed smart from at Newmarket (7f, good/good to firm) in the autumn, impressing in the Tattersalls Stakes (Group 3) then running well (one place behind Gstaad) in the Dewhurst; yard has landed this prize three times in the last four years; big player.
runners.comment: Impressive in the Tattersalls Stakes prior to running well in the Dewhurst; big player

runners.name: Oxagon
runners.spotlight: Very useful colt but possibly needs to dominate, having gained both wins from the front, latest when taking well to first-time cheekpieces and comfortably beating Avicenna by 2l in the Craven Stakes over C&D (good; Group 3) on seasonal debut; by no means certain to have his own way in this bigger field and he's held by a couple of today's opponents on Dewhurst running; otherwise may have more to offer in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Very useful but possibly needs to dominate; took well to cheekpieces in the Craven

runners.name: King's Trail
runners.spotlight: Son of Sea The Stars, whose stellar 3yo campaign included a success in the 2,000 Guineas; two from two, both outings over 1m on Kempton AW, last time looking value for more than the 1l winning margin in a conditions race that Notable Speech (from same yard) won in 2024 before following up in this contest; deserted by William Buick but the stable landed this Classic with the second string, ridden by James Doyle, in 2022; interesting contender who brings significant potential.
runners.comment: Similar type to his yard's winner of this race in 2024; brings big potential; interesting

runners.name: Power Blue
runners.spotlight: Juvenile campaign over sprint distances culminated in Group 1 win at the Curragh (good); held by Gstaad on Coventry running; respectable second to Thesecretadversary, who received 3lb, in 7f Group 3 at Leopardstown on reappearance but doesn't give the strong impression he'll benefit from going up further in trip; indeed, he seems more of a 6f Commonwealth Cup type.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner over 6f as a 2yo; not a guaranteed stayer in this first attempt at 1m

runners.name: Thesecretadversary
runners.spotlight: Irish colt who has built up a solid record that includes a good effort in Canadian Grade 1 on final 2yo start (sole 1m attempt); won going away from Power Blue, who conceded 3lb, in Group 3 at Leopardstown (7f, good to yielding) on reappearance; could go well but several others bring stronger claims in terms of class and/or potential.
runners.comment: Irish colt who has built up a solid record; won going away in 7f Group 3 on reappearance
runners.owner: Cayton Park Stud & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Bow Echo
runners.spotlight: Son of 2,000 Guineas winner Night Of Thunder and from the family of Dubawi; three from three last term, all starts over 1m, driven out for 1l success in the Royal Lodge at Newmarket (good to firm; Group 2) the final time; that wasn't necessarily a star performance but he keeps getting the job done and commands major respect, being a progressive colt who has a perfect record and has had a "faultless spring" according to his trainer; furthermore, he's already proven over C&D.
runners.comment: Already proven over C&D, having won the Royal Lodge to take record to 3-3; much respected
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Needle Match
runners.spotlight: Similar to Into The Sky in having raced at Newbury on both starts; did just enough to win 2yo maiden (6.5f, soft) then ran well, about 2l fourth to Alparslan, in the Greenham (7f, good); the way he shaped last time suggests additional improvement is plausible upped further in trip; by 2,000 Guineas winner Night Of Thunder; more interesting than bare ratings suggest.
runners.comment: Ran well in the Greenham; further improvement is plausible upped to 1m; not dismissed

runners.name: Avicenna
runners.spotlight: Two from two last year, both starts over 7f at Doncaster and getting up on the line in Listed grade the second time; finished nicely for 2l second to Oxagon, who dictated the pace, in the Craven over C&D (good) on reappearance having again been held up; open to further progress and could pick up some pieces in this big-field scenario, assuming the gallop is suitably strong.
runners.comment: Finished nicely for second in the Craven; could pick up some pieces if the pace is strong

runners.name: Alparslan
runners.spotlight: Reversed Dewhurst placings with Zavateri, an absentee from this race due to a setback, in the Greenham at Newbury (7f, good; Group 3) to take his record to 3-4; however, needs further progress, having finished behind several more of these in the Dewhurst, and is far from sure to get another easy lead (dictated the pace last time).
runners.comment: Had the run of things in the Greenham to take record to 3-4; another easy lead is unlikely

runners.name: Venetian Prince
runners.spotlight: Has finished behind some of today's rivals on two occasions, latest in the Craven when admittedly running well on bare figures; gained sole success (from five attempts) at novice level and looks well short of the required standard in this field; first-time headgear needs to make a difference.
runners.comment: Held by some of today's rivals on two pieces of form; first-time headgear needs to help

runners.name: Padraig Dawn
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; AW form this year comprises a win at Southwell (7f) and neck second, doing well having come from difficult position, at Newcastle (1m Listed); promising colt for first-season trainer Charlie Pike but faces a much stiffer task on turf debut; supplemented at a cost of £30,000.
runners.comment: Promising colt for his first-season trainer but faces a difficult task on turf debut

runners.name: Lord Britain
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mainly on AW, most recently 5l fourth (ran well but never dangerous) to King's Trail at Kempton on seasonal debut; bottom of this pack on the figures and trailed home last in the Royal Lodge on sole turf attempt; this outsider has plenty to prove and plenty to find.
runners.comment: Bottom of this pack on bare figures; finished last in the Royal Lodge on sole turf start

runners.name: Billecart
runners.spotlight: Has plenty to find in this field and needs to settle better to boot, having raced too freely in Listed event last time when bidding for a Newcastle (1m, AW) double; behind Distant Storm on sole turf attempt; unlikely to emulate his sire Night Of Thunder, who landed this prize in 2014.
runners.comment: Has plenty to find in this field and needs to settle better than last time

runners.name: Into The Sky
runners.spotlight: Showed useful form over 6.5f and 6f at Newbury (good/good to soft) last season, winning a maiden by sizeable margin then clear second in the Mill Reef (Group 2; hung left; collared near the finish); good prospect for Jim Boyle this term but doesn't appear to be crying out for 1m.
runners.comment: Mill Reef runner-up; good prospect for Jim Boyle but doesn't seem to be crying out for 1m
runners.owner: Mrs D Tabor, Dr P Wilson & R Stanbridge

races.race_name: Edgewood Stakes Presented By Accenture (Grade 2) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Lorelei Lee
runners.spotlight: Has made a pleasing start to her career with two wins from three starts and related to Grade 2 winner Somelikeithotbrown; scored narrowly in a Listed race, showing early speed to lead from a wide draw upped to this trip at Turfway Park six weeks ago; deserves respect despite being up in class.
runners.comment: This represents a step up in class but should prove competitive.

runners.name: Taken By The Wind
runners.spotlight: Finished third, beaten 15l, in an allowance Grade 2 at Oaklawn Park 35 days ago; winner of three of her five career runs; has won in classy company before and no surprise to see her go well.
runners.comment: Has won in classy company before and has a decent shout.
runners.owner: Magdalena Racing, Terry Bradshaw Et Al

runners.name: Time To Dream
runners.spotlight: Couldn't quite get the job done when 11-5 runner-up (Tampa Bay Downs) behind Dandona two months ago in a Grade 3.
runners.comment: Minor honours two months ago; wins-runs record of 2-5.

runners.name: Lovely Grey
runners.spotlight: Went close when second in a Listed race at Turfway Park behind Lorelei Lee six weeks ago; also engaged in race 13 at Churchill Downs.
runners.comment: Not beaten far six weeks ago; wins-runs record of 1-10.

runners.name: Dandona
runners.spotlight: Winner of two of her three career starts; got the job done by just over 1l two months ago at Tampa Bay Downs in a Grade 3; the trainer continues to be among winners recently.
runners.comment: Just over 1l winner two months ago; winner of two of her three career runs.

runners.name: Imaginationthelady
runners.spotlight: Went down by a head in a Grade 2 (Keeneland) behind Storm's Wake four weeks ago; winner of two of her four career starts and won a Grade 2 in October; should go well.
runners.comment: Can be in the thick of things; beaten narrowly last month.

runners.name: Tam Tam
runners.spotlight: Landed a narrow success in a Listed race at Gulfstream Park 34 days ago; aiming to complete a hat-trick and improved form the last twice; tried over an additional 1f now.
runners.comment: Scored narrowly five weeks ago; out to complete a hat-trick.

runners.name: Connect The Stars
runners.spotlight: Cracking effort in defeat over 7 1/2f at Gulfstream Park (Listed race) behind Tam Tam 34 days ago; up in distance by an extra 1f here.
runners.comment: Just beaten 34 days ago; tried over an additional 1f now.

runners.name: To A Flame
runners.spotlight: This daughter of Justify looks the part on breeding and two from four in her career so far; safely held at Keeneland last month behind Storm's Wake when not beaten that far in a Grade 2.
runners.comment: Finished seventh last month; has made a pleasing start to her career with two wins from four starts.

runners.name: Storm's Wake
runners.spotlight: On target three times in her last seven outings; a course winner; shock 22-1 winner (Keeneland) last month overcoming four of these rivals in a Grade 2.
runners.comment: Beat a bunch of these last month; on target three times in her last seven outings.

runners.name: Just Aloof
runners.spotlight: Looks the part on breeding (by Justify) and has made a pleasing start to her career with two wins from three starts; fair effort when half a length fourth to Storm's Wake last month at Keeneland in a Grade 2.
runners.comment: Not beaten far last month; winner of two of her three career starts.

runners.name: Lion Lake
runners.spotlight: A neck behind the winner, Storm's Wake, over 1m at Keeneland (Grade 2) last month when not beaten far; managed two wins in her last three outings; showing consistent form.
runners.comment: Beaten narrowly 27 days ago; has chalked up two wins from her last three.

runners.name: Bohemian
runners.spotlight: Fair form when just over 3l third in a black type, finishing well behind reopposing Indigo Woods at Fair Grounds two months ago; a previous winner here; on target twice in her last four outings.
runners.comment: Fair level of form two months ago; has chalked up two wins from her last four.

runners.name: Indigo Woods
runners.spotlight: Landed a narrow success in a black type at Fair Grounds two months ago; bids to pull off a hat-trick and unbeaten after two career runs; not discounted despite having more on her plate.
runners.comment: This represents a step up in class but could be in the mix.

races.race_name: Longines Kentucky Oaks (Grade 1) (Fillies) (Dirt)

runners.name: Prom Queen
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner at Gulfstream Park of maiden in February (by 8l) and Grade 2 Gulfstream Park Oaks (8.5f; beat My Miss Mo by about 3l in steadily run race); travelled strongly, made eye-catching move on turn and not at all hard ridden to assert in latter race, scoring with lots in hand while still a bit green; should be suited by 1m1f; much stiffer test and must adapt to big field, but open to considerable improvement and an exciting prospect.
runners.comment: Big move on turn to win 8.5f Grade 2 at Gulfstream on only third start; exciting prospect

runners.name: Always A Runner
runners.spotlight: Most promising winner of both starts in a maiden at Tampa Bay in February (by just over 6l) and Grade 3 Gazelle Stakes (1m1f) at Aqueduct in April; always going well but had a bit to do on latter occasion, going on really strongly to beat Pashmina by just over 1l, well on top at finish; stays well (will likely have to come from some way back) and is very good prospect.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in two and stayed on strongly to win Grade 3 at Aqueduct (1m1f) by 1l; promising
runners.owner: Douglas Scharbauer & Three Chimneys Farm LLC

runners.name: Counting Stars
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner of four races, notably Grade 2 Fantasy Stakes (well placed, beat Search Party by 5l) at Oaklawn Park last time out; possibly unlucky when just under 1l second to Explora in Grade 3 Honeybee there on penultimate start, plenty to do off moderate fractions; strong claims on first start at 1m1f.
runners.comment: Easy Grade 2 winner latest; looked unlucky when second to Explora time before; good chance

runners.name: Percy's Bar
runners.spotlight: Demoted to second in Grade 1 last year, when also third in Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies despite being hampered; quickened through inside gap, got first run, when beating Zany by about 2l in Grade 1 Ashland at Keeneland (8.5f) on reappearance; others may improve more at 1m1f but she possesses impressive tactical speed.
runners.comment: Breeders' Cup third; got first run, beat Zany 2l in 8.5f Grade 1 at Keeneland; good chance

runners.name: Explora
runners.spotlight: High-class juvenile, easy winner of Grade 2 Oak Leaf at Santa Anita and good second in Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies at Del Mar; winner of two of three starts this year, on latest start beating Counting Stars by just under 1l in Grade 3 Honeybee at Oaklawn; should stay 1m1f and is a tractable, focused runner, but has a two-month layoff (missed intended prep) to overcome.
runners.comment: Grade 2 win in 2025 and beat Counting Stars in Grade 3 at Oaklawn; player but 2 months off
runners.owner: Michael E Pegram, Karl Watson & Paul Weitman

runners.name: Meaning
runners.spotlight: Winner of three of four starts, notably Grade 2 Santa Anita Oaks by 2l from Brooklyn Blonde, tracking pace and asserting steadily in straight; only defeat when 4l fourth to Super Corredora in Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies at Del Mar, where behind Explora and Percy's Bar; solid place claims.
runners.comment: Behind Explora and Percy's Bar when fourth at Breeders' Cup but Grade 2 win latest; claims

runners.name: Pashmina
runners.spotlight: In frame in black-type races three times after winning a maiden at Fair Grounds by 10l; very good second, beaten only around 1l and well clear or rest, behind Always A Runner in Grade 3 Gazelle at Aqueduct (1m1f) last time, when she looked to be getting away from eventual winner but hopped tracks in straight; smart filly, bit more exposed than some, but an interesting outsider.
runners.comment: In frame in black-type races and Grade 3 (improved form); smart but bit exposed

runners.name: Resist
runners.spotlight: Reserve who now gets in; progressive form at lower level; best effort when about 2l third (Lovely Grey second) to Lorelei Lee in Listed race at Turfway Park last time; very stiff task.
runners.comment: Very stiff task based on third in Listed race at Turfway Park on latest start

runners.name: Zany
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner of both starts last year in maiden at Gulfstream Park and Grade 2 Demoiselle at Aqueduct in December; landed the odds in Listed race at Tampa in February; unsuited by slow pace, caught wide and stayed on well late when about 2l second to Percy's Bar in Grade 1 Ashland at Keeneland last month (again edged left in straight); will relish first run beyond 8.5f; serious claims.
runners.comment: Grade 2 winner in 2025 caught eye when second to Percy's Bar at Keeneland; serious claims

runners.name: Search Party
runners.spotlight: Useful form at Oaklawn to win maiden (by 6l) last December and Listed race (by neck) in February; in frame there since, unable to get on terms, when fourth to Explora in Grade 3 Honeybee and second to Counting Stars in Grade 2 Fantasy; will be suited by further than 8.5f but something to find.
runners.comment: Listed winner but never on terms when in frame in Graded company at Oaklawn; needs more

runners.name: Brooklyn Blonde
runners.spotlight: First race outside maiden company (winner at Santa Anita in March) when 2l second to Meaning in 8.5f Grade 2 Santa Anita Oaks last month, making an effort to challenge in straight but brushed aside; should stay 1m1f but needs to find a good bit more.
runners.comment: Good 2l second to Meaning in 8.5f Grade 2 at Santa Anita, brushed aside; needs to improve

runners.name: Lovely Grey
runners.spotlight: Reserve who now gets in; beaten on all five starts outside maidens and yet to tackle a Graded race; very stiff task.
runners.comment: Beaten all five starts outside maidens and yet to run in a Graded race; very stiff task

runners.name: Nycon
runners.spotlight: Reserve who now gets in; runner-up in black-type race and Listed event this year but well held when fourth behind Always A Runner in Grade 3 Gazelle at Aqueduct; lots to find.
runners.comment: Well held when 10l fourth to Always A Runner in Grade 3 Gazelle at Aqueduct; lots to find

runners.name: Dazzling Dame
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has won four races, notably black-type race at Colonial Downs in March; well held on her only go in better company when beaten 10l in Grade 3 at Churchill Downs last September; would be a shock winner against this quality of opponent.
runners.comment: Four lesser wins, notably black-type race; well held in a Grade 3 race; outsider
runners.owner: Respect The Valleys LLC, Medallion Racing & Madaket Stables

races.race_name: Elite Sand & Soil Hawkesbury Guineas (Group 3) (Turf)

runners.name: Skyhook
runners.spotlight: Beyond midfield, under pump turn and plugged home at Randwick second-up. Ready to peak third-up. Right in this if things pan out.
runners.owner: Ryan & Alexiou Racing, Stallion Match Et Al

runners.name: Buffalo
runners.spotlight: Plate (C5) winner Newcastle 28 Feb. Warming into it late at Randwick second-up. Can have a major say if things pan out.

runners.name: Karinska
runners.spotlight: Really good run coming from the back in H B Carr Stakes at Randwick at this trip. Could add some value to exotics.
runners.owner: Star Thoroughbreds Pty Ltd, Mrs D M Handley Et Al

runners.name: Hidden Achievement
runners.spotlight: Resuming (25-weeks). Others are packing more punch.

runners.name: Bird Whistle
runners.spotlight: Latest attempt this journey when reasonable effort without seriously threatening at Randwick at second run back. Stripping fitter and capable of making an impact.
runners.owner: R R Crosby & Mrs P L Crosby

runners.name: Estremo
runners.spotlight: Ran on fairly from back half at Randwick at first run off a long break. Will be fitter second-up. Good draw. Can play a role.
runners.owner: Star Thoroughbreds Pty Ltd, Paramount Racing Et Al

runners.name: Dusty Bay
runners.spotlight: Long time since last saluted. In the thick of it at Newcastle two back 31 Mar. Forwardly placed, continued to stick on at Randwick at last start this journey. First time around Hawkesbury.
runners.owner: Kooringal Stud, G L Hundy Et Al

runners.name: Cavallo Park
runners.spotlight: Settled close and ran an honest race at Randwick second-up. Not fancied.

runners.name: Regal Award
runners.spotlight: Came home hot on their heels to grab third South Pacific Classic at Randwick at second run back. Since trialled at Warwick Farm 23 Apr. Sharper now and highly rated in this line-up.
runners.owner: Ms D S Sealey, G & C Pastoral Co Pty Ltd & V A Oldfield

runners.name: Horseshoe Hill
runners.spotlight: Handy and acquitted herself fairly well at Randwick over this trip. Drawn well. Won only other time at Hawkesbury. Capable of making an impact.
runners.owner: Ridgmont, R I Hitchcock Et Al

runners.name: Wootton Lass
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Gave good account 3yo+ F&M (Bm78) at Randwick. Has her first look at 1400m here. Looks up against it.
runners.owner: I W Moses & Ms V G McIntosh

runners.name: Burma Star
runners.spotlight: Tame to line in small field in rain affected going at Caulfield at second run back. First time around Hawkesbury. Others carry more punch.

races.race_name: Pioneer Services Hawkesbury Crown (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Infancy
runners.spotlight: Out the back, closed with raking strides in the Sapphire Stakes at Randwick in second run from spell. Usually gets back. First time around Hawkesbury. Not fancied.
runners.owner: Miss S M Parkinson, Mrs K Lovett Et Al

runners.name: Chica Mojito
runners.spotlight: Freshened. Won the Open Hcp Wyong 10 Jan. Since trialled at Warwick Farm 23 Apr. Drawn to receive every chance.
runners.owner: B J Spicer, Mrs M Gamble Et Al

runners.name: Zaszou
runners.spotlight: Now 5 weeks between runs. Just wacked away without threatening over 1200m Star Kingdom Stakes at Rosehill. Since trialled at Hawkesbury 20 Apr. First time around Hawkesbury.
runners.owner: Bennett Racing Pty Ltd, R Menchise Et Al

runners.name: Luvnwar
runners.spotlight: First-up (17-weeks). Ex Michael Grantham in WA. Market watch advised.

runners.name: Konasana
runners.spotlight: Resuming (16-weeks). Prepared for return with 3 trials. Wait until she shows something.

runners.name: Oh Diamond Lil
runners.spotlight: Resuming (25-weeks). Tibbie Stakes winner Newcastle 19 Sep. Winner fresh and 3 trials will have her ready for this. Can lead or take a sit.
runners.owner: A G J Koolman, Mrs C J Inglis Et Al

runners.name: Bauhinia
runners.spotlight: Close placing in the Danish Stakes at Rosehill. Last at turn, ran on late at Randwick at first run off a long break. Strips fitter second run back. Could get into the placings.
runners.owner: Mrs C M Cook, Ms T Marchant Et Al

runners.name: Coco Jamboo
runners.spotlight: Last win was 4 May 2024. Quietly handled in back half, shaken up and finished full of running at Randwick at second run back. Hard to make a case.
runners.owner: D A Dickie, Mrs L Dickie Et Al

runners.name: Countyourblessings
runners.spotlight: Let-up (77-days). Ex John O'Shea & Tom Charlton in NSW. Raced in back half and was never in a position to mount a serious challenge at Randwick. Had two trials since.
runners.owner: D Dickson, M D Sullivan Et Al

runners.name: Yes Lulu
runners.spotlight: First-up (15-weeks). Goes into wider exotics fresh in this company.
runners.owner: Ciaron Maher Racing, Brut Racing 8 Et Al

runners.name: Chidiac
runners.spotlight: Now 4 weeks between runs. Striving for hat-trick. Ridden positively and showed plenty of mettle to win at Randwick second-up. Since trialled at Muswellbrook 22 Apr.

runners.name: Arriving Home
runners.spotlight: Latest attempt this journey when led from pillar to near the post in the 3yo+ (Bm78) here. Drawn to get a conservative run and under less weight. Can make her presence felt.
runners.owner: Ridgmont, R I Hitchcock Et Al

runners.name: Surfin' Bird
runners.spotlight: Kicks off second campaign (25-weeks). Tuned up with a couple of trials and is a first-up winner. Won 4 of 6 starts in first campaign. Others carry more punch.
runners.owner: First Light Racing Pty Ltd, Aristia Park Bloodstock Et Al

runners.name: La Roja
runners.spotlight: Let-up (70-days). In the thick of it at Randwick two back 7 Feb. Trailed speed, kept coming but winner too strong at Rosehill at this trip. Tougher test from the draw.
runners.owner: Snowden Racing Pty Ltd Syndicate, Ridgmont Syndicate Et Al

runners.name: Artistic Venture
runners.spotlight: Let-up (77-days). Ex John O'Shea & Tom Charlton in NSW. Raced in back half and never likely at Randwick first up. Had two trials since.
runners.owner: Tricolours Racing & Syndications Ltd, A C Lloyd Jones Et Al

runners.name: Our Queen
runners.spotlight: Okay at latest when settled back. One for multiples.

races.race_name: Richmond Club Hawkesbury Gold Cup (Group 3) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Vivy Air
runners.spotlight: Freshened. Close placing in the Coolmore Classic at Rosehill. Failed to threaten in rain affected going in the Doncaster at second run back. Easier task and better draw here. Respecting.
runners.owner: Ciaron Maher Racing, A R E Investments Et Al

runners.name: Osipenko
runners.spotlight: Given time to freshen up after 1st-up run. Won the Little Dance Randwick 4 Nov. Ran on late from the back at Caulfield first up. Strips fitter second run back and due for a win.
runners.owner: C J Waller, I Bruce Et Al

runners.name: Middle Earth
runners.spotlight: Had all before him at turn and went home tamely at Caulfield when off an extended spell. Too sharp for him here. Others with greater appeal.

runners.name: Waterford
runners.spotlight: Given time to freshen up after 1st-up run. Never in the call at Rosehill at first run back. Since trialled at Hawkesbury 20 Apr. First time around Hawkesbury.
runners.owner: R C Callander, S Saric Et Al

runners.name: Tavi Time
runners.spotlight: Made late rally from the back at Randwick at first run back. Fitter, proven second-up. Worth a look.
runners.owner: A Pazman, F Alam Et Al

runners.name: Amor Victorious
runners.spotlight: First-up (16-weeks). Given two trials and has figured first-up. Leads, handy. Placed only other visit to Hawkesbury.
runners.owner: Darby Racing, M Allenby Et Al

runners.name: Royal Supremacy
runners.spotlight: Import returning for second Australian campaign (25-weeks). The Metropolitan conqueror last time in. Two quiet trials for return. First time around Hawkesbury.
runners.owner: Australian Bloodstock, S Tsalikidis Et Al

runners.name: Shohisha
runners.spotlight: Fresh. Gave solid chase in a good effort at Rosehill. At optimum distance. Could get into the placings.
runners.owner: W N Boyde, Ms G M Chapo & Dodgy Investments Pty Limited

runners.name: Captain Furai
runners.spotlight: Shooting for hat-trick. In touch and fought on dauntlessly at Randwick. Nicely weighted. Should appreciate stepping up in distance.

runners.name: Palmetto
runners.spotlight: Fresh. Last win was 9 Mar 2025. Did little in choppy conditions at Randwick over a longer test. Switching to 1600m.
runners.owner: C R Smith, C E Holt Et Al

runners.name: Churchill's Choice
runners.spotlight: 5 weeks since last run. In the thick of it at Newcastle two back 6 Mar. Ridden cold and never in the picture at Rosehill second-up. Since trialled at Gosford 13 Apr.

runners.name: Sea King
runners.spotlight: 5 weeks since last run. Out of the money at Doomben second-up following market easing. Back over a trip short of best. Others have more zip.
runners.owner: OTI Sea King, L D Freeman Et Al

runners.name: War Eternal
runners.spotlight: On pace but conceded late at Randwick when drifting in the betting. Hard to make a case.
runners.owner: Bjorn Baker Racing, A D Mathews Et Al

runners.name: Imperialist
runners.spotlight: First-up (15-weeks). Landed Open Hcp win Taree 17 Oct. Two quiet trials for return. First time around Hawkesbury.
runners.owner: C J Waller, N A Greenhalgh Et Al

runners.name: Taken
runners.spotlight: Just missed at Flemington 28 Mar. Narrow miss in the Anniversary Vase in soft going at Caulfield 11 Apr. More suitable draw. Key contender.
runners.owner: C E Holt, Vanoosa Et Al

runners.name: Yet He Moves
runners.spotlight: Closed off late from the tail at Gold Coast first up. Strips fitter second run back. Can't enthuse.
runners.owner: T Schembri, Mrs M Schembri Et Al

runners.name: Hyperbolic
runners.spotlight: Went home tamely at Randwick at $16. Others are packing more punch.
runners.owner: M J Gregg, J Amedee Et Al

runners.name: Sky Lab
runners.spotlight: Never sighted at first run off a long break at Randwick. First time around Hawkesbury. Needs further, and rain would improve chances.
runners.owner: P Perry & Ms C G Collis

races.race_name: Ticketmaster Queensland Guineas (Group 2) (Turf)

runners.name: State Visit
runners.spotlight: On pace but found little at the business end here at second run back. Others rate stronger.

runners.name: Snitzel Dancer
runners.spotlight: Quickly working through her grades. Won the Hcp (C1) at Hawkesbury two back 7 Mar. Reeled in leaders to salute at Randwick. Drawn to effect.
runners.owner: B K Racing & Breeding Pty Ltd, R E McMillan Et Al

runners.name: Brave Monarch
runners.spotlight: Made easy work of Daybreak Lover win this venue second-up at $4.40. Even fitter third-up and well placed to win again. More suitable draw and has a great record under Martin Harley here. More ground a plus.
runners.owner: Hancox Bloodstock Puppet, K M Anderson Et Al

runners.name: Call Da Vinci
runners.spotlight: Got back, ran on late with purpose this track at second run back. Handles wet ground. Sharper now and not out of this.

runners.name: Spanish Treasure
runners.spotlight: Won the 3yo+ Mdn Plate Gold Coast 10 Jan. No match but kept on for a distant third in the Daybreak Lover this track. Ready for the mile now. Well into preparation now and capable of making an impact.
runners.owner: Mrs Z A Daly, B E Barnett Et Al

runners.name: Options
runners.spotlight: Settled back and didn't figure at first run off a long break in the South Pacific Classic at Randwick. Strips fitter second run back. Awkwardly drawn and rising in weight. Goes into wider exotics.

runners.name: Tupakara
runners.spotlight: Broke maiden at Wyong 4 Apr. Well back and made some minor headway but passing mainly tired rivals this venue. Shapes as a rough place prospect.

runners.name: Kilman
runners.spotlight: 4 weeks since last run. Established lead at Randwick Open, stuck to task and only denied late at this trip. Since trialled at Randwick 21 Apr. First time around Eagle Farm.
runners.owner: N Morgan & D H T Devine

runners.name: Bingi
runners.spotlight: Now 4 weeks between runs. Ex P G Moody & Katherine Coleman. Trained here. Finished out the back at Randwick over a longer test. Coming back in distance.

runners.name: Special Artist
runners.spotlight: Off pace, only battled home here as an odds drifter. Others with more appeal.

runners.name: Autumn King
runners.spotlight: Won a 1200m Class 1 at Beaumont two back. Ran home respectably into a non-threatening 5th at Randwick. Since trialled at Muswellbrook 22 Apr. Unexposed at trip.
runners.owner: Arrowfield Pastoral Pty Ltd, S A Dyson Et Al

runners.name: Mr Worthington
runners.spotlight: Ridden back and never a factor this venue at first run back. Usually gets back. Looks up against it.
runners.owner: Mrs K M Gleeson, J Sherman Et Al

runners.name: Another Cashie
runners.spotlight: No threat as a $41 chance in Daybreak Lover this venue. Untried in the wet. Rise to 1600m a new challenge. Others with greater appeal.
runners.owner: Calladunn, N V Schmidt Et Al

runners.name: Summation
runners.spotlight: Came home hot on their heels to grab third E&G Hcp (C1) at Doomben second-up. First try at 1600m. Others with stronger claims.
runners.owner: Think Big Stud, S J Williams Et Al

runners.name: Vandevelde
runners.spotlight: Attacked line late to claim third in the small field at Gold Coast at first run back. Sterner task awaits.
runners.owner: D R Foster, L K Wahlstrom Et Al

races.race_name: Ladbrokes Victory Stakes (Group 2) (Turf)

runners.name: Transatlantic
runners.spotlight: Resuming (25-weeks). Won the G1 Five Diamonds Prelude last time in. Just collared in Open company at Flemington first-up last campaign while tuned with 2 trials. On pacer.
runners.owner: Mrs Z A Daly, Segenhoe Thoroughbreds Australia Et Al

runners.name: Lady Of Camelot
runners.spotlight: Resuming after a (59-weeks) spell. Last prep was highlighted by a G1 placing. Just missed at Randwick 1 Mar 2025. Winner fresh and 2 trials will have her ready for this.

runners.name: Uncommon James
runners.spotlight: Prominent and showed plenty of ticker to just go under in thrilling finish in the Ascot Hcp this track when off an extended spell. Will be fitter second-up. Can lead or take a sit. A distance specialist.

runners.name: Reserve Bank
runners.spotlight: First-up (51-weeks). The Goodwood winner last preparation. Seen in 3 trials since he last faced the starter. Looking elsewhere.
runners.owner: S Tsalikidis, C Cortese Et Al

runners.name: Hidden Wealth
runners.spotlight: Gave good account Ascot Hcp this track at first run back. Has won multiple times in wet. Will be fitter second-up. Can challenge if things pan out.
runners.owner: Wessel Thoroughbreds, M A McLean Et Al

runners.name: In Flight
runners.spotlight: Raced off the speed and mustered well to get the money at Randwick,. Relishes the sting out of the ground. Has a tricky draw to contend with this time. Can again play a role if she gets the breaks.
runners.owner: Proven Thoroughbreds, Ms K A Tibbey Et Al

runners.name: Private Harry
runners.spotlight: First-up (30-weeks). Ex Nathan Doyle in NSW. Winner fresh and 2 trials will have him ready for this. Tactical speed, inside draw.
runners.owner: Kurrinda Bloodstock, Yu Long Investments Et Al

runners.name: Warnie
runners.spotlight: Resuming (20-weeks). Given two trials and has won first-up. Looks up against it.
runners.owner: Bennett Racing Pty Ltd, D Phillips Et Al

runners.name: Abounding
runners.spotlight: Reasonable effort without seriously threatening this track in second run from spell. One for multiples.
runners.owner: First Light Racing Pty Ltd, F L R Pretty Mixed Et Al

runners.name: Splash Back
runners.spotlight: Ran on late from the back at Randwick at first run off a long break. Strips fitter second run back. Usually gets back. First time around Eagle Farm.
runners.owner: Ronball, C McGregor & Miss Z J McGregor

runners.name: Payline
runners.spotlight: Last win was 4 Jan 2025. In the thick of it at Gold Coast two back 17 Jan. Game effort here at first run back. Will be fitter second-up.
runners.owner: Mrs C L Munce, T D Chivers Et Al

runners.name: Zarastro
runners.spotlight: Let-up (63-days). Threw the anchor out late after leading at Randwick in second run from spell. Two tick over trials since. Should be prominent.
runners.owner: Executive Rc Syndicate, H Richardson Et Al

runners.name: Pereille
runners.spotlight: Resuming (14-weeks). In the minors at Doomben first-up last campaign while readied with 2 trial hit-outs. Picked up cheques in 4 of 4 starts of last campaign (including this distance). Others rate stronger.
runners.owner: Australian Bloodstock, F J Biviano Et Al

races.race_name: The Unicorn Stakes (Grade 3) (Dirt)
Thomas Farms South Australian Derby (Group 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Strictly Business
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Ridden cold and never in the picture this track. Usually gets back. Could add some value to exotics.
runners.owner: Mhumhain Racing, D Insall Et Al

runners.name: After Summer
runners.spotlight: Refused to shirk task in Australian Oaks at Randwick. Makes a strong case in this company.
runners.owner: Sutton Racing Pty Ltd, S Chappell Et Al

runners.name: Geneva
runners.spotlight: Freshened. Good form across the ditch. Absorbs pressure well. New to Morphettville.

runners.name: Wigmore
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Better for first Aust run Ex-NZ. Raced in back half and failed to issue a serious challenge here at first run for new trainer. Keener on others.
runners.owner: OTI Wigmore, P G Bruce Et Al

runners.name: Savisanta
runners.spotlight: Now 4 weeks between runs. Close placing in the Alister Clark Stakes at Caulfield. Was a long way from the action in 2400m Australian Derby at Randwick. New to Morphettville.
runners.owner: C J Waller, D B Flynn Et Al

runners.name: Kaye Jay
runners.spotlight: Freshened. Close placing in the Alister Clark Stakes at Caulfield. Sent out $26 when laboured to finish 4th in choppy conditions at Randwick. Could get into the placings.
runners.owner: Mrs B K Saunders & B A E Saunders

runners.name: Engine Of War
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Ran on late from the back this track. Tackling stronger line-up.
runners.owner: MyRacehorse No 1, D E Gwynne Et Al

runners.name: Kazaru
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Started $51 when battled home in Open company this venue. Has her first look at 2518m here. Hard to warm to.
runners.owner: O T I Racing, A M Kerr Et Al

runners.name: Autumn Mystery
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Had all before him at turn and went home tamely here. Can't enthuse.
runners.owner: N J Thompson & Mrs D A Thompson

runners.name: Amazake
runners.spotlight: Void of early speed but he let down strongly Open company at Caulfield. Stiffer opposition here.
runners.owner: Ciaron Maher Racing, C M R Diminish Et Al

runners.name: Silvasista
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Shooting for hat-trick. Annexed the (Bm62) at Donald 14 Apr. Made easy work of VRC St Leger win at Flemington at $3.70.
runners.owner: Peachester Lodge Pty Ltd, S Disch Et Al

runners.name: Single Choice
runners.spotlight: Ran on well to bag a 1.3 len win in the Galilee Series Final at Caulfield. Among the dangers.
runners.owner: M Cumani, Brimac Racing Et Al

runners.name: Impulsive Reaction
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Bided his time at the back and let down smartly here. Others rate stronger.

runners.name: Cannae
runners.spotlight: Close placing in the Morphettville Guineas at Morphettville. Brave in defeat Port Adelaide Guineas here. Stamina on trial here. Not fancied.
runners.owner: D T O'Brien & Mrs J O'Brien

runners.name: Flying Brant
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Run down in the shadows after giving a good kick this venue. Rise to 2518m a new challenge. Tackling stronger line-up.
runners.owner: Campolina Pty Ltd, 84/85 Premiership Et Al

runners.name: Arabian Prince
runners.spotlight: Broke maiden at Kilmore 31 Mar. Really good run coming from the back in Galilee Series Final at Caulfield. Shapes as a rough place prospect.
runners.owner: Dr N Berry, Mrs M E Berry & Illowra Stud

runners.name: Lunar Force
runners.spotlight: In the thick of it at Kilmore two back 31 Mar. Made late rally from the back at Caulfield. Hard to make a case.
runners.owner: R Griffiths, High Calibre Racing et al

runners.name: Shotaro
runners.spotlight: Dominant on-pace performance in the 3yo+ Mdn Plate at Kilmore. Rise to 2518m a new challenge. Just out of his maiden and facing tougher challenge here.
runners.owner: K J Payne & Mrs V M Payne

runners.name: Borchard
runners.spotlight: Sent out $31 when unable to threaten at Balaklava. Has his first look at 2518m here. Others are packing more punch.
runners.owner: Ms L M Conlon, L M Conlon Et Al

runners.name: Accidental Bid
runners.spotlight: Only better for two Aust runs. Knuckled down well in sticky going winning the (Bm62) under a big impost at Pakenham. Looks up against it.
runners.owner: Australian Bloodstock, G & C Pastoral Co Pty Ltd Et Al

races.race_name: Keio Hai Spring Cup (Grade 2) (Turf)
W.A. Sires' Produce Stakes (Group 3) (Turf)

runners.name: Afireofgidgeecoals
runners.spotlight: Absolutely no fluke to his Karrakatta win despite the big SP. Extra trip looks fine. Can win again.

runners.name: Beatty
runners.spotlight: Lost the race at the start when slowly out in the Karrakatta. Very good juvenile who can atone here over 1400m.

runners.name: Street Monarch
runners.spotlight: Close up in sound performance 2yo Mdn here at her second race start after blowing in the market. Much harder here.
runners.owner: G J Hunter, R J Logie Et Al

runners.name: Run Rupert Run
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Not disgraced this track at his second race start. Looking elsewhere.
runners.owner: L K Fernie, D P Venables & M D Farmer

runners.name: Fingal
runners.spotlight: Acquitted self well with debut placing at Pinjarra Scarpside 15 Apr. Has to improve a lot however.

runners.name: Ruthless Angel
runners.spotlight: Never in the call this track. Home track advantage but has an adverse draw. Others with greater appeal.
runners.owner: Mrs R M Fortune, M D Fortune Et Al

runners.name: Jedi Dream
runners.spotlight: Given a cold ride in the Karrakatta when stormed home late in fine style. Should relish 1400m on that. In the mix.
runners.owner: Sm & Kb Bloodstock, K Sutherland Et Al

runners.name: Lady Torque
runners.spotlight: Settled back and passed a few late in the Karrakatta Plate here last outing. Gets CJP and a good gate. Likely improver.
runners.owner: Mrs C L Morton, Ridgeport Et Al

runners.name: Consignment
runners.spotlight: Prominent and fought off challengers in an exciting battle this venue. Big step up in grade and tricky gate.
runners.owner: N D Parnham, Mrs C A Parnham Et Al

runners.name: Wolf Whistle Zel
runners.spotlight: Failed to threaten this venue at second run back at cricket score odds. Hard to make a case.

runners.name: Clear Approval
runners.spotlight: Gave nothing else a chance when led all the way at Pinj S in quick time. Has the 1400m win ticked off. Great map.

runners.name: Vinny Nay
runners.spotlight: Promising debut performance this track 22 Apr. Strikes a harder race.

races.race_name: Oddschecker Ellen Chaloner Stakes (Listed Race) (Formerly The Kilvington Stakes) (Fillies & Mares)

runners.name: Flora Of Bermuda
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner in July 2024; campaigned mostly at Group 1 level since, making the frame on several occasions, most recently in the 2025 Sprint Cup at Haydock (6f, good) where she did well having met trouble; leading player back down in class.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner who has made the frame in several Group 1 events; leading contender

runners.name: Sky Majesty
runners.spotlight: Has come up short in Group 1 attempts, including last two appearances; record when racing below that level on turf is unblemished (5-5) and features Listed, Group 3 and Group 2 wins on British, French and Irish soil; strong claims back down in grade.
runners.comment: Record when racing below Group 1 level on turf is unblemished (5-5); strong claims

runners.name: Sayidah Dariyan
runners.spotlight: Had excuses (trip/draw) returned to Group 1 level in last two appearances; progressive otherwise and ran well in the Commonwealth Cup prior to Group 3 win at York (6f, good) last summer; may still have more to offer; major contender.
runners.comment: Best to forgive last two efforts; progressive otherwise and may still have more to offer

runners.name: Magic Basma
runners.spotlight: Ended last season in a consistent vein of form at Listed level, creditable third at Doncaster (6f) the final time; may continue to give her running but that won't be enough to belatedly double her tally; 0-9 since 2yo debut success.
runners.comment: Ended last season in a consistent vein of form but she's 0-9 since her debut success

runners.name: Rosy Affair
runners.spotlight: Record is 5-13; ended her 2025 campaign with an August double that comprised a Listed race at Pontefract (good to firm) and Group 3 at Deauville (soft), both over 6f; progressive and tactically versatile mare who warrants respect on reappearance.
runners.comment: Progressive; ended her 2025 campaign with an August double (Listed/Group 3); respected

runners.name: Hold A Dream
runners.spotlight: Novice winner as a 2yo; placed four times in Listed grade, most recently runner-up over C&D (good to firm) on final start in 2025; ran below market expectations at Bath on reappearance but consistent on the whole and may rebound.
runners.comment: Placed four times in Listed grade, including over C&D; below par on seasonal debut

runners.name: Paris Babe
runners.spotlight: Gained her French wins in maiden/handicap races; faded late over 7f on the AW finals card at Newcastle on British debut; may prove suited by this drop back in distance but the opposite is true of the step back up in class; stiff task.
runners.comment: Ex-French filly who faces a stiff task in this second British start

runners.name: Rhyme Dust
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage filly whose form wearing hood comprises a success in 6f novice event and two good efforts in Listed grade, third at Ayr when last seen (final outing for Mark Usher; remains with same owner); could go well in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Low-mileage filly who could go well in the retained hood; stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Celandine
runners.spotlight: Disappointing in similar event over C&D when last seen and is 0-5 since winning the Lowther as a 2yo; however, this seasonal debut may be the time to catch her; good third to Sayidah Dariyan in Group 3 at York (6f) on 2025 reappearance.
runners.comment: Seasonal debutante; good third to Sayidah Dariyan in York Group 3 on 2025 reappearance

races.race_name: Fitzdares Chelmer Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Fitzella
runners.spotlight: Likeable front-runner who made up into a useful juvenile, winning Group 3 Princess Margaret Stakes at Ascot and signing off with a good fifth in Newmarket's Cheveley Park Stakes; shaped encouragingly when fifth of 12 in Nell Gwyn Stakes back at Newmarket (7f) on her recent return, leading long way; this drop back to 6f promises to suit, so she's a big player.
runners.comment: Ascot Group 3 winner; fifth in Nell Gwyn at Newmarket on return; big player back at 6f

runners.name: Zooming
runners.spotlight: Promising filly who has improved with each of her three runs; ready winner of a 7f Newmarket novice in November and reappeared with a good fifth of 14 in the Fred Darling Stakes at Newbury (7f) two weeks ago; drops back in trip but capable of better still and firmly in the picture.
runners.comment: Good fifth in Fred Darling at Newbury on return; back in trip but capable of better

runners.name: Hassaleh
runners.spotlight: Built on debut promise when comfortably landing the odds in 7f maiden at Newmarket 16 days ago; this is much tougher but this Night Of Thunder filly looks to have a fair bit more to offer; very much one to consider.
runners.comment: Comfortably landed odds in 7f maiden at Newmarket 16 days ago; has fair bit more to offer

runners.name: Argentine Tango
runners.spotlight: Scored three times at 2yrs (including in 6f Listed event at Newmarket in June) and also runner-up in the Molecomb here; returned with a good fourth of 13 in Group 3 at Chantilly (5.5f, good) 18 days ago so must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Second in Molecomb here; back with good fourth in Chantilly Group 3; enters calculations

runners.name: Princess Petrol
runners.spotlight: Stylish winner of 6f Ascot novice in July and bettered that form when runner-up in the Sweet Solera at Newmarket (7f) and fourth in a Longchamp Group 2 (1m) in the autumn; hooded for her reappearance but she beat only one home in Fred Darling Stakes at Newbury; headgear quickly dispensed with.
runners.comment: Sweet Solera runner-up at Newmarket at 2yrs; beat one in Fred Darling at Newbury on return

runners.name: Flowerhead
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful 5f winner at 2yrs; not disgraced after six months off when a close eighth of 14 in 5f Listed race at Bath last month but this requires more.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when eighth in 5f Listed race at Bath last month but this requires more

runners.name: Soul Love
runners.spotlight: Starman filly who went 2-2 when impressively landing 6f Newcastle novice in March; only ninth of 13 in Group 3 at Chantilly 18 days ago though so has plenty to find on form.
runners.comment: Landed Newcastle novice but ninth in Group 3 at Chantilly since; plenty to find on form

runners.name: Hollywood Treasure
runners.spotlight: Landed 5f maiden (for Raphael Freire) last spring before showing much improved form to follow up for her new yard in 5f Newbury Listed event in August; only seventh of ten in Group 3 at Longchamp (5f) final run and faces no easy task on her return.
runners.comment: Landed Newbury Listed event in August but seventh in Group 3 at Longchamp final run

runners.name: Starfinch
runners.spotlight: Last on her debut (for Jessica Harrington) at the Curragh in August but she comfortably landed a 6f Southwell maiden in January before improving again when a close sixth of eight in 7f AW Listed event at Lingfield the following month; this demands another sizeable step forward though.
runners.comment: Won Southwell maiden; improved when sixth in 7f AW Listed event at Lingfield; more needed
runners.owner: Mrs Frank Barry & Mrs Luke Barry

races.race_name: Fitzdares Conqueror Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Blue Bolt
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and progressive filly who completed a hat-trick in a Listed race at Sandown (1m, good to firm) last July; held in second in her final two runs last season but the first was a Group 3 and latest was in the Group 1 Sun Chariot Stakes at Newmarket; sets the standard on this big drop back in class and she's a strong contender on her return.
runners.comment: Listed winner who was runner-up in a Group 1 in her final 3yo run; strong claims on return

runners.name: Shes Perfect
runners.spotlight: Won a Haydock maiden (7f, good to firm) as a 2yo; all three runs last season were in France and she suffered a heartbreaking demotion for connections when placed second in the French 1,000 Guineas at Longchamp (1m, good); well held at Chantilly in her final run last term but that was the French Oaks and she may have had stamina issues over an extended 1m2f; returns after 11 months off but she's still lightly raced and needs a close look on this drop back in trip and class.
runners.comment: Demoted to second in French 1,000 Guineas last season; respected back in trip on return

runners.name: Kon Tiki
runners.spotlight: Made it 3-3 when landing a Listed race at York (1m, good to firm) last May but she found things tough in the Group 1 Coronation Stakes at Ascot (never-dangerous seventh) the following month; has been on the sidelines since but she's still unexposed and could resume her progress back in calmer waters on her return.
runners.comment: Lost unbeaten record in a Group 1 last June but she's still unexposed; respected on return

runners.name: Never Let Go
runners.spotlight: Won the Sandringham Stakes at Royal Ascot last summer; runner-up in Listed events in two of her three runs since but she chased home a rival with a BHA rating of 94 at Kempton last month and this is a much tougher test; down the list.
runners.comment: Runner-up in Listed race at Kempton latest but this is a much tougher test; down the list

runners.name: Arisaig
runners.spotlight: Talented mare who ran well in Listed and Group events last season and was runner-up in a Class 2 handicap at Newmarket (1m, good) on her reappearance last month; should go well again but she has a bit to find on these figures and her last win was in July 2024.
runners.comment: Reliable mare who should go well again back in a Listed event but her last win was in 2024

runners.name: Betty Clover
runners.spotlight: Won a Listed race at York (5f, good) as a 2yo but she's 0-12 since and has had some major blips along the way; reappeared with fair fifth in Listed event at Kempton (1m) in March but Never Let Go was ahead of her in that race and the market leaders were well below form; only beaten a nose in the corresponding race 12 months ago but even that will need bettering today.
runners.comment: Fair effort on her Kempton reappearance but this looks a tough task on the figures

races.race_name: HKJC World Pool Palace House Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Beckford's Folly
runners.spotlight: Has been described as a challenge by connections but he's a talented one and the drop to 5f suited when beating a smart rival (later runner-up at the Breeders' Cup) in a Group 3 over C&D (good; won a novice on good to firm) in October; nothing special on his recent return here but he was eased when his winning chance had gone and the return to 5f can suit; much respected.
runners.comment: Drop to 5f suited when beating good rival in C&D Group 3 as 2yo; respected on 2nd run back

runners.name: Frost At Dawn
runners.spotlight: A record of two wins from 16 turf starts in no way does justice to the Group 1 form she's capable of, such as a close second in the King Charles III at Royal Ascot and a third to Asfoora in the Nunthorpe at York (both 5f, good to firm); the likely fast ground is a blessing and has good claims if on song.
runners.comment: Two terrific runs in Group 1s at 5f in 2025; likes fast ground; form claims if on song

runners.name: Asfoora
runners.spotlight: Superb Australian 7yo who has had a career to be proud of, with multiple wins in home country and Europe, laying down the law in the Group 1 King Charles III at Royal Ascot in 2024 and adding further top-level wins in the Nunthorpe (good to firm) at York and the Abbaye (very soft) at Longchamp in 2025; has stayed in Britain over the winter but connections expect her to improve for the run and that the stiff uphill finish will test her.
runners.comment: Superb Australian 7yo; three Group 1 wins in Europe but expected to improve for the run

runners.name: Jm Jungle
runners.spotlight: Below best from a high draw in the Group 1 Abbaye on final 2025 start; thoroughly reliable previously and he richly deserved his Group 2 win at Goodwood (5f, good to soft; effective on good to firm) last August; versatile track-wise but has never run at Newmarket so there is a query to answer.
runners.comment: Thoroughly reliable; richly deserved Group 2 win (5f) last August; having first course run
runners.owner: MPS Racing Ltd & The Ayrshire Tradesmen

runners.name: Shagraan
runners.spotlight: In action between May and October last year for Michael Appleby, finding a form spurt late on with a close second to First Instinct in a Group 3 at Newbury sandwiched by Listed 5f wins at Beverley and Ascot (good to firm and soft); more needed back with former trainer but may yet do better.
runners.comment: Form spurt later in 2025 with Listed 5f wins (good to firm/soft); may yet progress again

runners.name: Quinault
runners.spotlight: Front-runner whose 12 wins include a Group 3 at York (7f, good) last June; he was an excellent third of 19 in the Group 1 Champions Sprint at Ascot (6f) in October; has since run up to his best in a 5f AW race at Newcastle in January and it will be interesting to see how he gets on back at this trip on turf with his regular hood discarded; ran creditably in 6f Group 3 at Newmarket last time.
runners.comment: Front-runner; usually seen at 6f-7f but useful 5f run on AW in January; regular hood off

runners.name: First Instinct
runners.spotlight: Down the field from a poor draw in the Group 1 Abbaye at Longchamp in October but had previously confirmed herself an improving 3yo when beating Shagraan in a Group 3 at Newbury (5f, good to soft; acts well on good); has hopes of doing better as a 4yo but this is a tough start to the year.
runners.comment: Improved to win Group 3 (5f, good to soft) in September; this is a tough return

runners.name: Rumstar
runners.spotlight: Acts on flat tracks but he comes into his own when tackling a stiff test at 5f, as when winning this race last year, taking C&D record to 2-2, and roaring home to win a Group 3 at Sandown (good to firm) in July; cheekpieces worn in October are discarded; yard in form; not to be underestimated.
runners.comment: Suited by tracks with stiff finishes and won this last year; not to be underestimated

runners.name: Night Raider
runners.spotlight: Often leads; he's pure quality on the AW (4-4) but has had trouble sustaining his effort on turf, even allowing for being first home on his side in a Group 2 at Goodwood in August; gelded since last seen but whether he'll appreciate this stiff finish is questionable.
runners.comment: Front-runs; pure quality on AW (4-4) but had trouble sustaining effort on turf

runners.name: Ain't Nobody
runners.spotlight: Listed winner at Royal Ascot as a 2yo; out of luck since but he showed what can be done on a good day when chasing home Asfoora in the Group 1 Nunthorpe at York (good to firm; 100-1) last August; no approach to that form since, however, and although Ryan Moore is booked, the absence of cheekpieces raises a query.
runners.comment: Winless since 2yo days; fine 2nd to Asfoora at York last August (first-time cheekpieces)

runners.name: Washington Heights
runners.spotlight: Can lead; won 6f Group 3 here in 2024 but a beaten favourite in this race last year and sole success in 2025 came in a Listed York race (5f, good to firm); below best on recent comeback and a win looks unlikely.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner at 6f here in 2024; sole success since came in Listed company; win unlikely

runners.name: Miss Attitude
runners.spotlight: She divided Washington Heights and Jm Jungle in a York Listed race (5f, good to firm) last July and later won at that level in France but, even allowing for having a fitness edge, she has to raise her game to win today.
runners.comment: Won 5f Listed race in France as 5yo; no fitness queries but has to raise her game here

runners.name: Town And Country
runners.spotlight: An improver last May/June, giving First Instinct a fright in a Cork Listed race then winning at Sandown (both about 5f; good and good to firm), but she wasn't the same filly after; has plenty to find this time.
runners.comment: An improver last May/June but wasn't the same filly after; plenty to find on these terms

runners.name: Five Ways
runners.spotlight: All races at 6f, improving on front-running novice (good to firm) and AW Group 3 wins when beaten less than 3l in the Group 1 Middle Park on this course in September; from a yard that's much respected with sprinters but this still asks a lot on the drop to 5f.
runners.comment: All runs at 6f; two front-running wins before 4th in Group 1 here in September; tall order

races.race_name: Betfred 2000 Guineas Stakes (Group 1) (No Geldings)

runners.name: Gstaad
runners.spotlight: Proved rock-solid as a 2yo, readily winning the Coventry at Royal Ascot (6f) then posting form figures of 2221 at Group/Grade 1 level, including in the Dewhurst at Newmarket (7f) when doing well considering he was free to post and in the race; ended the campaign by overcoming a wide trip in the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf at Del Mar (1m, firm; won with something to spare); supplemented (effectively re-added) into this at a cost of £30,000 having been removed in late March due to a clerical error; leading contender in his bid to end a 2,000 Guineas drought for Aidan O'Brien, who has landed this Classic a record ten times but not since 2019.
runners.comment: Rock-solid as a 2yo; ran well in the Dewhurst prior to Breeders' Cup win; leading claims
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Distant Storm
runners.spotlight: Aptly bred, being by Night Of Thunder and out of a mare by another 2,000 Guineas winner, namely George Washington; 1,900,000euros breeze-up purchase last spring and duly made a big splash in his 2yo races, aside from when pulling too hard in the Acomb; showed smart from at Newmarket (7f, good/good to firm) in the autumn, impressing in the Tattersalls Stakes (Group 3) then running well (one place behind Gstaad) in the Dewhurst; yard has landed this prize three times in the last four years; big player.
runners.comment: Impressive in the Tattersalls Stakes prior to running well in the Dewhurst; big player

runners.name: Oxagon
runners.spotlight: Very useful colt but possibly needs to dominate, having gained both wins from the front, latest when taking well to first-time cheekpieces and comfortably beating Avicenna by 2l in the Craven Stakes over C&D (good; Group 3) on seasonal debut; by no means certain to have his own way in this bigger field and he's held by a couple of today's opponents on Dewhurst running; otherwise may have more to offer in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Very useful but possibly needs to dominate; took well to cheekpieces in the Craven

runners.name: King's Trail
runners.spotlight: Son of Sea The Stars, whose stellar 3yo campaign included a success in the 2,000 Guineas; two from two, both outings over 1m on Kempton AW, last time looking value for more than the 1l winning margin in a conditions race that Notable Speech (from same yard) won in 2024 before following up in this contest; deserted by William Buick but the stable landed this Classic with the second string, ridden by James Doyle, in 2022; interesting contender who brings significant potential.
runners.comment: Similar type to his yard's winner of this race in 2024; brings big potential; interesting

runners.name: Power Blue
runners.spotlight: Juvenile campaign over sprint distances culminated in Group 1 win at the Curragh (good); held by Gstaad on Coventry running; respectable second to Thesecretadversary, who received 3lb, in 7f Group 3 at Leopardstown on reappearance but doesn't give the strong impression he'll benefit from going up further in trip; indeed, he seems more of a 6f Commonwealth Cup type.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner over 6f as a 2yo; not a guaranteed stayer in this first attempt at 1m

runners.name: Thesecretadversary
runners.spotlight: Irish colt who has built up a solid record that includes a good effort in Canadian Grade 1 on final 2yo start (sole 1m attempt); won going away from Power Blue, who conceded 3lb, in Group 3 at Leopardstown (7f, good to yielding) on reappearance; could go well but several others bring stronger claims in terms of class and/or potential.
runners.comment: Irish colt who has built up a solid record; won going away in 7f Group 3 on reappearance
runners.owner: Cayton Park Stud & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Bow Echo
runners.spotlight: Son of 2,000 Guineas winner Night Of Thunder and from the family of Dubawi; three from three last term, all starts over 1m, driven out for 1l success in the Royal Lodge at Newmarket (good to firm; Group 2) the final time; that wasn't necessarily a star performance but he keeps getting the job done and commands major respect, being a progressive colt who has a perfect record and has had a "faultless spring" according to his trainer; furthermore, he's already proven over C&D.
runners.comment: Already proven over C&D, having won the Royal Lodge to take record to 3-3; much respected
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Needle Match
runners.spotlight: Similar to Into The Sky in having raced at Newbury on both starts; did just enough to win 2yo maiden (6.5f, soft) then ran well, about 2l fourth to Alparslan, in the Greenham (7f, good); the way he shaped last time suggests additional improvement is plausible upped further in trip; by 2,000 Guineas winner Night Of Thunder; more interesting than bare ratings suggest.
runners.comment: Ran well in the Greenham; further improvement is plausible upped to 1m; not dismissed

runners.name: Avicenna
runners.spotlight: Two from two last year, both starts over 7f at Doncaster and getting up on the line in Listed grade the second time; finished nicely for 2l second to Oxagon, who dictated the pace, in the Craven over C&D (good) on reappearance having again been held up; open to further progress and could pick up some pieces in this big-field scenario, assuming the gallop is suitably strong.
runners.comment: Finished nicely for second in the Craven; could pick up some pieces if the pace is strong

runners.name: Alparslan
runners.spotlight: Reversed Dewhurst placings with Zavateri, an absentee from this race due to a setback, in the Greenham at Newbury (7f, good; Group 3) to take his record to 3-4; however, needs further progress, having finished behind several more of these in the Dewhurst, and is far from sure to get another easy lead (dictated the pace last time).
runners.comment: Had the run of things in the Greenham to take record to 3-4; another easy lead is unlikely

runners.name: Venetian Prince
runners.spotlight: Has finished behind some of today's rivals on two occasions, latest in the Craven when admittedly running well on bare figures; gained sole success (from five attempts) at novice level and looks well short of the required standard in this field; first-time headgear needs to make a difference.
runners.comment: Held by some of today's rivals on two pieces of form; first-time headgear needs to help

runners.name: Padraig Dawn
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; AW form this year comprises a win at Southwell (7f) and neck second, doing well having come from difficult position, at Newcastle (1m Listed); promising colt for first-season trainer Charlie Pike but faces a much stiffer task on turf debut; supplemented at a cost of £30,000.
runners.comment: Promising colt for his first-season trainer but faces a difficult task on turf debut

runners.name: Lord Britain
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mainly on AW, most recently 5l fourth (ran well but never dangerous) to King's Trail at Kempton on seasonal debut; bottom of this pack on the figures and trailed home last in the Royal Lodge on sole turf attempt; this outsider has plenty to prove and plenty to find.
runners.comment: Bottom of this pack on bare figures; finished last in the Royal Lodge on sole turf start

runners.name: Billecart
runners.spotlight: Has plenty to find in this field and needs to settle better to boot, having raced too freely in Listed event last time when bidding for a Newcastle (1m, AW) double; behind Distant Storm on sole turf attempt; unlikely to emulate his sire Night Of Thunder, who landed this prize in 2014.
runners.comment: Has plenty to find in this field and needs to settle better than last time

runners.name: Into The Sky
runners.spotlight: Showed useful form over 6.5f and 6f at Newbury (good/good to soft) last season, winning a maiden by sizeable margin then clear second in the Mill Reef (Group 2; hung left; collared near the finish); good prospect for Jim Boyle this term but doesn't appear to be crying out for 1m.
runners.comment: Mill Reef runner-up; good prospect for Jim Boyle but doesn't seem to be crying out for 1m
runners.owner: Mrs D Tabor, Dr P Wilson & R Stanbridge

races.race_name: Derby City Distaff Stakes Presented By Ford (Grade 1) (Fillies & Mares) (Dirt)

runners.name: Splendora
runners.spotlight: High-class performer at 7f/1m, winner of five consecutive races, including Grade 1 victories in Breeders' Cup Filly & Mare Sprint (Del Mar in November, by 5l) and Beholder Mile (Santa Anita in March, by nearly 6l while coasting home); has tons of pace but happy to accept a lead; hard to beat.
runners.comment: Five straight wins include easy Grade 1 scores at Del Mar and Santa Anita; hard to beat

runners.name: Haulin Ice
