runners.spotlight: Superbly placed by trainer Saffie Joseph to win 12 of 21 career starts; five victories in 2025 included Grade 3s at Aqueduct (6.5f Vagrancy) in May and Gulfstream (7f Princess Rooney) in September; has picked up black-type event and Listed race at Oaklawn this year; speedy but sees out 7f.
runners.comment: Has won 12 of 21 starts, including two G3s and two lesser races in 2026; speedy
runners.owner: C2 Racing Stable LLC, WSS Racing & Agave Racing Stable

runners.name: R Disaster
runners.spotlight: Has won over 7f but best when dominating over shorter, as in Grade 2 Gallant Bloom at Aqueduct last September (6.5f, by three and a half lengths) and Grade 3 Hurricane Bertie at Gulfstream in March (6.5f, by just over 5l); faded late over 7f when 6l third in Grade 1 Madison at Keeneland last time.
runners.comment: Easy Grade 2 and Grade 3 winner who struggles to see out 7f in the very best company

runners.name: Ways And Means
runners.spotlight: Has won half of ten starts around this trip; very smart form to win Grade 2 Bed O' Roses Stakes at Saratoga in June on final start of 2025, pressing strong pace and drawing nearly 8l clear in fast time; beaten on both starts at Churchill Downs and back from 11-month break.
runners.comment: Five career wins, notably impressive 8l win in Grade 2 at Saratoga; 11-month break a worry

runners.name: Usha
runners.spotlight: Progressive form around this distance in 2025, winning three out of four including Grade 1 La Brea at Santa Anita by just over 5l from Formula Rossa; tactically versatile (has won making running and when dropped out) and can go well fresh; first start at Churchill Downs; more to come from her.
runners.comment: More to come after progressive 4yo season culminating in 5l win in Grade 1 at Santa Anita
runners.owner: Michael E Pegram, Karl Watson & Paul Weitman

runners.name: Autumn Evening
runners.spotlight: Trained by Todd Pletcher in 2025, her only win by head in allowance race at Gulfstream in January; ran poorly there on turf that March and not seen again; nothing to suggest she is a match for these, but Irad Ortiz is an eye-catching booking; first start at Churchill Downs.
runners.comment: Trained by Todd Pletcher in 2025, winning an allowance; stiff task after 14-month layoff

races.race_name: Twin Spires Turf Sprint Stakes Presented By Accenture (Grade 2) (Turf)

runners.name: Bear River
runners.spotlight: Finished ninth of 12 in an allowance Grade 2 at Keeneland last month; boasts winning form over C&D; one to consider despite having more on his plate and he has won in classy company before.
runners.comment: Likely player as peak figure in the past year is the best in the field.

runners.name: Yellow Card
runners.spotlight: On the premises when runner-up of 12 in an allowance Grade 2 at Keeneland behind My Boy Prince last month; has chalked up two wins from his last six; has won here; must go on the shortlist despite moving up in class.
runners.comment: This represents a step up in class but one to watch out for.

runners.name: Joe Shiesty
runners.spotlight: 1 1/2l behind the winner, My Boy Prince, at Keeneland when getting across to lead early from a wide stall last month in an allowance Grade 2; managed three wins in his last seven outings; has scored over C&D.
runners.comment: Not disgraced four weeks ago; managed three wins in his last seven outings.
runners.owner: Foster Family Racing, Lonnie Reynolds Et Al

runners.name: My Boy Prince
runners.spotlight: Had several of these behind in an allowance Grade 2 at Keeneland last month, getting the job done by just over 1l; proven that he knows how to get his head in front (seeking a tenth career win) and out to complete a hat-trick; can be on the premises despite this step up in class and jockey does well from inside draws here.
runners.comment: This represents a step up in class but should not be far away.

runners.name: Litigation
runners.spotlight: Somewhat disappointing when finishing fourth (3-1) in an allowance Grade 2 (Keeneland) last month, a race won by the reopposing My Boy Prince; brings winning C&D form to the table; has chalked up four wins from his last seven; has a reasonable shout as has some big wins on the CV and he can handle the step up in class.
runners.comment: Could have a say despite moving up in class; a bit below expectations four weeks ago.

runners.name: Wendelssohn
runners.spotlight: Finished last of seven at Oaklawn Park last month in a handicap Grade 3; has managed five wins from 19 career runs.
runners.comment: Last of seven last month; career record shows his ability to win (wins-runs record of 5-19).

runners.name: Its Bourbon Thirty
runners.spotlight: Finished eighth but wasn't beaten too far (just under 4l) in an allowance Grade 2 (Keeneland) last month; has chalked up three wins from his last seven; a winner over C&D.
runners.comment: Didn't run too badly in eighth last month; brings winning C&D form to the table.

runners.name: Mondogetsbuckets
runners.spotlight: 1 1/2l third to the reopposing My Boy Prince in an allowance Grade 2 (Keeneland) four weeks ago; normally pretty consistent and career wins-runs record of 4-13.
runners.comment: Respectable third four weeks ago; string of consistent efforts of late.

runners.name: Full Disclosure
runners.spotlight: Admirable career strike-rate (eight from 32 to date); was held in fifth in an allowance race on first outing for new trainer (Keeneland) 14 days ago.
runners.comment: Beaten 6l 14 days ago; career record shows his ability to win (wins-runs record of 8-32).

runners.name: Possiblemente
runners.spotlight: Bidding for hat-trick and has achieved nine wins from 30 career runs; came out on top by 1 1/2l over 6f at Turfway Park (allowance optional claimer) 51 days ago.
runners.comment: On target 51 days ago; has achieved nine wins from 30 career runs.

races.race_name: Longines Churchill Distaff Turf Mile Stakes (Grade 2) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Pin Up Betty
runners.spotlight: Specialist here with four wins from seven visits and producing consistent form; not far away, finishing fifth in the allowance Grade 1 Jenny Wiley Stakes (Keeneland) last month; this represents an easier task than her previous race; should go close if replicating her best form and possible she'll have come on from most recent start; was due to run at Churchill Downs Friday.
runners.comment: This represents a step up in class but should not be far away.

runners.name: Italian Soiree
runners.spotlight: Proven that she knows how to get her head in front (seeking a fourth career win); won an allowance optional claimer by just under 2l (Laurel Park) on debut for this handler three weeks ago; up in class to allowance company; covers an extra 2 1/2f here.
runners.comment: On target three weeks ago; career wins-runs record of 3-11.
runners.owner: Hit The Bid Racing Stable & CMNWLTH

runners.name: Movin' On Up
runners.spotlight: Has won at this track; didn't run badly when 22-1 third at Keeneland 20 days ago in a Grade 2; ought to be competitive.
runners.comment: One to take seriously; did well to finish third last month.

runners.name: Classic Q
runners.spotlight: C&D has proven suitable before; went down by three-quarters of a length in a Grade 3 at Gulfstream Park when ahead of Movin' On Up in February; up in class to allowance company.
runners.comment: Beaten narrowly in February; can boast winning form over this C&D.
runners.owner: Gary Barber, Blue Crevalle Racing & Eclipse T'Bred Partners

runners.name: Sweet Rebecca
runners.spotlight: Narrow scorer in an allowance listed (Gulfstream Park) five weeks ago; out to complete a hat-trick; more on her plate this time but ought to be in the mix.
runners.comment: Should give it a bold bid despite this step up in class.

runners.name: Portfolio Duration
runners.spotlight: Good winner in an allowance race (Tampa Bay Downs) five weeks ago; attempts to land a hat-trick and has made a pleasing start to her career with two wins from three starts; moves up into an allowance race.
runners.comment: Decisive winner 35 days ago; winner of two of her three runs to date.

runners.name: Maggie Go
runners.spotlight: Turned in a solid effort in defeat 35 days ago, when third in an allowance listed (Gulfstream Park), behind the reopposing Sweet Rebecca; seems to have stepped up on previous form the last twice; moves up into an allowance race.
runners.comment: Solid effort to finish third 35 days ago; shown recent signs of improvement.

runners.name: Vina Arana
runners.spotlight: Creditable fourth at Keeneland over 1m 1/2f (allowance race) three weeks ago; brings winning C&D form to the table; remains far from exposed; moves up into an allowance race.
runners.comment: Not disgraced last month; not had much racing for her age.
runners.owner: Sterling Stables LLC & MHM Stables LLC

runners.name: Temptable
runners.spotlight: Finished seventh but wasn't beaten too far (just over 3l) at Del Mar six months ago in a Grade 3; has earned plenty of prize money overseas, on target three from six starts.
runners.comment: Finished seventh six months ago; a money spinner overseas, winning three times from six runs.

races.race_name: Pat Day Mile Stakes Presented By SAP (Grade 2) (Dirt)

runners.name: Crude Velocity
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten after two career runs and bidding for a hat-trick; comfortable winner at Santa Anita last month in an allowance optional claimer; not discounted despite having more on his plate.
runners.comment: This represents a step up in class but a possible player.

runners.name: Englishman
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten after two career runs and nicely bred (by Maxfield); had something to spare at the finish when winning six weeks ago (Fair Grounds) in an allowance optional claimer; should not be far away as from low numbers over this C&D his jockey has an impressive record and he can handle the step up in class.
runners.comment: Can be in the thick of things despite being up in class.

runners.name: Trouble Calling
runners.spotlight: Gained narrow success in an allowance listed at Keeneland (muddy track) last month; bids to pull off a hat-trick and shown recent signs of improvement; this looks tougher but should prove competitive.
runners.comment: This represents a step up in class but looks a contender.

runners.name: Crown The Buckeye
runners.spotlight: Didn't start well (problems in the gate) at Aqueduct when beaten 1l to finish second in the Gotham Stakes in February.
runners.comment: Placed in the Gotham Stakes two months ago; career record of 2-6.
runners.owner: Paradise Farms Corp, David Staudacher & Hooties Racing LLC

runners.name: Secured Freedom
runners.spotlight: Related to Stakes winner Hold Tight and has a nice pedigree (by Practical Joke); not beaten too far (3 1/2l) in the San Felipe Stakes (Santa Anita) two months ago.
runners.comment: Not far away in Santa Anita two months ago.
runners.owner: Pierre J Amestoy, Leslie A Amestoy & Roger K Beasley

runners.name: Creole Chrome
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in the Blue Grass Stakes ahead of Great White when fourth at Keeneland 28 days ago; three from five in his career so far; this might be easier; steps down in trip.
runners.comment: Well held in the Blue Grass Stakes last month; winner of three of his five career runs.

runners.name: Stop The Car
runners.spotlight: A winner over this C&D; winner of two of his four career runs; was fourth of 12, beaten 8l, just ahead of Baytown Dreamer at Turfway Park 42 days ago in a Grade 3; now tackles shorter distance.
runners.comment: Held in fourth six weeks ago; brings winning C&D form to the table.

runners.name: Great White
runners.spotlight: On target twice in his last four outings; well beaten in the Blue Grass Stakes at Keeneland behind Creole Chrome, last month; this represents an easier task than his previous race; should be thereabouts; also engaged in race 12 at Churchill Downs.
runners.comment: He has a shout; on target twice in his last four outings.

runners.name: Baytown Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Finished tailed off at Turfway Park over 1m1f (Grade 3) six weeks ago; now tackles shorter distance.
runners.comment: Finished tailed off at Turfway Park over 1m1f (Grade 3) six weeks ago; now tackles shorter distance.
runners.owner: Baytown Partnership & Win The Day Racing LLC

runners.name: One More Freud
runners.spotlight: Scored narrowly in a black type in August at Monmouth Park upped to this trip; on a hat-trick after last win and unbeaten through two career starts; returns from a layoff; was entered at Oaklawn Park Friday.
runners.comment: Tight-margin winner in August; aiming to complete a hat-trick.

runners.name: Work
runners.spotlight: Behind Lockstocknpharoah in a black type when beaten 21l 49 days ago at Colonial Downs upped to 1m1f; has taken a step forward the last twice; dropped in distance here.
runners.comment: Held when eighth 49 days ago; seems to be improving based on last two runs.

runners.name: Lockstocknpharoah
runners.spotlight: Not beaten too far when fifth at Colonial Downs 49 days ago in a black type; two from three in his career so far; first run on dirt; drops in trip.
runners.comment: Not disgraced in fifth 49 days ago; two from three in his career so far.
runners.owner: Let It Ride Equine Holdings III LLC

races.race_name: American Turf Stakes (Grade 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Stark Contrast
runners.spotlight: Form choice on second to Gstaad in Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf at Del Mar last November, beaten just under 1l after short of room; landed odds cosily in Listed race at Santa Anita in January (1m) before second to Fulleffort on Tapeta in 1m1f Grade 3 Jeff Ruby Steaks at Turfway, no extra last 1f; must go well back on the grass and at best distance.
runners.comment: Second in G1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf and in G3 on Tapeta at Turfway; must go well

runners.name: Remember Mamba
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten in three starts, scoring with much more in hand than bare half-length margin in Grade 3 Transylvania at Keeneland (8.5f) last time, set plenty to do and well on top when surging to lead close home (from Honey Dutch and Vasy); an exciting prospect but has a wide draw.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in three on turf, including Grade 3 last time when did well to win; good prospect

runners.name: Honey Dutch
runners.spotlight: Consistent type placed on all six starts on turf; sole win in maiden at Kentucky Downs on first occasion last September but best effort when close second on latest one in Grade 3 Transylvania at Keeneland (8.5f), duelling with eventual fourth Street Beast throughout, picked off close home; sound claims of another place.
runners.comment: Placed all six starts on turf; best effort last time when close second in Grade 3; claims

runners.name: Vasy
runners.spotlight: Very useful form to beat Honey Dutch by a neck in Listed race at Gulfstream in February; beaten under 1l by Remember Mamba and Honey Dutch in Grade 3 Transylvania (8.5f) at Keeneland last time; wide draw to overcome here but surely enters each-way calculations.
runners.comment: Another good effort when under 1l third in Grade 3 at Keeneland; solid each-way claims

runners.name: Blackout Time
runners.spotlight: Very useful form on dirt, best effort under 3l second to Ted Noffey in Grade 1 Breeders' Futurity (8.5f) at Keeneland last October; disappointing at Oaklawn this year in Grade 2 Rebel and Grade 1 Arkansas Derby, both times finding little; turf switch looks speculative, but dangerous if adapting.
runners.comment: Best effort on dirt second in Grade 1 last year, but two poor efforts recently; tries turf
runners.owner: BR Thoroughbreds Inc, Lance Gasaway & Magdalena Racing

runners.name: Street Beast
runners.spotlight: Very useful efforts around 1m on turf (fourth to Gstaad in Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf) and on Tapeta; creditable fourth, making running at steady pace until no extra last 1f, to Remember Mamba in Grade 3 Transylvania at Keeneland; faces more pace pressure here but has place chance.
runners.comment: Fourth in Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf and in Grade 3 at Keeneland last time; place chance

runners.name: Final Score
runners.spotlight: Smart juvenile over 8.5f on turf last year when winner of maiden at Saratoga, Grade 3 With Anticipation there (travelled strongly, by 4.5l) and Grade 2 Bourbon at Keeneland (favourite, cosily by just over 1l); has wide draw and seven-month absence to overcome but highly talented.
runners.comment: Smart form to win Grade 3 and Grade 2 last year; wide draw and lengthy absence to overcome

runners.name: Alpyland
runners.spotlight: Progressive form to win four races at up to 1m on turf since last September, including Listed race at Gulfstream Park in January and black-type event at Tampa Bay in March; needs to find more to beat these but has handy tactical speed and going the right way; stays 8.5f.
runners.comment: Winner of four turf races, nothing at this level but going the right way

runners.name: Blinging It Back
runners.spotlight: Sole win in maiden at Kentucky Downs last September; good third in black-type race at Gulfstream last time, finishing well; does not seem on a par with best of these.
runners.comment: Third in black-type race last time, best effort, but needs a good deal more here

runners.name: Black Hornet
runners.spotlight: Successful on both starts on turf in maiden and black-type event at Fair Grounds in February; forced wide and never able to challenge when beaten 18l in Grade 3 Jeff Ruby Steaks on Tapeta at Turfway last time; could pick up threads of improvement back on this surface but plenty to find.
runners.comment: Won two minor turf races at Fair Grounds in February then well beaten in Grade 3 on Tapeta

runners.name: Steel Imperium
runners.spotlight: All three starts on Tapeta surface at Turfway Park, winning a maiden in February (6f) and half-length second in black-type event (8.5f), short of room and forced wide; plenty to find on form but shapes as if capable of better.
runners.comment: All three starts on Tapeta at Turfway, second in black-type event latest; much more needed

runners.name: Let's Be Frank
runners.spotlight: Never-nearer 1l third in Grade 2 Pilgrim at Aqueduct last October, but well beaten in Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf; first start for Michael McCarthy when second in maiden at Santa Anita (1m) last time, , again running on belatedly; could reach minor place.
runners.comment: 3rd in Grade 2 last year but again beaten in maiden last time; late run could grab a place

runners.name: Greenwich Village
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of two black-type events at Santa Anita (6.5f and 1m) since switched to turf; on both occasions he did well to win, in tight early on latest occasion and running on wide to get up close home; shapes as if capable of a lot better and must not be overlooked.
runners.comment: Did well to win both black-type races since switched to turf; open to lots of progress
runners.owner: SF Racing LLC, Starlight Racing Et Al

runners.name: Thousandsticks
runners.spotlight: Completed Gulfstream Park hat-trick on turf in Listed race in January (8.5f), responding well to pressure; below that form when around 3l fourth to Vasy in Listed race there latest, not finding much; needs to bounce back and two-month break could help.
runners.comment: Completed hat-trick in Listed at Gulfstream in January; below form since but now fresher

races.race_name: Churchill Downs Stakes Presented By Ford (Grade 1) (Dirt)

runners.name: Banishing
runners.spotlight: Generally races over a bit further nowadays, as when well beaten in Grade 1 company in Pegasus World Cup at Gulfstream and Saudi Cup at Riyadh, and when winning Grade 2 Godolphin Mile at Meydan last time by just over 2l; dual 7f winner earlier in career; tends to find Grade 1 company a challenge.
runners.comment: Won Grade 2 Godolphin Mile at Meydan last time; finds Grade 1 company a tough challenge
runners.owner: Sharaf Mohammed Al Hariri / David Jacobson & Lawrence Roman

runners.name: Knightsbridge
runners.spotlight: Dominated Grade 3 series for older milers at Gulfstream last three starts, impressive in Mr Prospector (7f) in December, Fred W. Hooper in January and Gulfstream Park Mile (by 11l) in February; very impressive in all three, recording outstanding time performances off strong fractions; venue switch could cause concern but 10l winner of maiden over C&D on debut; has not had much racing for his age but comes here fresh.
runners.comment: Dominating winner of three fast-time Grade 3s at Gulfstream, latest by 11l; C&D winner

runners.name: Imagination
runners.spotlight: Speedy sort, gradually dropped in trip and found form over 6f last autumn; winner of Grade 2 Santa Anita Sprint Championship and second in Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Sprint (beaten around 2l by Bentornato); landed good prize by under 1l in Riyadh Dirt Sprint, again at 6f, this February; sound claims.
runners.comment: Grade 2 winner and Breeders' Cup Sprint second who won in Saudi Arabia; sound claims
runners.owner: SF Racing LLC, Starlight Racing Et Al

runners.name: Crazy Mason
runners.spotlight: Smart performer at 7f/1m, placed in Graded stakes on five of last six starts, including Grade 1 Forego (7f) at Saratoga last August; ran on well from poor position when second in Grade 3 Commonwealth at Keeneland (7f) last month; will not stay with these early but could reach a place.
runners.comment: Smart performer over 7f/1m; ran creditably in Grade 3 at Keeneland last time; place chance

runners.name: Point Dume
runners.spotlight: Game front-runner who has won all three starts in 2026 in handicap and Listed race at Laurel then Grade 2 Carter at Aqueduct; touched off hot favourite Book'em Danno on latest start under excellent ride from Edwin Gonzalez; suited by 7f, but this group of horses is a fair bit tougher.
runners.comment: Narrow Grade 2 winner at Aqueduct last time, gamely responding to good ride; stiff task

runners.name: Cornucopian
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten at up to 7f, impressive winner of maiden at Oaklawn and allowance at Santa Anita; landed odds in Grade 3 San Carlos at latter venue over this trip, taking time to get on top but scoring by nearly 3l; open to further progress and looks a very smart performer in the making.
runners.comment: Unbeaten in three up to 7f, notably Grade 3 at Santa Anita latest; very smart prospect
runners.owner: SF Racing LLC, Starlight Racing Et Al

runners.name: T O Elvis
runners.spotlight: Very smart Japanese-trained sprinter; winner of last four races (three by at least 4l) including Grade 3 Capella Stakes at Nakayama last December by 5l; that form stacks up well with these, according to Racing Post Ratings, but best form at 6f.
runners.comment: Very smart Japanese-trained sprinter, won Grade 3 by 5l; best form at 6f

runners.name: Macho Music
runners.spotlight: Best effort to win Grade 2 Pat Day Mile here last year by 3l, making all; disappointing on all three starts since, including Listed race at Gulfstream in March (beaten 5l, finding little); lots of ability if recapturing his best form on second start here.
runners.comment: Grade 2 winner here (1m) in 2025 but disappointing since; return to this track offers hope
runners.owner: Mark Fletcher Taylor, Rohan Crichton & Daniel L Walters

runners.name: Disruptor
runners.spotlight: Progressive form in five starts, including two impressive wins over 7f; second favourite, found plenty under pressure to edge out Disco Time by 1l, pair clear, in 8.5f Grade 3 Challenger at Tampa Bay latest; solid, smart prospect with pace who should handle drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Third win when outbattling Disco Time by 1l in Grade 3 last time (8.5f); two wins at 7f
runners.owner: Repole Stable, Spendthrift Farm LLC Et Al

runners.name: Disco Time
runners.spotlight: Developed into very smart performer while unbeaten on first five starts, winning 1m Listed race at Aqueduct (1m; travelled strongly and well clear after 7f) in November by 10l; favourite, pulled too hard (sweating) when eighth in Grade 1 Pegasus World Cup at Gulfstream (1m1f) and again weak at the finish when 1l second to Disruptor in Grade 3 Challenger at Tampa Bay (8.5f); likely to prove suited by dropping to 7f.
runners.comment: Smart winner of five, notably Listed by 10l; not stay 1m1f and 8.5f since; 7f will suit

runners.name: Hall Of Fame
runners.spotlight: Progressed into very useful sort over further than this 7f last year, winning Grade 3 Mineshaft at Fair Grounds (8.5f) in February; creditable efforts in frame after, but having first start for ten months today; second in maiden over this trip here on 2024 debut.
runners.comment: 8.5f Grade 3 winner and has run since over further than 7f; first start for ten months

races.race_name: Old Forester Bourbon Turf Classic Stakes (Grade 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Rhetorical
runners.spotlight: Very smart miler, winner of Grade 1 Turf Mile at Keeneland and 2l fourth to Notable Speech in Breeders' Cup Mile at Del Mar; solid reappearance when close third in Grade 1 Maker's Mark Mile at Keeneland; very much the one to beat on form, but has plenty of speed and not certain to be suited by further than 8.5f.
runners.comment: Grade 1 winner and close fourth in Breeders' Cup Mile in 2025; some doubts at 1m1f
runners.owner: Gary Barber, Cheyenne Stable LLC & Wachtel Stable

runners.name: Corruption
runners.spotlight: Smart front-runner, reeled in final 1f by Far Bridge in Grade 3 Pan American at Gulfstream Park (1m4f) last time; seems suited by further than this distance, though landed odds in C&D allowance race last May; needs to find bit more.
runners.comment: Smart front-runner hauled in by Far Bridge at Gulfstream (1m4f) last time; needs more

runners.name: Program Trading
runners.spotlight: Formerly high class at around 1m1f, winning three Grade 1s including this race in 2024; defeated on all six starts since and well below his best when 2l third in Grade 2 at Fair Grounds last time; blinkers could spark a revival in theory, but Flavien Prat rides Asbury Park.
runners.comment: High class at best around 1m1f, landing 2024 renewal; below that since and now blinkered

runners.name: Mercante
runners.spotlight: Second in this in 2025, coming off Grade 3 Kentucky Cup Classic win; runner-up in same warm-up race at Turfway Park this year, beaten just under 1l by Willy D's; in decent form again but needs to step up on balance of form.
runners.comment: Second in this last year; creditable effort on reappearance; needs to find more on balance

runners.name: Make Me King
runners.spotlight: Smart form at around this distance, including in Middle East on last four starts; won at Doha in Qatar in February before creditable fourth to impressive Ombudsman in Group 1 Dubai Turf; experienced, tactically versatile gelding but needs to career-best effort to prevail.
runners.comment: Smart form in Middle East last four starts, fourth in Group 1 Dubai Turf; claims

runners.name: Gold Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Tough sort with 11 wins from 31 starts; winner of Grade 2 events at Santa Anita and Del Mar in 2025; again came from off pace to win Grade 3 San Luis Rey at former venue in March, responding gamely; others have better form but he should not be far away.
runners.comment: Veteran with two Grade 2 wins in 2025; added Grade 3 gamely last time; minor place claims
runners.owner: Agave Racing Stable, Little Red Feather Racing Et Al

runners.name: Dashman
runners.spotlight: Narrow Grade 3 winner at Gulfstream Park last January but lightly raced and has struggled in three starts since; beaten 5l by Far Bridge (Corruption second) in Grade 3 on same course latest start; would be surprise winner.
runners.comment: Grade 3 winner last year but lightly raced and struggled in three starts since; outsider

runners.name: Test Score
runners.spotlight: Progressive form around this trip, particularly since blinkered (won two of last three starts) at end of 2025; excellent ride (established good early position) from Manny Franco to win Grade 1 Pegasus World Cup Invitational at Gulfstream in January (1m1f) by neck from stablemate One Stripe; goes very well fresh and three-month absence is no concern.
runners.comment: Won Pegasus World Cup Turf by a neck under good ride; should give another good account

runners.name: Astronomer
runners.spotlight: Talented on his day but inconsistent and finds winning a challenge; just three wins from 25 starts and has lost 19 consecutively; closed belatedly when fourth in Pegasus World Cup Turf at Gulfstream in January but below that form since; frustrating.
runners.comment: Fourth in Pegasus World Cup Turf in January but has now lost 19 consecutively; frustrating

runners.name: Asbury Park
runners.spotlight: Improved last autumn and decisive 2l winner of Grade 2 Jockey Club Derby at Aqueduct (1m3f) in October, settled in behind, waiting for a run then powering to front and much the best; well-bred type (by Frankel) open to lots more improvement; seems to have enough speed to drop back to 1m1f on reappearance; Flavien Prat rides him rather than Program Trading.
runners.comment: Impressive Grade 2 winner last October; Flavien Prat looks significant booking on comeback
runners.owner: Peter M Brant & St Elias Stable

races.race_name: Kentucky Derby Presented By Woodford Reserve (Grade 1) (Dirt)

runners.name: Further Ado
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin winner at Keeneland of maiden last October (by 20l) and Grade 1 Blue Grass Stakes (1m1f; by 11l from depleted field in very fast time) in April; also Grade 2 scorer here in between in Kentucky Jockey Club Stakes; too soon to say he is best at Keeneland but faces a much more competitive field from wide draw; strong claims, if running back to his last performance with ground loss likely and facing sterner fractions.
runners.comment: Easy winner of depleted Grade 1 Blue Grass at Keeneland; also Grade 2 winner here; claims

runners.name: Commandment
runners.spotlight: Solid, tactically versatile, professional racehorse with a ton of talent; answered every call to win last four starts, notably at Gulfstream in Grade 2 Fountain of Youth Stakes (beat Chief Wallabee by a neck) in February and 1m1f Grade 1 Florida Derby (slow pace, got up close home to beat The Puma by a nose) in March; will stay 1m2f and no rival seems more capable of handling the turmoil of this big field (also course winner in maiden); big chance.
runners.comment: Completed four-timer in Grade 1 Florida Derby; hugely likeable and will stay; big chance

runners.name: Renegade
runners.spotlight: Strong-finishing 4l winner of Sam F. Davis Stakes at Tampa Bay in February and Grade 1 Arkansas Derby (1m1f) at Oaklawn following month; clocked notably fast final split-time on second occasion, despite coming wide, powering away from Silent Tactic most impressively and still full of running hitting the line; will have to overcome traffic problems from difficult inside draw, but bound to relish 1m2f and holds strong claims.
runners.comment: Impressive in Tampa Listed race and Grade 1 Arkansas Derby at Oaklawn; must go well
runners.owner: Robert Low, Lawana L Low & Repole Stable

runners.name: The Puma
runners.spotlight: Likeable, enthusiastic runner capable of change of gear; won Grade 3 Tampa Bay Derby from below-par Further Ado, but best effort when beaten a nose by Commandment in 1m1f Grade 1 Florida Derby, looking likely winner when hitting the front in straight; doubts at 1m2f and needs playing late; thriving.
runners.comment: Best effort nose second to Commandment in 1m1f Grade 1 Florida Derby; chance if staying
runners.owner: OGMA Investments LLC, JR Ranch Et Al

runners.name: Wonder Dean
runners.spotlight: Strong-staying Japanese runner with very useful form back home; fourth in valuable Saudi Derby then won 9.5f Grade 2 UAE Derby by two and a half lengths from profligate Six Speed, getting up close home as winner paid for his exuberance; will get behind but could make frame late with clear run.
runners.comment: Got up close home to win 9.5f Grade 2 UAE Derby by nearly 3l; frame chance with clear run

runners.name: Chief Wallabee
runners.spotlight: Strong form at Gulfstream Park when placed behind Commandment in Grade 2 Fountain Of Youth Stakes in February (second, beaten a neck) and Grade 1 Florida Derby (third, beaten half a length) following month; gave impression he failed to go through with effort last time and masterful trainer now adds blinkers; could easily step forward and must be considered.
runners.comment: Blinkered after two close-up placed efforts behind Commandment at Gulfstream; bear in mind

runners.name: So Happy
runners.spotlight: Progressive form to win three out of four in Southern California; answered stamina doubts at 1m1f to land Grade 1 Santa Anita Derby decisively from tiring Potente, more patiently ridden than on previous start; shapes as though he should stay 1m2f, though son of sprinter Runhappy; claims on form.
runners.comment: Winner of 1m1f Grade 1 Santa Anita Derby, more patiently ridden; chance if staying 1m2f
runners.owner: Norman Stables Llc And Saints Or Sinners

runners.name: Incredibolt
runners.spotlight: Progressive juvenile in 2025, winning Grade 3 Street Sense here (same race won by last year's Derby winner Sovereignty); much better effort since (ran poorly in Grade 3 at Gulfstream in January) when 4l winner of black-type Virginia Derby (1m1f) at Colonial Downs last time, powering clear; should stay 1m2f and looks type to be suited by big field and strong pace here; interesting.
runners.comment: Grade 3 winner here in 2025; bounced back from poor run to win black-type by 4l; enticing

runners.name: Intrepido
runners.spotlight: Grade 1 win in American Pharoah Stakes at Santa Anita last October looks flattering on basis of three starts since; found little on latest start when 10l fourth to So Happy in Grade 1 Santa Anita Derby; not certain to stay 1m2f and lots of questions to answer.
runners.comment: Longshot on basis of three lesser runs since Grade 1 score last October
runners.owner: Dutch Girl Holdings LLC & Irving Ventures LLC

runners.name: Litmus Test
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent type who may struggle to reproduce Grade 2 win and fourth in Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile last year; blinkers off, disappointing when distant seventh to Renegade in Grade 1 Arkansas Derby last time; looks sure to be part of the early pace here with headgear reinstated.
runners.comment: Grade 2 winner last year but disappointing this year; looks sure to be part of pace early
runners.owner: SF Racing LLC, Starlight Racing Et Al

runners.name: Danon Bourbon
runners.spotlight: Exciting Japanese runner; has dominated competition at home with clearcut wins on dirt all three starts, notably in a valuable stakes race (1m1f) at Nakayama in March; has a long, raking stride and always in control of his races; has a massive challenge to overcome here but not to be passed over lightly.
runners.comment: Unbeaten Japanese dirt runner, will relish 1m2f test; not to be discounted on US debut

runners.name: Potente
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of maiden and Grade 2 San Felipe Stakes at Santa Anita this year, powering ahead in latter event and looking sure to stay beyond 8.5f (well clear after the line); set contested pace and tired when beaten nearly 3l by So Happy as favourite for Grade 1 Santa Anita Derby; open to improvement after just three starts and looks likely to be ridden much more patiently here; not to be written off.
runners.comment: Smart form after three starts, Grade 2 winner and Grade 1 second at Santa Anita; potential

runners.name: Pavlovian
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive in ten starts, winning maiden at Santa Anita in 2025 and Listed Sunland Park Derby (by a nose) in February; another good effort when head second to Emerging Market in Grade 2 Louisiana Derby at Fair Grounds; likeable but others have more potential.
runners.comment: Best effort when beaten a head by Emerging Market in Grade 2 at Fair Grounds; needs more

runners.name: Albus
runners.spotlight: Impressive Tampa Bay maiden winner, showing long stride and excellent balance; took advantage of pace collapsing up front when getting up late on in Grade 2 Wood Memorial at Aqueduct latest start; will stay 1m2f but likely to get well back early on; needs a blistering pace up front.
runners.comment: Late-running Grade 2 Wood Memorial winner at Aqueduct; needs another pace collapse

runners.name: Emerging Market
runners.spotlight: Remarkable time performance to win maiden at Tampa Bay in February, then tenacious, despite inexperience, when following up in Grade 2 Louisiana Derby (9.5f) at Fair Grounds, beating Pavlovian by a head; the only winner of this with just two previous starts was Leonatus in 1883, but this colt is a special talent; chance to buck history depends on clear run from wide draw.
runners.comment: Impressed in maiden; game in Grade 2 win at Fair Grounds but last twice-raced winner 1883

runners.name: Six Speed
runners.spotlight: Headstrong front-runner, winner of three races up to 1m at Meydan around turn of year, including Grade 3; did far too much when picked off late by decisive winner Wonder Dean in Grade 2 UAE Derby (9.5f) latest start; needs to handle preliminaries and settle better, or else will set torrid pace and tire.
runners.comment: Grade 3 winner, Grade 2 second at Meydan but markedly headstrong and unlikely to stay 1m2f
runners.owner: Brunetti Dugan Stables, Black Type Thoroughbreds et al

runners.name: Golden Tempo
runners.spotlight: Narrow Grade 3 Lecomte scorer in January; creditable third in Grade 2s at Fair Grounds since for Risen Star Stakes (beaten 6l) in February and Louisiana Derby (beaten 1l by Emerging Market) in March; gets well behind early; still lightly raced but needs pace-collapse to aid winning chance.
runners.comment: Grade 3 scorer, third in two Grade 3s since, all at Fair Grounds; gets behind; stiff task

runners.name: Great White
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of Listed race on Tapeta at Turfway Park, then well-beaten fifth behind Further Ado in Grade 1 Blue Grass at Keeneland; has to prove he acts on dirt, let alone can run with this type of rival.
runners.comment: Narrow winner of Grade 3 on Tapeta, well beaten on dirt debut; very stiff task

runners.name: Ocelli
runners.spotlight: Maiden after six starts and closed against tired rivals when third in Grade 2 Wood Memorial latest; tough race in which to break his maiden.
runners.comment: Third in Grade 2 Wood Memorial, closing belatedly; still a maiden
runners.owner: Ashley Durr, Anthony Tate & Front Page Equestrian LLC

runners.name: Robusta
runners.spotlight: Reserve; best effort when beaten a head by Potente in Grade 2 San Felipe at Santa Anita in March; well beaten there in Grade 1 Santa Anita Derby; useful type but needs to find a lot more, if getting a run.
runners.comment: Reserve; beaten head in Grade 2 but well beaten in Grade 1, both Santa Anita; tough task

runners.name: Corona De Oro
runners.spotlight: Reserve; impressive winner of maiden at Fair Grounds in March, then good third in Grade 3 Lexington at Keeneland following month; very tall order if getting his opportunity.
runners.comment: Reserve; third in Grade 3 at Keeneland, but nothing to suggest he is good enough yet
runners.owner: On Our Own Stable LLC, Commonwealth Stable Et Al

races.race_name: Betfred Handicap (Heritage Handicap)

runners.name: Double Rush
runners.spotlight: Promising for Charles Hills as a 3yo, winning a C&D handicap at the Craven meeting before a good third from a tricky draw at York; ended 2025 with a low-key run at Ascot but, having been switched to Andrew Balding's stable, he created a huge impression here 17 days ago, supported as though defeat was out of the question and winning accordingly; 8lb well in and looks a Group horse in waiting; obvious chance for the stable that won this prize in 2021 and 2024.
runners.comment: 2-2 over C&D; left rivals trailing when heavily backed on stable debut here; 8lb well in

runners.name: Sir Les Patterson
runners.spotlight: Enough evidence to point to him being a superior performer on AW but that is reflected in a handicap mark that is 9lb lower on grass; that said, it is still 11lb higher than when twice beaten at the Curragh last September and he'll need to leave his turf form well behind if he is to win.
runners.comment: Seemingly much happier on AW; previous turf efforts need leaving well behind him

runners.name: Addison Grey
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo; hugely encouraging seasonal return at Newbury (6f, good; gelded during his absence) a fortnight ago, missing a beat at the start but moving through to lead inside the final furlong before being picked off close home; 2lb well in and this flashy grey appeals as the type to win a major sprint handicap this year; solid contender.
runners.comment: Unexposed and progressive; pleasing Newbury return 15 days ago; 2lb well in; solid chance

runners.name: Coul Angel
runners.spotlight: Upped his game following a gelding operation last summer, winning over C&D in September before making giant strides on AW; returned from a break to do best of the rest behind runaway winner Double Rush over C&D 17 days ago; could have a nice prize in him this year but the 5lb turnaround with Double Rush may be insufficient.
runners.comment: Making good progress but he has nearly 5l to find with Double Rush on latest C&D run

runners.name: El Bodon
runners.spotlight: 0-11 on turf with his best efforts coming on slower than good; back up in the weights after a productive AW campaign and he has enough to prove back on turf, all the more so after his tame effort at Newcastle on Good Friday.
runners.comment: Two AW wins this winter but poor latest; 0-11 on turf with best form on slower ground

runners.name: Brian
runners.spotlight: Patchy overall record; didn't enjoy the smoothest of passages at Newbury two weeks ago but he was beaten over 5l at the finish; others are stronger.
runners.comment: Inconsistent and latest Newbury eighth didn't suggest he was the answer today

runners.name: Invictus Gold
runners.spotlight: Goes well at Newmarket; third to Double Rush at last season's Craven meeting (2lb better off today) before an impressive win in the 3yo handicap on this card; another promising run on his reappearance 17 days ago when finishing third, just over 5l behind Double Rush; has bigger days ahead of him but he may be battling for another minor placing.
runners.comment: Good course form, including a win on this card last year; held twice by Double Rush though

runners.name: Indian Run
runners.spotlight: 6lb lower than for his close sixth in this race 12 months ago; he had the benefit of a pipe-opener before that race, though, and he comes here after a 264-day layoff; should be ending his losing run soon but perhaps not in this field.
runners.comment: Ran well for 6th in this race last year and 6lb lower today; 264-day absence to overcome

runners.name: Apollo One
runners.spotlight: Stable star who runs in this race for the fourth time, recording best results when second in 2024 and third in 2023; went without a win in 2025 but the ability was still intact, second in the Portland (5.6f, good) and an Ascot Group 3 (6f, soft); faced some stiff tasks in the Middle East this year but his second in a Listed race at Jebel Ali (5f, dirt) was promising, finishing with a flourish for second; fair mark back on grass; should run well.
runners.comment: Placed in this race in 2023 & 2024; returns to Britain/turf on a fair mark; should go well

races.race_name: Betfred Suffolk Stakes (Heritage Handicap)

runners.name: Mister Winston
runners.spotlight: Down the field when 33-1 in C&D Cambridgeshire last September, before being gelded; however, he was reliable and progressive earlier last season and reappeared with a front-running 1m win here (favourite) 18 days ago; has 5lb penalty (same as his revised mark) but needs plenty of respect.
runners.comment: Front-running 1m win here (favourite) 18 days ago; has 5lb penalty (same as revised mark)

runners.name: The Lost King
runners.spotlight: Progressive over 7f-8.6f on AW this year, on last two starts winning the Lincoln Trial at Wolverhampton and close third at the AW Championships at Newcastle; turf mark has been raised in tandem, which leaves him with marked improvement to find in this sphere, but he needs a second look.
runners.comment: Progressive over 7f-8.6f on AW this year; turf mark has been raised in tandem

runners.name: Bullet Point
runners.spotlight: Can front-run; 1m wins here last April and in 17-runner race at the York Ebor meeting in August, with his two seconds at Ascot in between including the Royal Hunt Cup; last two outings leave him with a bit to prove, as they were 1m1f, but it's not too hard to excuse them and he is a strong contender.
runners.comment: Big performances in notable 1m h'caps last April-August; strong contender despite 1m1f

runners.name: Alcarath
runners.spotlight: Ran away with a novice at Epsom in September on third start, making all; 11-8 favourite for handicap debut at Redcar (again 1m2f on good) in November but went in snatches and finished only fourth; retains clear potential but he's nothing like as battle-hardened as most of these runners.
runners.comment: Retains clear potential but he's nothing like as battle-hardened as most of these runners

runners.name: Fifth Column
runners.spotlight: 1m winner at Newmarket and York last summer but failed to shine on last two outings, when co-second favourite for the C&D Cambridgeshire on first occasion; only mid-division when 13-2 in the Spring Cup at Newbury two weeks ago but not beaten far and the draw looked a problem; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Mid-division in the Spring Cup at Newbury but not beaten far and the draw looked a problem

runners.name: Al Arbeed
runners.spotlight: Unraced on firmer than good; won debut on Southwell AW; close fourth on handicap debut there (1m) last October is markedly better than what he's done in three starts since, but he made late gains over 7f at Musselburgh on latest start to prompt some hope given today's step up in trip.
runners.comment: Late gains over 7f at Musselburgh latest prompts some hope given today's step up in trip

runners.name: Erzindjan
runners.spotlight: Easily first home in his near-side group when strong-finishing fourth of 23 in the Cambridgeshire over C&D (good to firm) in September; just 1lb higher today and that wasn't his only solid performance in a good handicap last term, so he could be a major each-way player.
runners.comment: Easily first home in his group when 4th of 23 in the Cambridgeshire over C&D in September

runners.name: Tycoon
runners.spotlight: Windsor maiden winner for the Gosdens last April, followed by a 1m3f Listed third and big-money sale; only mid-division in his four handicaps since, 20-1 off this mark behind Mr Winston here (1m) on reappearance; back up by a furlong but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Mid-division in his four handicaps, 20-1 off this mark behind Mr Winston here on return

runners.name: Botanical
runners.spotlight: He wasn't at his very best last year but took a step back in the right direction when front-running fourth of 21 off this mark in the Lincoln at Doncaster five weeks ago on first run for new yard; no stamina worries but there is a concern about good to firm ground.
runners.comment: Front-running 4th in Lincoln at Doncaster for new yard; concern about good to firm ground
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Thunder Wonder
runners.spotlight: Won at Musselburgh last April and June; failed to make a serious impact since, albeit fourth of 12 back at Musselburgh three weeks ago; non-runner at Redcar on Thursday.
runners.comment: Won at Musselburgh last April and June; non-runner at Redcar on Thursday

runners.name: Marhaba Ghaiyyath
runners.spotlight: Went close at Glorious Goodwood (1m2f, good) last July but well beaten in C&D Cambridgeshire in September; failed to settle and suffered interference on return, doing pretty well in the circumstances, but disappointing favourite at Pontefract 11 days ago; connections turn to cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Disappointing favourite at Pontefract 11 days ago; connections turn to cheekpieces

runners.name: Astro King
runners.spotlight: Excelled in top handicaps at York and Newmarket in 2023, including with a C&D win in the Cambridgeshire (good to firm; 3lb lower today); however, he only once threatened to hit those heights in 2024 and, after wind surgery, missed all of 2025; that leaves him with a 658-day absence to overcome but his handicap mark has been slashed.
runners.comment: Has a 658-day absence to overcome but his handicap mark has been slashed

races.race_name: Sky Bet Thirsk Hunt Cup Handicap

runners.name: Blue Rc
runners.spotlight: 7f course winner on good to firm last summer and has done little but progress since, winning over 1m at Southwell on New Year's Day; excellent second of 14 at Newcastle (1m again) since, with reopposing Flight Plan and Apiarist in behind; should continue the good work back on turf; top of the list.
runners.comment: Progressive course winner; excellent second in strong AW handicap on Good Friday; player

runners.name: Sea Force
runners.spotlight: Won a Nottingham maiden (1m, good) and a Yarmouth handicap (1m1f, soft) last year; respectable sixth (Mirsky third) of 12 in 1m1f Musselburgh handicap on reappearance three weeks ago; wandered around a little under pressure there and now refitted with the cheekpieces he wore once last season; could resume his progression and much respected from a handy inside stall.
runners.comment: Two wins in 2025; fair sixth on return and could resume progression in refitted headgear

runners.name: Flight Plan
runners.spotlight: Failed to build on a decent fourth at Lingfield (1m2f) in February when behind Blue Rc at Newcastle (1m) on Good Friday but the fact he made all in this race off 8lb lower last year means a revival can't be discounted, particularly as he's well drawn to attack again; tongue-tied first time.
runners.comment: 8lb lower than when winning this last year; revival isn't ruled out in new tongue-tie
runners.owner: Fine Clarets Racing, Tp Watson & C Wood

runners.name: City Of Poets
runners.spotlight: Has looked quirky on occasions but he produced a decidedly useful effort when coming with a strong run to get up late in 8.6f Wolverhampton handicap in November; matched that form when a close third there on reappearance six weeks ago; won a fast-ground novice on turf last year (also second here on debut); one of two solid contenders for James Tate.
runners.comment: Has odd quirk but done well on AW in recent starts; one of two solid contenders for yard

runners.name: Esherann
runners.spotlight: Pair of 1m wins on good ground in Ireland last summer, namely a Naas maiden (wore cheekpieces) and Curragh handicap (visored); low-mileage new recruit for Declan Carroll but he'll need to leave last month's Newmarket reappearance with a tongue-tie added (retained) well behind to play a leading role here.
runners.comment: Two wins in Ireland last year but well held on recent first run for Declan Carroll

runners.name: Apiarist
runners.spotlight: Three fine efforts on AW to start the year but his run of good form came to a halt when well behind Blue Rc at Newcastle over Easter; acts on turf but has a much better strike-rate on AW; drawn widest.
runners.comment: Started year in good form on AW but lesser run latest; others preferred back on turf

runners.name: A War Eagle
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who was having only his second start for this yard when clinging on over the extended 7f at Beverley (good to firm) nine days ago; only raised 3lb but this is a step up from that Class 4 and his draw isn't ideal for one who usually races close to the pace.
runners.comment: Only nudged up 3lb for recently Beverley success but this is a much stronger race

runners.name: Mirsky
runners.spotlight: Hit a hot spell at the beginning of last summer, winning over C&D (good to firm) before following up at the Epsom Derby meeting (8.5f, good to soft); creditable third (Sea Force sixth) of 12 on Musselburgh reappearance (1m1f, good) three weeks ago; likely to be competitive again.
runners.comment: C&D win last May; creditable third of 12 on reappearance; likely to be in the mix

runners.name: Bellarchi
runners.spotlight: Last four wins at Musselburgh, including two last month, latterly producing a career-best performance to see off ten rivals over 1m on good; also went close at this course last year; a further 4lb rise demands even more but can't rule out in her current mood.
runners.comment: In the form of life with two Musselburgh wins last month; can't discount in current mood

runners.name: Dashing Darcey
runners.spotlight: Just second outing for Geoff Harker when runner-up off 1lb higher at Haydock (1m, good) last May but never involved when dropped to 7f at York the following month and not seen since (gelded); this is a tough race to return in.
runners.comment: Second off 1lb higher last May but off since a lesser run the following month; gelded
runners.owner: C H McGie & Northumbria Leisure Ltd

runners.name: Accentuate
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a Windsor novice (1m, good) last June; far from disgraced despite only beating one home when fifth on his handicap debut at Ascot (good to firm) last July and perhaps unsuited by soft ground when well held there in September; sold for 23,000gns in November and gelded ahead of this return for a new stable; drawn wide and probably best watched this time.
runners.comment: Lightly raced new recruit for Tim Easterby but drawn wide and perhaps best watched

runners.name: Redarna
runners.spotlight: Sprung a 66-1 surprise in this on good to firm in 2022 (also fourth in 2024); added career win number 17, as an 11yo, at Ayr (1m, good to soft) last September but arrives with something to prove after finishing tailed off behind a couple of these on his Musselburgh reappearance.
runners.comment: Won this in 2022 (also fourth in 2024) but recent reappearance was poor

runners.name: Rhoscolyn
runners.spotlight: Last year's Golden Mile win at Goodwood (strong record there) shows he's still smart on his day; respectable return to action when fifth in good conditions race over 7f here three weeks ago but he's gained the vast majority of his ten career wins on slower ground than he's likely to encounter this afternoon.
runners.comment: Golden Mile winner last year; fair reappearance but ideally wants slower ground

runners.name: Al Mubhir
runners.spotlight: Smart at his best for William Haggas, winning a Listed race at Sandown (1m2f, good) in July 2024; campaigned solely in Australia in 2025 and well beaten on his last two starts; potentially well handicapped back in Britain for a new yard after wind surgery, but he carries obvious risks.
runners.comment: Smart at his best and back in Britain after spell in Australia; has had wind surgery

races.race_name: HKJC World Pool Palace House Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Beckford's Folly
runners.spotlight: Has been described as a challenge by connections but he's a talented one and the drop to 5f suited when beating a smart rival (later runner-up at the Breeders' Cup) in a Group 3 over C&D (good; won a novice on good to firm) in October; nothing special on his recent return here but he was eased when his winning chance had gone and the return to 5f can suit; much respected.
runners.comment: Drop to 5f suited when beating good rival in C&D Group 3 as 2yo; respected on 2nd run back

runners.name: Frost At Dawn
runners.spotlight: A record of two wins from 16 turf starts in no way does justice to the Group 1 form she's capable of, such as a close second in the King Charles III at Royal Ascot and a third to Asfoora in the Nunthorpe at York (both 5f, good to firm); the likely fast ground is a blessing and has good claims if on song.
runners.comment: Two terrific runs in Group 1s at 5f in 2025; likes fast ground; form claims if on song

runners.name: Asfoora
runners.spotlight: Superb Australian 7yo who has had a career to be proud of, with multiple wins in home country and Europe, laying down the law in the Group 1 King Charles III at Royal Ascot in 2024 and adding further top-level wins in the Nunthorpe (good to firm) at York and the Abbaye (very soft) at Longchamp in 2025; has stayed in Britain over the winter but connections expect her to improve for the run and that the stiff uphill finish will test her.
runners.comment: Superb Australian 7yo; three Group 1 wins in Europe but expected to improve for the run

runners.name: Jm Jungle
runners.spotlight: Below best from a high draw in the Group 1 Abbaye on final 2025 start; thoroughly reliable previously and he richly deserved his Group 2 win at Goodwood (5f, good to soft; effective on good to firm) last August; versatile track-wise but has never run at Newmarket so there is a query to answer.
runners.comment: Thoroughly reliable; richly deserved Group 2 win (5f) last August; having first course run
runners.owner: MPS Racing Ltd & The Ayrshire Tradesmen

runners.name: Shagraan
runners.spotlight: In action between May and October last year for Michael Appleby, finding a form spurt late on with a close second to First Instinct in a Group 3 at Newbury sandwiched by Listed 5f wins at Beverley and Ascot (good to firm and soft); more needed back with former trainer but may yet do better.
runners.comment: Form spurt later in 2025 with Listed 5f wins (good to firm/soft); may yet progress again

runners.name: Quinault
runners.spotlight: Front-runner whose 12 wins include a Group 3 at York (7f, good) last June; he was an excellent third of 19 in the Group 1 Champions Sprint at Ascot (6f) in October; has since run up to his best in a 5f AW race at Newcastle in January and it will be interesting to see how he gets on back at this trip on turf with his regular hood discarded; ran creditably in 6f Group 3 at Newmarket last time.
runners.comment: Front-runner; usually seen at 6f-7f but useful 5f run on AW in January; regular hood off

runners.name: First Instinct
runners.spotlight: Down the field from a poor draw in the Group 1 Abbaye at Longchamp in October but had previously confirmed herself an improving 3yo when beating Shagraan in a Group 3 at Newbury (5f, good to soft; acts well on good); has hopes of doing better as a 4yo but this is a tough start to the year.
runners.comment: Improved to win Group 3 (5f, good to soft) in September; this is a tough return

runners.name: Rumstar
runners.spotlight: Acts on flat tracks but he comes into his own when tackling a stiff test at 5f, as when winning this race last year, taking C&D record to 2-2, and roaring home to win a Group 3 at Sandown (good to firm) in July; cheekpieces worn in October are discarded; yard in form; not to be underestimated.
runners.comment: Suited by tracks with stiff finishes and won this last year; not to be underestimated

runners.name: Night Raider
runners.spotlight: Often leads; he's pure quality on the AW (4-4) but has had trouble sustaining his effort on turf, even allowing for being first home on his side in a Group 2 at Goodwood in August; gelded since last seen but whether he'll appreciate this stiff finish is questionable.
runners.comment: Front-runs; pure quality on AW (4-4) but had trouble sustaining effort on turf

runners.name: Ain't Nobody
runners.spotlight: Listed winner at Royal Ascot as a 2yo; out of luck since but he showed what can be done on a good day when chasing home Asfoora in the Group 1 Nunthorpe at York (good to firm; 100-1) last August; no approach to that form since, however, and although Ryan Moore is booked, the absence of cheekpieces raises a query.
runners.comment: Winless since 2yo days; fine 2nd to Asfoora at York last August (first-time cheekpieces)

runners.name: Washington Heights
runners.spotlight: Can lead; won 6f Group 3 here in 2024 but a beaten favourite in this race last year and sole success in 2025 came in a Listed York race (5f, good to firm); below best on recent comeback and a win looks unlikely.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner at 6f here in 2024; sole success since came in Listed company; win unlikely

runners.name: Miss Attitude
runners.spotlight: She divided Washington Heights and Jm Jungle in a York Listed race (5f, good to firm) last July and later won at that level in France but, even allowing for having a fitness edge, she has to raise her game to win today.
runners.comment: Won 5f Listed race in France as 5yo; no fitness queries but has to raise her game here

runners.name: Town And Country
runners.spotlight: An improver last May/June, giving First Instinct a fright in a Cork Listed race then winning at Sandown (both about 5f; good and good to firm), but she wasn't the same filly after; has plenty to find this time.
runners.comment: An improver last May/June but wasn't the same filly after; plenty to find on these terms

runners.name: Five Ways
runners.spotlight: All races at 6f, improving on front-running novice (good to firm) and AW Group 3 wins when beaten less than 3l in the Group 1 Middle Park on this course in September; from a yard that's much respected with sprinters but this still asks a lot on the drop to 5f.
runners.comment: All runs at 6f; two front-running wins before 4th in Group 1 here in September; tall order

races.race_name: Betfred 2000 Guineas Stakes (Group 1) (No Geldings)

runners.name: Gstaad
runners.spotlight: Proved rock-solid as a 2yo, readily winning the Coventry at Royal Ascot (6f) then posting form figures of 2221 at Group/Grade 1 level, including in the Dewhurst at Newmarket (7f) when doing well considering he was free to post and in the race; ended the campaign by overcoming a wide trip in the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf at Del Mar (1m, firm; won with something to spare); supplemented (effectively re-added) into this at a cost of £30,000 having been removed in late March due to a clerical error; leading contender in his bid to end a 2,000 Guineas drought for Aidan O'Brien, who has landed this Classic a record ten times but not since 2019.
runners.comment: Rock-solid as a 2yo; ran well in the Dewhurst prior to Breeders' Cup win; leading claims
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Distant Storm
runners.spotlight: Aptly bred, being by Night Of Thunder and out of a mare by another 2,000 Guineas winner, namely George Washington; 1,900,000euros breeze-up purchase last spring and duly made a big splash in his 2yo races, aside from when pulling too hard in the Acomb; showed smart from at Newmarket (7f, good/good to firm) in the autumn, impressing in the Tattersalls Stakes (Group 3) then running well (one place behind Gstaad) in the Dewhurst; yard has landed this prize three times in the last four years; big player.
runners.comment: Impressive in the Tattersalls Stakes prior to running well in the Dewhurst; big player

runners.name: Oxagon
runners.spotlight: Very useful colt but possibly needs to dominate, having gained both wins from the front, latest when taking well to first-time cheekpieces and comfortably beating Avicenna by 2l in the Craven Stakes over C&D (good; Group 3) on seasonal debut; by no means certain to have his own way in this bigger field and he's held by a couple of today's opponents on Dewhurst running; otherwise may have more to offer in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Very useful but possibly needs to dominate; took well to cheekpieces in the Craven

runners.name: King's Trail
runners.spotlight: Son of Sea The Stars, whose stellar 3yo campaign included a success in the 2,000 Guineas; two from two, both outings over 1m on Kempton AW, last time looking value for more than the 1l winning margin in a conditions race that Notable Speech (from same yard) won in 2024 before following up in this contest; deserted by William Buick but the stable landed this Classic with the second string, ridden by James Doyle, in 2022; interesting contender who brings significant potential.
runners.comment: Similar type to his yard's winner of this race in 2024; brings big potential; interesting

runners.name: Power Blue
runners.spotlight: Juvenile campaign over sprint distances culminated in Group 1 win at the Curragh (good); held by Gstaad on Coventry running; respectable second to Thesecretadversary, who received 3lb, in 7f Group 3 at Leopardstown on reappearance but doesn't give the strong impression he'll benefit from going up further in trip; indeed, he seems more of a 6f Commonwealth Cup type.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner over 6f as a 2yo; not a guaranteed stayer in this first attempt at 1m

runners.name: Thesecretadversary
runners.spotlight: Irish colt who has built up a solid record that includes a good effort in Canadian Grade 1 on final 2yo start (sole 1m attempt); won going away from Power Blue, who conceded 3lb, in Group 3 at Leopardstown (7f, good to yielding) on reappearance; could go well but several others bring stronger claims in terms of class and/or potential.
runners.comment: Irish colt who has built up a solid record; won going away in 7f Group 3 on reappearance
runners.owner: Cayton Park Stud & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Bow Echo
runners.spotlight: Son of 2,000 Guineas winner Night Of Thunder and from the family of Dubawi; three from three last term, all starts over 1m, driven out for 1l success in the Royal Lodge at Newmarket (good to firm; Group 2) the final time; that wasn't necessarily a star performance but he keeps getting the job done and commands major respect, being a progressive colt who has a perfect record and has had a "faultless spring" according to his trainer; furthermore, he's already proven over C&D.
runners.comment: Already proven over C&D, having won the Royal Lodge to take record to 3-3; much respected
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Needle Match
runners.spotlight: Similar to Into The Sky in having raced at Newbury on both starts; did just enough to win 2yo maiden (6.5f, soft) then ran well, about 2l fourth to Alparslan, in the Greenham (7f, good); the way he shaped last time suggests additional improvement is plausible upped further in trip; by 2,000 Guineas winner Night Of Thunder; more interesting than bare ratings suggest.
runners.comment: Ran well in the Greenham; further improvement is plausible upped to 1m; not dismissed

runners.name: Avicenna
runners.spotlight: Two from two last year, both starts over 7f at Doncaster and getting up on the line in Listed grade the second time; finished nicely for 2l second to Oxagon, who dictated the pace, in the Craven over C&D (good) on reappearance having again been held up; open to further progress and could pick up some pieces in this big-field scenario, assuming the gallop is suitably strong.
runners.comment: Finished nicely for second in the Craven; could pick up some pieces if the pace is strong

runners.name: Alparslan
runners.spotlight: Reversed Dewhurst placings with Zavateri, an absentee from this race due to a setback, in the Greenham at Newbury (7f, good; Group 3) to take his record to 3-4; however, needs further progress, having finished behind several more of these in the Dewhurst, and is far from sure to get another easy lead (dictated the pace last time).
runners.comment: Had the run of things in the Greenham to take record to 3-4; another easy lead is unlikely

runners.name: Venetian Prince
runners.spotlight: Has finished behind some of today's rivals on two occasions, latest in the Craven when admittedly running well on bare figures; gained sole success (from five attempts) at novice level and looks well short of the required standard in this field; first-time headgear needs to make a difference.
runners.comment: Held by some of today's rivals on two pieces of form; first-time headgear needs to help

runners.name: Padraig Dawn
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; AW form this year comprises a win at Southwell (7f) and neck second, doing well having come from difficult position, at Newcastle (1m Listed); promising colt for first-season trainer Charlie Pike but faces a much stiffer task on turf debut; supplemented at a cost of £30,000.
runners.comment: Promising colt for his first-season trainer but faces a difficult task on turf debut

runners.name: Lord Britain
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mainly on AW, most recently 5l fourth (ran well but never dangerous) to King's Trail at Kempton on seasonal debut; bottom of this pack on the figures and trailed home last in the Royal Lodge on sole turf attempt; this outsider has plenty to prove and plenty to find.
runners.comment: Bottom of this pack on bare figures; finished last in the Royal Lodge on sole turf start

runners.name: Billecart
runners.spotlight: Has plenty to find in this field and needs to settle better to boot, having raced too freely in Listed event last time when bidding for a Newcastle (1m, AW) double; behind Distant Storm on sole turf attempt; unlikely to emulate his sire Night Of Thunder, who landed this prize in 2014.
runners.comment: Has plenty to find in this field and needs to settle better than last time

runners.name: Into The Sky
runners.spotlight: Showed useful form over 6.5f and 6f at Newbury (good/good to soft) last season, winning a maiden by sizeable margin then clear second in the Mill Reef (Group 2; hung left; collared near the finish); good prospect for Jim Boyle this term but doesn't appear to be crying out for 1m.
runners.comment: Mill Reef runner-up; good prospect for Jim Boyle but doesn't seem to be crying out for 1m
runners.owner: Mrs D Tabor, Dr P Wilson & R Stanbridge

races.race_name: Turners Handicap

runners.name: Man Of Vision
runners.spotlight: Gave trouble in the stalls on his first two runs last year (gelded afterwards) but also showed a good level of ability; made no mistake in a 7f novice at Wolverhampton in December, running out the easy winner of a race that has been subsequently been franked; bred to carry on improving and the drop in trip shouldn't be an issue; leading claims.
runners.comment: Promise in first two starts before an easy AW win (all 7f); more to come now handicapping

runners.name: Sovereign Spell
runners.spotlight: First past the post at Windsor last summer but demoted on a technicality; 2-2 in 2026, winning a Wolverhampton maiden easily before gamely seeing off Advertised over C&D 16 days ago; 6lb rise could be enough to scupper the hat-trick bid.
runners.comment: 2-2 this year, latterly beating Advertised over C&D last month; 6lb rise may find him out

runners.name: Naval Light
runners.spotlight: 360,000gns breeze-up 2yo; still seeking a first win and he has been expensive to follow thus far; that said, his Goodwood second (6f, good) last summer reads well and when third at Doncaster five weeks ago (7f, good to soft) he travelled through the race like a smart horse; the combination of a drop back in trip and the addition of cheekpieces could see him step forward now handicapping.
runners.comment: Five-race maiden but not short of ability; headgear tried now back to 6f and handicapping

runners.name: Comical Point
runners.spotlight: 5f win on 2yo debut; came up short in Group 2 and Listed company afterwards but still displayed useful ability; sent off favourite for a conditions event at Wolverhampton (6f; tongue-tie added) on last month's return and finished a close third despite hanging to his right; getting back on a straight track should suit and this Commonwealth Cup entrant makes some appeal on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Highly tried at 2yrs following debut win; satisfactory return; respected on handicap debut

runners.name: Ten Carat Harry
runners.spotlight: Developed into a useful performer on AW this winter, reeling off a five-timer (6f) prior to a good third in Listed company (7f); solid efforts in defeat on his last two starts and too soon to pigeonhole him as an AW specialist given his two turf runs were on soft ground and early in his career; likely vulnerable off topweight all the same.
runners.comment: Made giant strides on AW; should be effective on turf too but now high in the weights

runners.name: Kesta
runners.spotlight: Ran 12 times as a 2yo, winning three times; the ground may have been too soft when down the field at Newbury on his final run last year but likely a few of these will have his measure.
runners.comment: 12 runs as a 2yo, winning three; into a deep race on his return and others appeal more

runners.name: Pilu
runners.spotlight: Won a Bath maiden (5.7f, good to firm) on debut and followed up in a Salisbury novice (6f, good to soft) four weeks later; last of four in a conditions race back at Salisbury in October; improvement essential now handicapping but it's not out of the question.
runners.comment: Won first two starts before lesser run in Salisbury conditions event; improvement needed

runners.name: Advertised
runners.spotlight: Won twice on AW last year but he took his form to a new level when fourth of 14 in a York sales race last October (7f, good); lumped up 11lb for that but showed there was no fluke about the performance by finishing second in a C&D handicap on his reappearance 16 days ago (behind Sovereign Spell); didn't look in love with the track on that occasion but he is on an upward path and remains unexposed as a sprinter; 4lb rise demands more though.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with a good run and his C&D 2nd last month was even better; 4lb higher today

runners.name: First Legion
runners.spotlight: Came to hand early last year, winning a York novice on debut before finishing second in the Listed National Stakes at Sandown; only sixth in the C&D handicap won by Sovereign Spell 16 days ago and he needs that run to have brought him on significantly.
runners.comment: Promising early 2yo but he has rather plateaued since; only midfield over C&D on return

runners.name: Angel Of Anfield
runners.spotlight: Promise behind useful opposition in his first three starts and his Windsor win in August (6f, good) saw him defeat one who has boosted the form subsequently; gelded before finishing second at Kempton on his handicap debut 206 days ago; 2lb higher for his reappearance but still of interest for last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: Rock-solid 2yo form; could progress further this year; stable won this race last year

runners.name: Front Line Fury
runners.spotlight: Two 6f wins last summer; not at his best at Chester when last seen but he was drawn wide and there were possible trip/ground excuses too (wore cheekpieces which are absent today); has undergone wind surgery over the winter; returns for a stable ticking along nicely; may have more to offer.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins last year; had wind surgery since last seen; yard in form; not fully exposed

runners.name: Rydale Frosty
runners.spotlight: 6f novice win at Windsor last July before appearing to improve when fourth in the Group 3 Molecomb at Glorious Goodwood (5f, good; form hasn't worked out at all); struggled thereafter and he makes his seasonal/handicap debut off a tough mark; has been gelded since last seen.
runners.comment: Handicapped on his Molecomb 4th and that form hasn't worked out; gelded since latest run

races.race_name: Oddschecker Ellen Chaloner Stakes (Listed Race) (Formerly The Kilvington Stakes) (Fillies & Mares)

runners.name: Flora Of Bermuda
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner in July 2024; campaigned mostly at Group 1 level since, making the frame on several occasions, most recently in the 2025 Sprint Cup at Haydock (6f, good) where she did well having met trouble; leading player back down in class.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner who has made the frame in several Group 1 events; leading contender

runners.name: Sky Majesty
runners.spotlight: Has come up short in Group 1 attempts, including last two appearances; record when racing below that level on turf is unblemished (5-5) and features Listed, Group 3 and Group 2 wins on British, French and Irish soil; strong claims back down in grade.
runners.comment: Record when racing below Group 1 level on turf is unblemished (5-5); strong claims

runners.name: Sayidah Dariyan
runners.spotlight: Had excuses (trip/draw) returned to Group 1 level in last two appearances; progressive otherwise and ran well in the Commonwealth Cup prior to Group 3 win at York (6f, good) last summer; may still have more to offer; major contender.
runners.comment: Best to forgive last two efforts; progressive otherwise and may still have more to offer

runners.name: Magic Basma
runners.spotlight: Ended last season in a consistent vein of form at Listed level, creditable third at Doncaster (6f) the final time; may continue to give her running but that won't be enough to belatedly double her tally; 0-9 since 2yo debut success.
runners.comment: Ended last season in a consistent vein of form but she's 0-9 since her debut success

runners.name: Rosy Affair
runners.spotlight: Record is 5-13; ended her 2025 campaign with an August double that comprised a Listed race at Pontefract (good to firm) and Group 3 at Deauville (soft), both over 6f; progressive and tactically versatile mare who warrants respect on reappearance.
runners.comment: Progressive; ended her 2025 campaign with an August double (Listed/Group 3); respected

runners.name: Hold A Dream
runners.spotlight: Novice winner as a 2yo; placed four times in Listed grade, most recently runner-up over C&D (good to firm) on final start in 2025; ran below market expectations at Bath on reappearance but consistent on the whole and may rebound.
runners.comment: Placed four times in Listed grade, including over C&D; below par on seasonal debut

runners.name: Paris Babe
runners.spotlight: Gained her French wins in maiden/handicap races; faded late over 7f on the AW finals card at Newcastle on British debut; may prove suited by this drop back in distance but the opposite is true of the step back up in class; stiff task.
runners.comment: Ex-French filly who faces a stiff task in this second British start

runners.name: Rhyme Dust
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage filly whose form wearing hood comprises a success in 6f novice event and two good efforts in Listed grade, third at Ayr when last seen (final outing for Mark Usher; remains with same owner); could go well in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Low-mileage filly who could go well in the retained hood; stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Celandine
runners.spotlight: Disappointing in similar event over C&D when last seen and is 0-5 since winning the Lowther as a 2yo; however, this seasonal debut may be the time to catch her; good third to Sayidah Dariyan in Group 3 at York (6f) on 2025 reappearance.
runners.comment: Seasonal debutante; good third to Sayidah Dariyan in York Group 3 on 2025 reappearance

races.race_name: Betfred Handicap (Heritage Handicap)

runners.name: Double Rush
runners.spotlight: Promising for Charles Hills as a 3yo, winning a C&D handicap at the Craven meeting before a good third from a tricky draw at York; ended 2025 with a low-key run at Ascot but, having been switched to Andrew Balding's stable, he created a huge impression here 17 days ago, supported as though defeat was out of the question and winning accordingly; 8lb well in and looks a Group horse in waiting; obvious chance for the stable that won this prize in 2021 and 2024.
runners.comment: 2-2 over C&D; left rivals trailing when heavily backed on stable debut here; 8lb well in

runners.name: Sir Les Patterson
