runners.spotlight: Enough evidence to point to him being a superior performer on AW but that is reflected in a handicap mark that is 9lb lower on grass; that said, it is still 11lb higher than when twice beaten at the Curragh last September and he'll need to leave his turf form well behind if he is to win.
runners.comment: Seemingly much happier on AW; previous turf efforts need leaving well behind him

runners.name: Addison Grey
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo; hugely encouraging seasonal return at Newbury (6f, good; gelded during his absence) a fortnight ago, missing a beat at the start but moving through to lead inside the final furlong before being picked off close home; 2lb well in and this flashy grey appeals as the type to win a major sprint handicap this year; solid contender.
runners.comment: Unexposed and progressive; pleasing Newbury return 15 days ago; 2lb well in; solid chance

runners.name: Coul Angel
runners.spotlight: Upped his game following a gelding operation last summer, winning over C&D in September before making giant strides on AW; returned from a break to do best of the rest behind runaway winner Double Rush over C&D 17 days ago; could have a nice prize in him this year but the 5lb turnaround with Double Rush may be insufficient.
runners.comment: Making good progress but he has nearly 5l to find with Double Rush on latest C&D run

runners.name: El Bodon
runners.spotlight: 0-11 on turf with his best efforts coming on slower than good; back up in the weights after a productive AW campaign and he has enough to prove back on turf, all the more so after his tame effort at Newcastle on Good Friday.
runners.comment: Two AW wins this winter but poor latest; 0-11 on turf with best form on slower ground

runners.name: Brian
runners.spotlight: Patchy overall record; didn't enjoy the smoothest of passages at Newbury two weeks ago but he was beaten over 5l at the finish; others are stronger.
runners.comment: Inconsistent and latest Newbury eighth didn't suggest he was the answer today

runners.name: Invictus Gold
runners.spotlight: Goes well at Newmarket; third to Double Rush at last season's Craven meeting (2lb better off today) before an impressive win in the 3yo handicap on this card; another promising run on his reappearance 17 days ago when finishing third, just over 5l behind Double Rush; has bigger days ahead of him but he may be battling for another minor placing.
runners.comment: Good course form, including a win on this card last year; held twice by Double Rush though

runners.name: Indian Run
runners.spotlight: 6lb lower than for his close sixth in this race 12 months ago; he had the benefit of a pipe-opener before that race, though, and he comes here after a 264-day layoff; should be ending his losing run soon but perhaps not in this field.
runners.comment: Ran well for 6th in this race last year and 6lb lower today; 264-day absence to overcome

runners.name: Apollo One
runners.spotlight: Stable star who runs in this race for the fourth time, recording best results when second in 2024 and third in 2023; went without a win in 2025 but the ability was still intact, second in the Portland (5.6f, good) and an Ascot Group 3 (6f, soft); faced some stiff tasks in the Middle East this year but his second in a Listed race at Jebel Ali (5f, dirt) was promising, finishing with a flourish for second; fair mark back on grass; should run well.
runners.comment: Placed in this race in 2023 & 2024; returns to Britain/turf on a fair mark; should go well

races.race_name: Betfred Suffolk Stakes (Heritage Handicap)

runners.name: Mister Winston
runners.spotlight: Down the field when 33-1 in C&D Cambridgeshire last September, before being gelded; however, he was reliable and progressive earlier last season and reappeared with a front-running 1m win here (favourite) 18 days ago; has 5lb penalty (same as his revised mark) but needs plenty of respect.
runners.comment: Front-running 1m win here (favourite) 18 days ago; has 5lb penalty (same as revised mark)

runners.name: The Lost King
runners.spotlight: Progressive over 7f-8.6f on AW this year, on last two starts winning the Lincoln Trial at Wolverhampton and close third at the AW Championships at Newcastle; turf mark has been raised in tandem, which leaves him with marked improvement to find in this sphere, but he needs a second look.
runners.comment: Progressive over 7f-8.6f on AW this year; turf mark has been raised in tandem

runners.name: Bullet Point
runners.spotlight: Can front-run; 1m wins here last April and in 17-runner race at the York Ebor meeting in August, with his two seconds at Ascot in between including the Royal Hunt Cup; last two outings leave him with a bit to prove, as they were 1m1f, but it's not too hard to excuse them and he is a strong contender.
runners.comment: Big performances in notable 1m h'caps last April-August; strong contender despite 1m1f

runners.name: Alcarath
runners.spotlight: Ran away with a novice at Epsom in September on third start, making all; 11-8 favourite for handicap debut at Redcar (again 1m2f on good) in November but went in snatches and finished only fourth; retains clear potential but he's nothing like as battle-hardened as most of these runners.
runners.comment: Retains clear potential but he's nothing like as battle-hardened as most of these runners

runners.name: Fifth Column
runners.spotlight: 1m winner at Newmarket and York last summer but failed to shine on last two outings, when co-second favourite for the C&D Cambridgeshire on first occasion; only mid-division when 13-2 in the Spring Cup at Newbury two weeks ago but not beaten far and the draw looked a problem; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Mid-division in the Spring Cup at Newbury but not beaten far and the draw looked a problem

runners.name: Al Arbeed
runners.spotlight: Unraced on firmer than good; won debut on Southwell AW; close fourth on handicap debut there (1m) last October is markedly better than what he's done in three starts since, but he made late gains over 7f at Musselburgh on latest start to prompt some hope given today's step up in trip.
runners.comment: Late gains over 7f at Musselburgh latest prompts some hope given today's step up in trip

runners.name: Erzindjan
runners.spotlight: Easily first home in his near-side group when strong-finishing fourth of 23 in the Cambridgeshire over C&D (good to firm) in September; just 1lb higher today and that wasn't his only solid performance in a good handicap last term, so he could be a major each-way player.
runners.comment: Easily first home in his group when 4th of 23 in the Cambridgeshire over C&D in September

runners.name: Tycoon
runners.spotlight: Windsor maiden winner for the Gosdens last April, followed by a 1m3f Listed third and big-money sale; only mid-division in his four handicaps since, 20-1 off this mark behind Mr Winston here (1m) on reappearance; back up by a furlong but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Mid-division in his four handicaps, 20-1 off this mark behind Mr Winston here on return

runners.name: Botanical
runners.spotlight: He wasn't at his very best last year but took a step back in the right direction when front-running fourth of 21 off this mark in the Lincoln at Doncaster five weeks ago on first run for new yard; no stamina worries but there is a concern about good to firm ground.
runners.comment: Front-running 4th in Lincoln at Doncaster for new yard; concern about good to firm ground
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Thunder Wonder
runners.spotlight: Won at Musselburgh last April and June; failed to make a serious impact since, albeit fourth of 12 back at Musselburgh three weeks ago; non-runner at Redcar on Thursday.
runners.comment: Won at Musselburgh last April and June; non-runner at Redcar on Thursday

runners.name: Marhaba Ghaiyyath
runners.spotlight: Went close at Glorious Goodwood (1m2f, good) last July but well beaten in C&D Cambridgeshire in September; failed to settle and suffered interference on return, doing pretty well in the circumstances, but disappointing favourite at Pontefract 11 days ago; connections turn to cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Disappointing favourite at Pontefract 11 days ago; connections turn to cheekpieces

runners.name: Astro King
runners.spotlight: Excelled in top handicaps at York and Newmarket in 2023, including with a C&D win in the Cambridgeshire (good to firm; 3lb lower today); however, he only once threatened to hit those heights in 2024 and, after wind surgery, missed all of 2025; that leaves him with a 658-day absence to overcome but his handicap mark has been slashed.
runners.comment: Has a 658-day absence to overcome but his handicap mark has been slashed

races.race_name: HKJC World Pool Palace House Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: Beckford's Folly
runners.spotlight: Has been described as a challenge by connections but he's a talented one and the drop to 5f suited when beating a smart rival (later runner-up at the Breeders' Cup) in a Group 3 over C&D (good; won a novice on good to firm) in October; nothing special on his recent return here but he was eased when his winning chance had gone and the return to 5f can suit; much respected.
runners.comment: Drop to 5f suited when beating good rival in C&D Group 3 as 2yo; respected on 2nd run back

runners.name: Frost At Dawn
runners.spotlight: A record of two wins from 16 turf starts in no way does justice to the Group 1 form she's capable of, such as a close second in the King Charles III at Royal Ascot and a third to Asfoora in the Nunthorpe at York (both 5f, good to firm); the likely fast ground is a blessing and has good claims if on song.
runners.comment: Two terrific runs in Group 1s at 5f in 2025; likes fast ground; form claims if on song

runners.name: Asfoora
runners.spotlight: Superb Australian 7yo who has had a career to be proud of, with multiple wins in home country and Europe, laying down the law in the Group 1 King Charles III at Royal Ascot in 2024 and adding further top-level wins in the Nunthorpe (good to firm) at York and the Abbaye (very soft) at Longchamp in 2025; has stayed in Britain over the winter but connections expect her to improve for the run and that the stiff uphill finish will test her.
runners.comment: Superb Australian 7yo; three Group 1 wins in Europe but expected to improve for the run

runners.name: Jm Jungle
runners.spotlight: Below best from a high draw in the Group 1 Abbaye on final 2025 start; thoroughly reliable previously and he richly deserved his Group 2 win at Goodwood (5f, good to soft; effective on good to firm) last August; versatile track-wise but has never run at Newmarket so there is a query to answer.
runners.comment: Thoroughly reliable; richly deserved Group 2 win (5f) last August; having first course run
runners.owner: MPS Racing Ltd & The Ayrshire Tradesmen

runners.name: Shagraan
runners.spotlight: In action between May and October last year for Michael Appleby, finding a form spurt late on with a close second to First Instinct in a Group 3 at Newbury sandwiched by Listed 5f wins at Beverley and Ascot (good to firm and soft); more needed back with former trainer but may yet do better.
runners.comment: Form spurt later in 2025 with Listed 5f wins (good to firm/soft); may yet progress again

runners.name: Quinault
runners.spotlight: Front-runner whose 12 wins include a Group 3 at York (7f, good) last June; he was an excellent third of 19 in the Group 1 Champions Sprint at Ascot (6f) in October; has since run up to his best in a 5f AW race at Newcastle in January and it will be interesting to see how he gets on back at this trip on turf with his regular hood discarded; ran creditably in 6f Group 3 at Newmarket last time.
runners.comment: Front-runner; usually seen at 6f-7f but useful 5f run on AW in January; regular hood off

runners.name: First Instinct
runners.spotlight: Down the field from a poor draw in the Group 1 Abbaye at Longchamp in October but had previously confirmed herself an improving 3yo when beating Shagraan in a Group 3 at Newbury (5f, good to soft; acts well on good); has hopes of doing better as a 4yo but this is a tough start to the year.
runners.comment: Improved to win Group 3 (5f, good to soft) in September; this is a tough return

runners.name: Rumstar
runners.spotlight: Acts on flat tracks but he comes into his own when tackling a stiff test at 5f, as when winning this race last year, taking C&D record to 2-2, and roaring home to win a Group 3 at Sandown (good to firm) in July; cheekpieces worn in October are discarded; yard in form; not to be underestimated.
runners.comment: Suited by tracks with stiff finishes and won this last year; not to be underestimated

runners.name: Night Raider
runners.spotlight: Often leads; he's pure quality on the AW (4-4) but has had trouble sustaining his effort on turf, even allowing for being first home on his side in a Group 2 at Goodwood in August; gelded since last seen but whether he'll appreciate this stiff finish is questionable.
runners.comment: Front-runs; pure quality on AW (4-4) but had trouble sustaining effort on turf

runners.name: Ain't Nobody
runners.spotlight: Listed winner at Royal Ascot as a 2yo; out of luck since but he showed what can be done on a good day when chasing home Asfoora in the Group 1 Nunthorpe at York (good to firm; 100-1) last August; no approach to that form since, however, and although Ryan Moore is booked, the absence of cheekpieces raises a query.
runners.comment: Winless since 2yo days; fine 2nd to Asfoora at York last August (first-time cheekpieces)

runners.name: Washington Heights
runners.spotlight: Can lead; won 6f Group 3 here in 2024 but a beaten favourite in this race last year and sole success in 2025 came in a Listed York race (5f, good to firm); below best on recent comeback and a win looks unlikely.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner at 6f here in 2024; sole success since came in Listed company; win unlikely

runners.name: Miss Attitude
runners.spotlight: She divided Washington Heights and Jm Jungle in a York Listed race (5f, good to firm) last July and later won at that level in France but, even allowing for having a fitness edge, she has to raise her game to win today.
runners.comment: Won 5f Listed race in France as 5yo; no fitness queries but has to raise her game here

runners.name: Town And Country
runners.spotlight: An improver last May/June, giving First Instinct a fright in a Cork Listed race then winning at Sandown (both about 5f; good and good to firm), but she wasn't the same filly after; has plenty to find this time.
runners.comment: An improver last May/June but wasn't the same filly after; plenty to find on these terms

runners.name: Five Ways
runners.spotlight: All races at 6f, improving on front-running novice (good to firm) and AW Group 3 wins when beaten less than 3l in the Group 1 Middle Park on this course in September; from a yard that's much respected with sprinters but this still asks a lot on the drop to 5f.
runners.comment: All runs at 6f; two front-running wins before 4th in Group 1 here in September; tall order

races.race_name: Betfred 2000 Guineas Stakes (Group 1) (No Geldings)

runners.name: Gstaad
runners.spotlight: Proved rock-solid as a 2yo, readily winning the Coventry at Royal Ascot (6f) then posting form figures of 2221 at Group/Grade 1 level, including in the Dewhurst at Newmarket (7f) when doing well considering he was free to post and in the race; ended the campaign by overcoming a wide trip in the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf at Del Mar (1m, firm; won with something to spare); supplemented (effectively re-added) into this at a cost of £30,000 having been removed in late March due to a clerical error; leading contender in his bid to end a 2,000 Guineas drought for Aidan O'Brien, who has landed this Classic a record ten times but not since 2019.
runners.comment: Rock-solid as a 2yo; ran well in the Dewhurst prior to Breeders' Cup win; leading claims
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Distant Storm
runners.spotlight: Aptly bred, being by Night Of Thunder and out of a mare by another 2,000 Guineas winner, namely George Washington; 1,900,000euros breeze-up purchase last spring and duly made a big splash in his 2yo races, aside from when pulling too hard in the Acomb; showed smart from at Newmarket (7f, good/good to firm) in the autumn, impressing in the Tattersalls Stakes (Group 3) then running well (one place behind Gstaad) in the Dewhurst; yard has landed this prize three times in the last four years; big player.
runners.comment: Impressive in the Tattersalls Stakes prior to running well in the Dewhurst; big player

runners.name: Oxagon
runners.spotlight: Very useful colt but possibly needs to dominate, having gained both wins from the front, latest when taking well to first-time cheekpieces and comfortably beating Avicenna by 2l in the Craven Stakes over C&D (good; Group 3) on seasonal debut; by no means certain to have his own way in this bigger field and he's held by a couple of today's opponents on Dewhurst running; otherwise may have more to offer in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Very useful but possibly needs to dominate; took well to cheekpieces in the Craven

runners.name: King's Trail
runners.spotlight: Son of Sea The Stars, whose stellar 3yo campaign included a success in the 2,000 Guineas; two from two, both outings over 1m on Kempton AW, last time looking value for more than the 1l winning margin in a conditions race that Notable Speech (from same yard) won in 2024 before following up in this contest; deserted by William Buick but the stable landed this Classic with the second string, ridden by James Doyle, in 2022; interesting contender who brings significant potential.
runners.comment: Similar type to his yard's winner of this race in 2024; brings big potential; interesting

runners.name: Power Blue
runners.spotlight: Juvenile campaign over sprint distances culminated in Group 1 win at the Curragh (good); held by Gstaad on Coventry running; respectable second to Thesecretadversary, who received 3lb, in 7f Group 3 at Leopardstown on reappearance but doesn't give the strong impression he'll benefit from going up further in trip; indeed, he seems more of a 6f Commonwealth Cup type.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner over 6f as a 2yo; not a guaranteed stayer in this first attempt at 1m

runners.name: Thesecretadversary
runners.spotlight: Irish colt who has built up a solid record that includes a good effort in Canadian Grade 1 on final 2yo start (sole 1m attempt); won going away from Power Blue, who conceded 3lb, in Group 3 at Leopardstown (7f, good to yielding) on reappearance; could go well but several others bring stronger claims in terms of class and/or potential.
runners.comment: Irish colt who has built up a solid record; won going away in 7f Group 3 on reappearance
runners.owner: Cayton Park Stud & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Bow Echo
runners.spotlight: Son of 2,000 Guineas winner Night Of Thunder and from the family of Dubawi; three from three last term, all starts over 1m, driven out for 1l success in the Royal Lodge at Newmarket (good to firm; Group 2) the final time; that wasn't necessarily a star performance but he keeps getting the job done and commands major respect, being a progressive colt who has a perfect record and has had a "faultless spring" according to his trainer; furthermore, he's already proven over C&D.
runners.comment: Already proven over C&D, having won the Royal Lodge to take record to 3-3; much respected
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Needle Match
runners.spotlight: Similar to Into The Sky in having raced at Newbury on both starts; did just enough to win 2yo maiden (6.5f, soft) then ran well, about 2l fourth to Alparslan, in the Greenham (7f, good); the way he shaped last time suggests additional improvement is plausible upped further in trip; by 2,000 Guineas winner Night Of Thunder; more interesting than bare ratings suggest.
runners.comment: Ran well in the Greenham; further improvement is plausible upped to 1m; not dismissed

runners.name: Avicenna
runners.spotlight: Two from two last year, both starts over 7f at Doncaster and getting up on the line in Listed grade the second time; finished nicely for 2l second to Oxagon, who dictated the pace, in the Craven over C&D (good) on reappearance having again been held up; open to further progress and could pick up some pieces in this big-field scenario, assuming the gallop is suitably strong.
runners.comment: Finished nicely for second in the Craven; could pick up some pieces if the pace is strong

runners.name: Alparslan
runners.spotlight: Reversed Dewhurst placings with Zavateri, an absentee from this race due to a setback, in the Greenham at Newbury (7f, good; Group 3) to take his record to 3-4; however, needs further progress, having finished behind several more of these in the Dewhurst, and is far from sure to get another easy lead (dictated the pace last time).
runners.comment: Had the run of things in the Greenham to take record to 3-4; another easy lead is unlikely

runners.name: Venetian Prince
runners.spotlight: Has finished behind some of today's rivals on two occasions, latest in the Craven when admittedly running well on bare figures; gained sole success (from five attempts) at novice level and looks well short of the required standard in this field; first-time headgear needs to make a difference.
runners.comment: Held by some of today's rivals on two pieces of form; first-time headgear needs to help

runners.name: Padraig Dawn
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; AW form this year comprises a win at Southwell (7f) and neck second, doing well having come from difficult position, at Newcastle (1m Listed); promising colt for first-season trainer Charlie Pike but faces a much stiffer task on turf debut; supplemented at a cost of £30,000.
runners.comment: Promising colt for his first-season trainer but faces a difficult task on turf debut

runners.name: Lord Britain
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mainly on AW, most recently 5l fourth (ran well but never dangerous) to King's Trail at Kempton on seasonal debut; bottom of this pack on the figures and trailed home last in the Royal Lodge on sole turf attempt; this outsider has plenty to prove and plenty to find.
runners.comment: Bottom of this pack on bare figures; finished last in the Royal Lodge on sole turf start

runners.name: Billecart
runners.spotlight: Has plenty to find in this field and needs to settle better to boot, having raced too freely in Listed event last time when bidding for a Newcastle (1m, AW) double; behind Distant Storm on sole turf attempt; unlikely to emulate his sire Night Of Thunder, who landed this prize in 2014.
runners.comment: Has plenty to find in this field and needs to settle better than last time

runners.name: Into The Sky
runners.spotlight: Showed useful form over 6.5f and 6f at Newbury (good/good to soft) last season, winning a maiden by sizeable margin then clear second in the Mill Reef (Group 2; hung left; collared near the finish); good prospect for Jim Boyle this term but doesn't appear to be crying out for 1m.
runners.comment: Mill Reef runner-up; good prospect for Jim Boyle but doesn't seem to be crying out for 1m
runners.owner: Mrs D Tabor, Dr P Wilson & R Stanbridge

races.race_name: Turners Handicap

runners.name: Man Of Vision
runners.spotlight: Gave trouble in the stalls on his first two runs last year (gelded afterwards) but also showed a good level of ability; made no mistake in a 7f novice at Wolverhampton in December, running out the easy winner of a race that has been subsequently been franked; bred to carry on improving and the drop in trip shouldn't be an issue; leading claims.
runners.comment: Promise in first two starts before an easy AW win (all 7f); more to come now handicapping

runners.name: Sovereign Spell
runners.spotlight: First past the post at Windsor last summer but demoted on a technicality; 2-2 in 2026, winning a Wolverhampton maiden easily before gamely seeing off Advertised over C&D 16 days ago; 6lb rise could be enough to scupper the hat-trick bid.
runners.comment: 2-2 this year, latterly beating Advertised over C&D last month; 6lb rise may find him out

runners.name: Naval Light
runners.spotlight: 360,000gns breeze-up 2yo; still seeking a first win and he has been expensive to follow thus far; that said, his Goodwood second (6f, good) last summer reads well and when third at Doncaster five weeks ago (7f, good to soft) he travelled through the race like a smart horse; the combination of a drop back in trip and the addition of cheekpieces could see him step forward now handicapping.
runners.comment: Five-race maiden but not short of ability; headgear tried now back to 6f and handicapping

runners.name: Comical Point
runners.spotlight: 5f win on 2yo debut; came up short in Group 2 and Listed company afterwards but still displayed useful ability; sent off favourite for a conditions event at Wolverhampton (6f; tongue-tie added) on last month's return and finished a close third despite hanging to his right; getting back on a straight track should suit and this Commonwealth Cup entrant makes some appeal on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Highly tried at 2yrs following debut win; satisfactory return; respected on handicap debut

runners.name: Ten Carat Harry
runners.spotlight: Developed into a useful performer on AW this winter, reeling off a five-timer (6f) prior to a good third in Listed company (7f); solid efforts in defeat on his last two starts and too soon to pigeonhole him as an AW specialist given his two turf runs were on soft ground and early in his career; likely vulnerable off topweight all the same.
runners.comment: Made giant strides on AW; should be effective on turf too but now high in the weights

runners.name: Kesta
runners.spotlight: Ran 12 times as a 2yo, winning three times; the ground may have been too soft when down the field at Newbury on his final run last year but likely a few of these will have his measure.
runners.comment: 12 runs as a 2yo, winning three; into a deep race on his return and others appeal more

runners.name: Pilu
runners.spotlight: Won a Bath maiden (5.7f, good to firm) on debut and followed up in a Salisbury novice (6f, good to soft) four weeks later; last of four in a conditions race back at Salisbury in October; improvement essential now handicapping but it's not out of the question.
runners.comment: Won first two starts before lesser run in Salisbury conditions event; improvement needed

runners.name: Advertised
runners.spotlight: Won twice on AW last year but he took his form to a new level when fourth of 14 in a York sales race last October (7f, good); lumped up 11lb for that but showed there was no fluke about the performance by finishing second in a C&D handicap on his reappearance 16 days ago (behind Sovereign Spell); didn't look in love with the track on that occasion but he is on an upward path and remains unexposed as a sprinter; 4lb rise demands more though.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with a good run and his C&D 2nd last month was even better; 4lb higher today

runners.name: First Legion
runners.spotlight: Came to hand early last year, winning a York novice on debut before finishing second in the Listed National Stakes at Sandown; only sixth in the C&D handicap won by Sovereign Spell 16 days ago and he needs that run to have brought him on significantly.
runners.comment: Promising early 2yo but he has rather plateaued since; only midfield over C&D on return

runners.name: Angel Of Anfield
runners.spotlight: Promise behind useful opposition in his first three starts and his Windsor win in August (6f, good) saw him defeat one who has boosted the form subsequently; gelded before finishing second at Kempton on his handicap debut 206 days ago; 2lb higher for his reappearance but still of interest for last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: Rock-solid 2yo form; could progress further this year; stable won this race last year

runners.name: Front Line Fury
runners.spotlight: Two 6f wins last summer; not at his best at Chester when last seen but he was drawn wide and there were possible trip/ground excuses too (wore cheekpieces which are absent today); has undergone wind surgery over the winter; returns for a stable ticking along nicely; may have more to offer.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins last year; had wind surgery since last seen; yard in form; not fully exposed

runners.name: Rydale Frosty
runners.spotlight: 6f novice win at Windsor last July before appearing to improve when fourth in the Group 3 Molecomb at Glorious Goodwood (5f, good; form hasn't worked out at all); struggled thereafter and he makes his seasonal/handicap debut off a tough mark; has been gelded since last seen.
runners.comment: Handicapped on his Molecomb 4th and that form hasn't worked out; gelded since latest run

races.race_name: Boodles Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Daiquiri Bay
runners.spotlight: Sole win in eight runs was a Chester maiden; best efforts, however, when third in both the 1m6f Melrose at York (good to firm; led over 2f out) and another valuable handicap over C&D (good; finishing strongly) in October; gelded soon afterwards; interesting prospect as 4yo.
runners.comment: Close, strong-finishing 3rd in valuable C&D handicap (good) in October; gelded soon after
runners.owner: A Stennett, G Kennington & C Hodgson

runners.name: Bellum Justum
runners.spotlight: Seemingly ring-rusty on Group 3 return at Newbury two weeks ago, the same story as in 2025 when he followed that race with a win at this meeting in a four-runner 1m4f Group 2 with first-time cheekpieces; they were kept for two modest efforts last June but have not been called on this season; this is his first handicap and he has a big chance if springing back to his best.
runners.comment: This is his first handicap; big chance if springing back to best, as he did here in 2025

runners.name: Gamrai
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) on reappearance last term, before failing to go on in his next two starts; however, he was gelded later in the autumn and led 2f out for an impressive 3l win when back in action five weeks ago in useful handicap at Kempton (1m3f, AW); 9lb higher today but likely to stay 1m4f and, after just five races, could still have potential.
runners.comment: 9lb higher after breezing home in front at Kempton (1m3f); has had just five races

runners.name: Pride Of Donegal
runners.spotlight: Consistent 4yo who twice made all over 1m4f on Wolverhampton AW once the visor was enlisted; respectable third of five on turf debut at Thirsk (1m4f, good; hung left) three weeks ago but this race should be a lot harder.
runners.comment: Respectable third of five on turf debut at Thirsk but this race should be a lot harder

runners.name: Hopewell Rock
runners.spotlight: 2-2 as 2yo and progressive last term in his first three handicaps, short-headed by another unexposed sort at Leicester (1m4f) on third occasion; underperformed on heavy going (he won on it as a 2yo) when joint-favourite for the November Handicap at Doncaster on final outing but that's not hard to forgive and this lightly raced 4yo, gelded since latest start, surely has more to offer.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo, gelded since latest start, who surely has more to offer
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Nolton Cross
runners.spotlight: Six wins from his 24 AW starts, the latest in February, but 0-9 on turf and he was last seen in this sphere in 2023; fit from AW action (well beaten in hot race last time) but other options seem more likely.
runners.comment: 0-9 on turf and he was last seen in this sphere in 2023; fit from AW action

runners.name: Nightime Dancer
runners.spotlight: 100-1 for the Derby at Epsom but showed improvement in making late headway from the back to finish eighth of 18 runners; similar form later last summer in Group 3s at Newmarket and Newbury; gelded in March and some improvement is needed but he is lightly raced and may be capable of it.
runners.comment: Ran creditably in the Derby and Group 3s on final three starts last term; gelded since

runners.name: Military Academy
runners.spotlight: AW Listed winner for the Gosdens in November 2024 but he hasn't been near that form since a Listed second last June; second and third places early this year came in small fields and other options look more solid.
runners.comment: 2nd and 3rd places early this year came in small fields and other options look more solid

runners.name: Oneforthegutter
runners.spotlight: Scored at the Newmarket July meeting (1m6f) last year but didn't do a lot afterwards; he's back 1lb below that last winning mark, so will pop up in front again at some stage, but he was well held on last year's reappearance at this fixture.
runners.comment: Will pop up again at some stage but well held on last year's reappearance at this fixture

races.race_name: Air Charter Services "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Won No More Than One Race)

runners.name: Nanoscience
runners.spotlight: Has improved with each of her four runs, justifying favouritism at Kempton (7f) four weeks ago despite racing freely and wide; shaped well over 1m here (good) last autumn; open to further progress and high on the list.
runners.comment: Progressive filly; justified favouritism at Kempton four weeks ago; high on the list

runners.name: Sierra Sands
runners.spotlight: Catterick winner last October; made a pleasing reappearance/handicap debut over C&D (good ground) 18 days ago, always prominent and finishing fourth; Ryan Moore keeps the ride; may improve for the outing; respected.
runners.comment: Pleasing reappearance/handicap debut over C&D; may improve for the outing; respected

runners.name: Try Storm Cat
runners.spotlight: Well held at 33-1 over C&D on stable debut 18 days ago; only 2lb lower here and others make much more appeal.
runners.comment: Well held at 33-1 over C&D on stable debut; others make much more appeal

runners.name: Thaluna
runners.spotlight: Confirmed debut promise when winning at York (6f, good) last October before coming up short in Listed race here three weeks later; personal best needed on her first go in a handicap but she's lightly raced and in good hands.
runners.comment: Came up short in Listed race on final 2yo start; lightly raced and in good hands

runners.name: Cotai Lights
runners.spotlight: Comes into the reckoning on his third at Naas (7f, soft) in March and he was probably unsuited by the drop back to 6f at Newcastle on Good Friday; still relatively lightly raced and not ruled out in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Good third at Naas in March and unsuited by drop back in distance next time; not ruled out

runners.name: Splash
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much on her first two (AW) starts but she nearly sprang a 40-1 surprise over C&D (good) last autumn, making most of the running; unexposed and interesting on handicap debut after her winter break.
runners.comment: Nearly sprang 40-1 surprise over C&D; interesting on handicap debut after winter break

runners.name: Stellar Vision
runners.spotlight: Finished a place ahead of Sierra Sands when third over C&D (good) at the Craven meeting, racing freely and in the firing line throughout; going the right way and should be in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Third over C&D at the Craven meeting; going the right way and in the mix again

runners.name: King Of Chaos
runners.spotlight: Has blown hot and cold since winning at Lingfield (7f, AW) in February, and he ran poorly over the same C&D on Monday; enough to prove.
runners.comment: Has blown hot and cold since Lingfield AW win; ran poorly there on Monday; enough to prove

runners.name: Velvet Rhythm
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on all three outings (7f/6f) as a 2yo; has a modest opening handicap mark and her pedigree suggests she can do better this season; not without hope.
runners.comment: Made frame on all three 2yo outings; pedigree suggests she can do better this season
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Paroda Diva
runners.spotlight: Just one win from 12 attempts, but she hasn't been long with her current yard and she seemed unsuited by the drop back to 5f when a never-nearer fourth of eight at Catterick ten days ago; looks well handicapped and the return to 7f should be right up her street.
runners.comment: Shaped nicely when never-nearer fourth over 5f at Catterick; return to 7f looks a big plus

runners.name: Lohoobb
runners.spotlight: Made the frame twice over 6f last summer; missed next intended outing due to lameness; bred to be suited by 7f+ and may well have more to offer now tackling a handicap over this trip after nearly ten months off.
runners.comment: Made frame twice over 6f last summer; bred to be suited by 7f+ and could be unexposed

races.race_name: National Stud Handicap

runners.name: Classic Encounter
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a good third to Man Of La Mancha over 1m1f on this course (good to firm); shaped as if retaining all his ability when seventh of 22 in the Spring Mile at Doncaster, short of room closing stages; should be spot-on with that run behind him and looks ready to strike.
runners.comment: Good third here on final 4yo start; shaped well on return; looks ready to strike

runners.name: Skipper
runners.spotlight: Campaigned in Qatar and France in 2025; went off joint-favourite on return to Britain and won nine-runner race at Redcar (1m, good to firm) 12 days ago, digging deep; only 2lb higher and should be in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Returned to Britain and won at Redcar 12 days ago; only 2lb higher and in the mix again

runners.name: Spanish Voice
runners.spotlight: Sole turf run resulted in a win here (1m2f, good) last spring; has flattered to deceive on his last two AW runs (1m4f/9.4f), fading after racing freely; today's drop back to 1m looks well worth a try and he may still be unexposed.
runners.comment: 1-1 on turf; has flattered to deceive last two AW starts; this drop in trip could help

runners.name: Final Night
runners.spotlight: Four wins at Kempton between October and March but he could finish only sixth of nine there when a warm favourite four weeks ago; will need a personal best to regain the winning thread and his mark has risen steeply since his last go on turf.
runners.comment: All four wins on AW; mark has risen steeply since his last go on turf

runners.name: Sterling Knight
runners.spotlight: Fine stable servant with 11 wins to his name, although recent efforts have been underwhelming; only seventh of ten at the Craven meeting here and he needs to be revived by the presence of Ryan Moore in the saddle.
runners.comment: Recent runs underwhelming; needs to be revived by the presence of Ryan Moore in the saddle

runners.name: Man Of La Mancha
runners.spotlight: On a roll when last seen, winning at Sandown (1m, soft) and on this course (1m1f, good to firm) in second half of last season; that form looks solid and he's been nudged up only 3lb for the second success; leading claims if ready to roll after seven months off.
runners.comment: On a roll when last seen, winning twice; big chance if ready to roll after winter break

runners.name: Cogitate
runners.spotlight: Consistent of late on the AW, runner-up at Kempton in October and March, but his turf efforts have been more up and down; never better than midfield in Spring Cup at Newbury two weeks ago and only 1lb lower today.
runners.comment: Never better than midfield in Spring Cup at Newbury two weeks ago and only 1lb lower today

runners.name: Advancing
runners.spotlight: Mostly in good form on the AW during the winter, winning at Wolverhampton (8.6f) and Kempton (1m); however, he was below par back at Wolverhampton six weeks ago and has something to prove on a rare encounter with turf.
runners.comment: Raced mostly on AW; below par last time; something to prove on rare encounter with turf

runners.name: Slipper Time
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at Salisbury (7f, good to firm) last summer and ran well on handicap debut at Newbury (7f, good) next time; disappointing when upped to 1m at Kempton but he's certainly bred to stay; still lightly raced.
runners.comment: Disappointing upped to 1m at Kempton final 3yo start but he's bred to stay;  lightly raced

runners.name: Zryan
runners.spotlight: Won twice on the AW over the winter but he seems to have gone off the boil, well beaten at Newcastle on Good Friday; not so effective yet on turf; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Two winter AW wins but seems to have gone off the boil; questions to answer back on turf

runners.name: Carron
runners.spotlight: Seems more effective on AW (3-4) than turf (1-9) and finished down the field in the Spring Mile at Doncaster (14-1) five weeks ago; now has cheekpieces to accompany the usual tongue-strap.
runners.comment: Seems more effective on AW (3-4) than turf (1-9) and was down the field in the Spring Mile

runners.name: I Still Have Faith
runners.spotlight: On a lengthy losing run but he's knocking firmly at the door, just edged out in a first-time tongue-strap at Lingfield (1m) four weeks ago; goes well at Newmarket and makes plenty of each-way appeal.
runners.comment: On a long losing run but knocking firmly at the door; makes plenty of each-way appeal
runners.owner: J Morley D Reynolds C Watkins & Partner

runners.name: Footwork
runners.spotlight: Signed off 2025 with a low-key effort but he'd previously won convincingly at Haydock (good to firm) and run well when third at Kempton (AW), both over 1m; not ruled out if cherry ripe after 192 days off.
runners.comment: Signed off 2025 with a low-key effort but previous two runs give him definite claims

runners.name: Mr Professor
runners.spotlight: Had his favoured soft ground when winning the Lincoln in 2024; below par since, last seen finishing in midfield at Newcastle last June; back below his last winning mark but may find conditions too quick.
runners.comment: Below par since winning 2024 Lincoln; below last winning mark but would prefer soft ground
